title: So far so good....but they didn't last!!, new updated version are not that good!!
body: Update 8/11/21,Well I have had this new set for about 2 weeks and the updated earbud version is a bit smaller, battery do last way longer and touch button is not like the ones I had before that were clicker, after using them I can say that the Bluetooth connection is very clunky the one before this one will connect to any device available w/o any effort in my part this new one is a pain in freaking butt, I don't use them on my phones constantly because I also use them with my PC and every single time I switch from my PC to my phone I have paired the buds to my phone every single time which is really annoying because it won't get saved on my phone i have no idea why after pairing it it won't stay saved and the same issue when trying to connect back to my PC, I have placed them on the charging dock and removed them so it will connect to my PC I really liked the old system where the connection was more effective and effortless, so with this ones if I don't have the dock closer to my PC i won't be able to connect them because is near impossible to set them to discovery mode which it dosent work on mines at least, you suppost to tap twice on the power button to set it to discovery mode yeah that dosent work at all, so after this thing die which i know it will at some point ill try another brand i'm done with this brand, lower my rating again to 1 star.Update 7/29/21,after my last update I was able to contact the seller and he was able to verify that my purchase was within the warranty coverage time so they send me a new pair I was not expecting anything really bc weeks before after one of my reviews they contacted me via email saying that they will send a new set if I update my review but they never did so I was skeptical when I was told this time around that a new set was on the way, but I have to say they do stand behind there product and are true to their word bc 1 week 1/2 later I got my new set, which is an updated version of what I bought originally the same model but one can tell the desing is a bit different they look bit smaller the performance still the same which I never had any issues with it just the battery life but after all, I have experience with this product I can yet say if it would be a reliable product or not maybe this updated version will last well is yet to be seem will update if i run into any other issue, but for now i can say that they do stand behind their product and I'm happy how they handle my issue hence why i updated this review to 4 stars.Update 7/13/21,the right earbud stop working, it connects and 5 sec later it disconnects and I can't turn back on and it won't charge either, I place in the dock and the charging lights turn on for like 20 sec and it turns off I clean the connectors with alcohol just in case they were dirty and still the same result nothing is happening, drop the overall rating to 1 star bc this thing didn't even last a full year.Update: 6/22/21,the battery life has stayed the same and it hasn't dropped the duration of the 4hr give it or take a few minutes here and there so after 10 months from my original review they still perform well I won't recommend it because I haven't tried any other brand so I can make an educated assessment in comparison to any other earbud in the market.I bought this back on 8/11/20 since I have used them daily and I don't have any complaints other than the battery life is not now what it used to be, when I first got them they will last somewhere in the range of 5 1/2 hours now the max range is 4 hrs so they have drop-down significantly rather quick I was expecting that 6 months to 1 year down the road not so fast, I usually used them at work to play audio from videos or music w/o disturbing anyone else and also I don't use both at the same time just one at the time so on 8hr works good for now I will update if the battery life drops more under the 4 hr I get now. But besides that, the sound is pretty decent for the price nice soft bass and deep sound, connection wise is good sometimes it struggles but a quick turn off/on, and it sync quite fast, so battery life is my only concern.

title: Buds cut out and loose connection when using during active sports
body: Upd.: After few weeks of usage I have some updates. First of all, whatever is said in the original review is still true provided you're sitting behind your desk and do not move around. But after I wrote the original review I took it our for a run. Surprisingly, as soon as I started actively moving, it started cutting out on me every 30-40 seconds. Of course, it is impossible to use them like that. I took them off and finished my run without them. Then I did initial round of troubleshooting, resetting, reconnecting etc. and the second day I took it out but had another buds with me (the kind where two buds are connected with a wire). 15 minutes into my run I stopped and switched to those back ups.Then I returned these buds and god replacement sent out. Tech support said that it was because "interference with other electronic devices". Yeah, right. I'm sitting in the office with tens of electronic devices around me, and they work just fine, but as soon as I get out onto a forest trail, it suddenly sees "electronic interference"?So, I was told it might have been a bad device and I agreed to get a replacement.Got it 5 days ago and was taking out those buds almost every day. Everything was more or less OK (3-5 cut outs in 30min run) but today it started cutting out every 30-40 seconds again. I did some more experimenting and realized the following: Your own body shields these buds from connected device. If you put your connected device somewhere on your lower back on the left side - it completely blocks the signal, as there is lot of flesh and bones between the right bud and your connected device.If you connect your buds to your smartwatch, as I do, and wear it on your left wrist, then as you run or walk, you'll be moving your arm in and out of area of good connection, causing cut outs. Even if you have some long sleeve closing - it will reduce signal a lot and your buds will start cutting out. And don't blame the connected devices - I tested that with another set of buds, it is the intrinsic problem with this specific model of buds.As such, these buds are only good in office, where you keep your connected device close to your right bud and do not actively move around. But in the office you can use something more comfortable and with higher sound quality. The whole point of these buds is active sports, they are waterproof and light, so you can take them out for runs, hikes, etc. Unfortunately, they fail miserably in their intended use.Original review:I was surprised how comfortable these are and how good the sounds quality is.I have more expensive (around $180) buds from a well known brand name, and I was quite disappointed with them. The problems were: impossible to get a good fit, buds were falling out all the time, material was causing my ears to itch like crazy, very poor sound quality which depended on how they fit, absent bass and noticable lag.These buds that are almost three times cheaper, have none of those problems:Great fit, stay in the earsSolid bass, no squeaking treblesAlmost undetectable lag, less than 100ms.Great passive noise cancelling, based on the tiny holes on the buds I suspect that this thing might have some kind of active noise cancelling, but haven't read anything about it and haven't tested it yet.Another nice touch: wireless charger. I just need to put it on my charging plate, no plug/unplug hassle.Have yet to see what the battery life is and if it makes my ears itch after long time use.

title: Worked (emphasis on the -ed part of that) pretty well...
body: Very sleek, very user friendly. Sound quality is pretty good, the case is sturdy, and although simple in design, has a charge indicator inside (in the form of 4 leds). I mainly bought these for the waterproofing so I could use them in the shower. Will update this review once I try them out there. But so far so good!Update: Well they work in the shower, but I'm not sure they work very well outside of it. Not long after I had bought them (I'd say it started after the first 2 weeks) I started having connection issues. When listening to music or podcasts, one of the earbuds would randomly shut off for a few seconds, then it would happen to the other bud in succession. This would happen a few times before the buds would "calm down" and act as they should. At first, I thought this was just a quirk of theirs, and I wasn't too bothered by it. But now it happens every time I try to use the earbuds, so I basically have to wait a few minutes before they work properly. Not great.So that's issue #1, but issue #2 is the big one, and why I dropped the rating down so drastically. Once again, only after a couple weeks of using these, they started having more issues. This time it was the fact that one of the buds just wouldn't charge while in the case. Now I'm a man who likes to listen to podcasts to help me fall asleep, so I would leave the earbuds in over night, which of course meant that the buds would run out of battery often, which is fine. But now nearly every morning when I put them in the case, the red indicater light shows up for both, so I think "good they must be charging" so then I go about my day not worrying about them. This now often ends in disappointment, because although the right ear bud charges perfectly fine, the left one does not. I'll use it for about 10 minutes before the annoying robotic voice of a probably enslaved woman kept in the dark reaches of whatever horror of a factory these are made in screams in (just) my left ear telling me that the left bud has a "low battery, please charge." So then I have to replace the buds into the case and fidget and mess with the left bud until the red light stays on for longer than a minute, or otherwise, I'll just be back to one ear listening.I realize that was quite the read, but I'm just bummed out by these ear buds. I really like how well they work in the shower (although if they do get touched by enough hot water, the volume will start changing, I'm guessing the buds think the water is my finger interacting with them), but I've just experienced too many nights where I just want to relax and fall asleep with the buds in, only to be yelled at by the disembodied voice of some poor factory worker. Also, I never got my free warranty, which I was supposed to get if I left a 5 star review (which I initially did) so I can't even get them replaced. Not that I'd want to anyways, cause bottom line, they suck. I know people say "you get what you pay for" but I guess I still think the $40 I paid for them is a substantial amount for what is very likely a cheap tech to manufacture. Maybe I'll just have to suck it up and buy a more expensive set. Insert shrug emoji.

title: These are not good for a variety of reasons
body: I bought these headphones because I didn't have much time to browse and saw them listed as "Amazon's Choice". I figured they must be pretty good at a price point of $39.99 with a blessing from Amazon. I was replacing a pair that I had lost which I loved and were significantly cheaper. I figured the extra few bucks would be worth it for a better product. When I got them, I was sorely disappointed. Here's why:The recharging case - Mine is good for about a day whether I use the headphones or not. After that, the case is basically dead. It isn't capable of restoring a charge on the headphones even once. Good headphones will have a case with a robust battery that holds a charge for a long time when not in use and is capable of recharging the headphones multiple times.The in-ear controls - The button on these headphones is a tactile switch, which means it's a button that you need to press until it clicks. When you are wearing the headphones and try to push the button, it's very uncomfortable pressing hard into your ear. Good headphones will have a sensor instead of a button. A very light tap on the sensor will get you a response.Control architecture - The controls don't make sense. One click pauses the music but two clicks redials the last number you called. This is dumb. Headphone users are generally not trying to control their phone calls with the in-ear controls. They are typically trying to control their music. Pause/answer incoming call, skip ahead, skip back, volume, that's about all you need.Noise cancelling - These don't cancel noise, they just put an object into your ear. To me, noise cancelling means there is active technology working to dampen the ambient noises. These have the same noise cancelling technology as a carrot. If you shove it in your ear, things aren't as loud. This is a totally false claim.Sound quality - Not great as the volume increases. I don't usually listen to anything very loudly in my headphones, but even at responsibly loud levels, the sound starts falling apart. Too much attempt at bass distorts the music to a point that makes it virtually un-listenable.Charging - The case charges by a micro-USB connection. This is a relatively small complaint, but USB-C is where everything is going, so new tech should really come with USB-C.Overall, I am really irritated that these headphones represent the most expensive ones I have purchased while simultaniously being the worst performing headphones I've tried. I'm not a music snob and I'm not someone who uses them constantly so the $50 or less range feels right to me. I've owned 3 sets of wireless earbuds and the other two sets were outstanding and cost $25 or less.At the end of the day, there is a huge market out there full of really good earbuds. Don't buy into the idea that "Amazon's Choice" means much in terms of quality or value and don't get tricked into believing that you'll get something better if you pay more. These earbuds are awful.

title: Underwhelming sound, horrible quality assurance, fake reviews?
body: I got this item after seeing there’s 100k positive reviews so I thought they must be doing something right and not all of the reviews are bought!Well the first thing I felt when I put these on was “wow the sound quality is so underwhelming!”. There’s virtually no Bass coming through and everything sounds really dull. Probably the last time I felt so underwhelmed by sound quality was when I used the headphones that were provided on an airplane.But if you want to use these for listening to podcasts or taking calls, I see absolutely no problem with them. The mic is strong and you can walk far without having to carry your phone around in your place. Weirdly though, if I put the phone in my pocket, the sound keeps going in and out as I walk, specially if I put my hand in pocket!Another thing is that these earbuds sometimes just randomly turn on while in the box and pair with your phone and you wonder why you can’t hear anything suddenly until you realize headphones were connected!I also don’t know what the function of the 4 blue LED lights in the charging case is but only one of them turns on for me under any condition (charging the case, charging partial drained earbuds, charging fully drained earbuds) so I assume there’s manufacturing defect there as well.By the way, I did try to use them in the shower since they are advertised as waterproof and nothing happened to them so that’s good but showering with earbuds is not for me I guess, specially with such underwhelming sound quality 😁Overall, I think my 4 yr old iphone SE speakers sound better than these earbuds which is contrary to what I read in some reviews but at this price point, I would’ve been very surprised to have nice sound quality! But I expected from them to not turn on randomly or get cut-off while my phone is in my pocket. I’ve ordered a replacement and will update the review if these problems don’t persist.UPDATE: I ordered a replacement pair because the LEDs on my original pair did not work. The LEDs on the new pair works and now what I see is that the earbuds turn on randomly while in the box and remain on, essentially draining their batteries until you notice and turn them off again. Sometimes they automatically pair with your phone in the middle of a phone call leaving you wondering why you cannot hear anything suddenly until you realize your phone was paired with the earbuds randomly.I think these earbuds are just absolutely horrible with the random issues I've noticed between the original purchase and the replacement I've received and I absolutely do not recommend them to anybody and unless I've been extremely unlucky with the two pairs I've had my hands on, majority of these positive reviews are probably fake and the seller should be applauded for the number of positive reviews they've managed to round up.

title: An absolutely catastrophe in a box
body: For those who don't want to read the full in depth review I am about to drop on these headphones, I'll keep it short. These suck. Look for another set, cuz these do not live up to their claims.Man where to start. The sound quality is awful. You'd have better sound quality with sticks shoved in your ears. Its so monotone and dull. The touch controls were not very responsive whatsoever, then they stopped responding completely just 2 weeks into the purchase period. Tonight as I write up this review, they went on strike, and completely went a-wall. Shutting themselves on and off repeatedly, maxing the volume out, then turning it back down. Disconnecting, then reconnecting. Total electrical failure. The battery life is not great either. I think at best when I first opened the box and charged them up, I used them for 2 to 3 hours before needing to recharge them, that is until the case stopped recognizing the headphones in their little pods, and would only charge them if jiggled into the right spot. Bump the case, the headphones would turn on, and now you can't hear the person on the phone anymore. Fantastic. They also advertise them as being waterproof. IXP8, meaning they can be submerged about 1 meter. Thats not much, but you can drop them in your cup or in a pool or take a shower with them and they should be perfectly fine. Yeah, no. I did use these in the shower. They did get wet, and would reduce the volume levels to a whisper quiet level. I let them sit in silica packets (the little packages you get with most things that state "do not eat") to pull the water and moisture out of them, but they were still never the same. Now credit where credit is due. They do provide plenty of ear buds to make sure you can get them to fit your ears properly, but unfortunately as much as I tried to find a pair that would fit comfortably and not fall out, they would either just fall out after a short period of time, or they would just be uncomfortable and painful to have in my ears. I mean every single little thing about these headphones is a total joke. I am so excited to return these pieces of junk, and to find something else that is better, and can at least live up to its name.To sum it up, these have terrible sound quality, they are not waterproof, the touch controls are a laughing stock, they are either to tight or too loose, the battery life is below average, and with time they might just develop a mind of thier own and have a complete electrical breakdown. Buy something else.

title: Update - Warranty Coverage Conditioned on Review
body: UPDATE - I originally liked these, and when the button on one bud stopped working, Tozo was quick to offer a whole replacement pair. They asked if I would please leave a review and asked that I notify them when it’s done. I said sure. I liked how amenable that Tozo was to making this right, so I was happy to give them a positive review, but it took me a while to get around to it. In the meantime, a few weeks passed. I assumed that it was due to international shipping and didn’t think anything would of it. When I finally left the review and notified Tozo that I had reviewed them like they asked, (still assuming the order had not arrived because of international shipping), they said, ok, we’ll ship your item now (which they did, and it arrived a few days later). I was annoyed that they didn’t tell me they were waiting on the review before they shipped, and went to go update my review to warn buyers needing warranty replacements not to promise a review or insist on shipping before you leave one, and that’s when I saw that Amazon had temporarily suspended reviews for Tozo because they were improperly conditioning warranty work on positive reviews. Today is the first day that I’ve checked back and been able to review them. I had kinda hoped Amazon would have kicked them off for deceptive practices. Not only are they breaching contracts with existing customers by adding improper conditions on warranty work, but, as a result of this practice, the reviews for this item are improperly inflated, which dupes future customers. I’ll never buy from them again. Shame on Amazon for knowingly allowing merchants with such dishonest practices back into their marketplace.Love these! I've had mine several months. I paired then separately so I can listen to one at a time (to keep an ear on my kiddos while I listen to my audiobooks). The battery on each lasts all day with intermittent use (but still on and connected to my phone all day). I get about 4.5 hours of continuous use.My left earbud power button stopped working and the customer service was amazing. I messaged the manufacturer about the problem, and they immediately sent me a replacement without making me jump through a bunch of hoops. Very impressed.

title: Big disappointment
body: I have been looking for wireless bluetooth ear buds for some time now. The ones with a neck thing with the wires going to the earpiece has been a bother as I constantly snag them in some way which eventually results in them being non-working on one side or the other. So, I finally took the plunge and bought these based on a glowing review in an article from airwatchdog. Super disappointment especially for the price.Let's start with the physical product. This set does not come with additional rubber ear pieces so I cannot change to the size I need for my ears. They became painful to wear in no time. They are a bit bulky and I assume since there was no ear piece options, that would explain why it was hard to keep them in my ears.Next, the sound quality was not good. This could be due to not having the correct ear piece size. However when I pressed them into my ears to hear what I expected to be great sound, this was not the case. The sound was rather weak on the bass. They sounded really cheap. I do admit when someone was talking to me I could not hear them talking even though they weren't fitting well.Connectivity is a huge issue. They are easy to pair with devices. That's the only good thing I can say. There are a couple of problems to mention here. One is staying connected to a device after you've paired them with multiple devices. For me this was my pc and my iPhone. I would be listening to my phone and as soon as I turned on my pc or walked by it, the headphones would jump to the pc and dump my phone. The other problem is using only one earpiece. When I need to wear only one, I finally figured out that I had to take them both out of the charging storage box at the same time, let them pair to each other, then sometimes I had to lay the one I didn't want on the desk because if I put it back in the box the one in my ear would beep at me. Sometimes it wouldn't.So overall, I am very unhappy and will be returning them. I tried using them for a couple of days and it isn't worth the hassle. I guess I will keep with the older wired bluetooth style because they can stay connected to the right device, provide great sound, last longer from a charge, and are comfortable.

title: Trendy with good sound quality -read update
body: The casing looks great and the sound is pretty good too. I'm not used to earbuds without any wires yet but these seem like a good start. The only problem I've had so far is the right one keeps falling out of my ear. Two days in and I've already had a close call with the earbud falling out as a walked over a storm drain. I wouldn't trust running with them in. In the package there is a variety of sizes of rubber pieces to choose from but even with me using the XL piece they still don't fit tightly. I recognize my ear canal might just be wide but it's something to take note of.Also, the earpieces are very touch sensitive with no actual buttons. So, when they fall out, I usually pause or increase the volume by accident trying to pick it up again. This is probably a universal problem that I'm not used to yet.UPDATE:It's been less than a year (10 months ish) and they are slowly dying out on me. The charging port won't connect at all to the earbuds. Neither one of them and it's only a matter of time before they lose charge. Furthermore, because I am accustomed to just popping them back in the holder to turn them off, I didn't realize the problem till they were put back into my bag and my phone was still connected to the earbuds. I disconnected the ear buds from my phone and turned them off manually by holding down both buds. One time I wasn't even able to do that because if you hold one earbud down it just increases the volume or decreases it so because they weren't communicating with each other they just simultaneously changing the volume. At this point they still work, and the sound quality is the same, but I am slowly putting these buds out to pasture. It's like watching your favorite pet suffering in pain without having the resources or cure to save them. Heartbreaking. I will be in the market for new earbuds sooner than anticipated for what I had expected. I love the look of them nonetheless but unless you want something to just last a yearish, it's best to keep looking.Side note, I am still communicating with health professionals about this, but they may have contributed to some severe migraines during the time period that the Bluetooth connectivity was going wonky.

title: Very disappointed.
body: Based on the other reviews for these headphones I had high hopes, the price is great, and they seemed like a clear choice. Ive only ever owned the Bluetooth headphones that are connected to each other, and never had a problem with them. I decided to give these a try after I lost my last pair. But after the first day, I disliked them, after a week, I’ve come to loathe them and have rebought the set I’ve previously owned.1. Touch response on a headphone you have to constantly re-adjust while you’re working out is infuriating. As I’m moving and working out, my jaw is inevitably moving to breathe, and thus moves my ears, so I have to re-adjust headphones from time to time. Not a problem with any other headphone. With these though, I’m constantly pausing music, turning up or down volume to ridiculous levels. I’d rather just stomp them into the ground after a few times of this.2. The Bluetooth connectivity is flaky at best. Again, I’m moving around at the gym, doing my thing. I move in a different way, breathe more heavily, or someone just walks by me, music cuts in and out from one ear to the other, they both stop, then start again. Uh, what? Never had this problem with Bluetooth headphones that are connected to each other. Extremely annoying.3. The last straw was today, just trying to charge them. As I said previously, the touch “buttons” for these are just annoying. I try and turn them off, it just turns the volume up til I’m going deaf. So I say “screw it. I’ll just charge them before I go to the gym later.” That time rolls around, I put them in the charger for an hour til I’m ready to go. Come back to the charger to see only a red light (charging light) for the right ear, and I think “ok, the left must be fully charged already.” As I’m walking to the door for the gym, not 10 minutes later I get the “battery low” voice in the left ear...and yes I made sure it was fully connected to charge. For whatever reason, the connection for charging is already flawed.I was going to save these as an emergency pair when i have no other options. After this debacle today, they’re going in the trash.

title: Do NOT buy these; I've had them 6 months and I hate them
body: I've had these ear buds now for 6 months, and I can definitively say that I hate them. I would return them if I could. I will probably just throw them away.Pros: Great sound quality(when it works), decent battery life(most of the time), multiple ear sizes, nice case.Cons: 1. The left earbud does not always hold a charge even though the case is fully charged and I'll leave them charging overnight. Within 2-3 minutes of use, the battery low message will start sounding and then it just dies. Which is annoying when you're at the gym and don't have a backup.2. And this is the biggest reason I hate these earbuds, if anyone else is within 30 feet of me with another bluetooth device, the audio starts cutting out every 3-5 seconds. So, you basically can't use these at the gym, in a store, or near anyone with a cellphone or ear buds... And when it started cutting out, I thought, "this is the worst", but it got even worse. Sometimes it will cut out for a full 15 seconds at a time of just silence. So, you can't listen to podcasts or youtube. Forget audiobooks. The only thing you get is choppy music at best. And it's not only my set. I bought a pair for my fiance at the same time and it does the same thing for him.So, frustrated I spent money on these I was determined to make them work. I can at least wear them when I'm out jogging, or cleaning around the house right? Wrong again. If I'm jogging, turn my head quickly, or bend quickly, the audio also cuts out... So FML, but I hope my review saves at least one other person the money and trouble of buying these. I really want to give them a good review, but I just can't. They're too unreliable and the fact that they can't be used near other bluetooth devices in this day and age makes these things obsolete. I'll probably throw them in the trash because it would be wrong to sell them and I don't hate anyone enough to give it to them.Please do NOT buy this product. (I was fooled by the 5 star reviews which I now see have similar complaints, but the people were given a free set to change the bad reviews to 5 star one.)

title: Hmm...
body: Okay, I'll be the first to admit I am in NO WAY a "techie," far from it. I am not an audiophile but I like to hear my music clear and crisp. I know I'm behind the times but these were my very first pair of blue tooth ear plugs. The price was okay. Arrived in a reasonable amount of time. The instructions were easy to follow and they paired easily with the MP3 I bought for work. I'm a night shift nurse & tunes help pass the night away. They came fully charged; no one wants to wait to play with their new toys. I tried them in my house & they were spotty. It was probably me but they felt weighty and like they were going to fall out. (Yes, I had the right tip size-I'm not stupid) I didn't know what to expect, this is all a new learning curve for me. We don't have a large house but they kept cutting out. I had to run out to Walmart & Sam's club so I thought I'd give them a try, roughly an hour trip. I pulled into Sam's parking lot, put them in and turned them on. Probably 25 yards from car to door. They cut out at LEAST 10 times. "Re-pairing" said into my ears at least 5 times. I thought, "well it's probably because I'm outside." Got inside THE SAME THING! When they weren't saying "re-pairing," they were off more than they were playing. I was done at Sam's & figured I'd try them in Walmart. The very same issues. By the time I got home I was so AGGRAVATED they went right back into the charger, into the packaging and into a box to be returned. I immediately went on line and started the return process. I don't know if I was right or wrong. It was my first pair of BT buds but I'm pretty sure your suppose to hear music when they're on. I gave 1 star for noise cancellation because I heard NOTHING other than noise and "re-pairing." 3 stars for sound because I heard "re-pairing" quite well. 3 stars for battery life because I thought I'd stay in the middle to be fair. I never had them on long enough to drain the battery. I ended up paying a little more for another set of BT buds from a different manufacturer. Hopefully they will be better. We'll see... Hope this helps.

title: I don’t understand why the high reviews?
body: I have now bought this product twice. First, in 2020 and then in November 2021 because my first pair quit working all of a sudden, and I thought it was just defective. Well, I was wrong.After one month of use, my second pair has quit as well. One of the earbuds will not accept charge, so even if it’s been charging all night, the right ear bud still says low battery (You literally hear a little computerized voice that says low battery) while the left earbud is working perfectly fine.The same thing happened with the previous pair btw.Furthermore, you have to charge after every use because the battery life is terrible! And when I say terrible, I mean terrible!!!! For example, you don’t have to use them for the battery life to die on you. Just being in my gym bag, in the case, not being used, the battery starts to go out.Btw, I work out almost every day, and I always Have music playing so having a great pair of earbuds is crucial! But, let’s say over the weekend, I don’t go to the gym, and I just leave them charging in my gym bag? Well, they’ll be dead come Monday morning. Like WTH! They weren’t even being used. Who makes a battery life like that?Additionally, they fall out of your ear very easily and when you try to adjust them, they either turn the song off, replay the song from the beginning, or play another song all together. Since when did y’all start putting little sensors on the earbuds? I don’t need that! If you’re going to make that feature, at least make them fit the ear properly!I’m so frustrated because I have yet to find a perfect pair of earbuds. If you know a pair, please let me know ASAP!I hope this review proved helpful. I do not understand where all these high reviews came from. That’s what sold me in the first place: I, like you, read reviews. I thought for sure my first pair was just defective cause thousands of people can’t be wrong. LOL. Now after this has happened twice, I will never buy this pair again and neither should you. Save yourself some money and keep looking.

title: SUCH A MISTAKE
body: So, I should’ve just gotten the newest T12’s in rose gold because they were on sale cheaper than these and are at least pretty, which is important since these T10’s are basically nothing more than a paperweight. I decided to get these T10’s because of the sheer number of reviews and the star count, the other models from TOZO having horrible reviews, I thought well these must be awesome. Wrong. Charged the case when they arrived, charged the earbuds, found the ear tips that fit the best, handy dandy instructions in front of me and excitedly went to pair. The left earbud has only worked once, after placing it in the charger and pulling it out a few times. Never had a problem with the right ear bud (but I’ve had them less than 24 hours). The earbuds are very chunky, honestly surprised they actually stay in my ears. Cat snuggled my face and one fell out tho. I don’t care how waterproof electronics allegedly are, I never put them in or under water, so I can’t comment to that aspect. The sound quality is nice, but max volume is low AF. There’s no noise cancellation at all, I can hear the tv playing on volume level 7 while the earbuds are on max while listening to Netflix. The earbuds aren’t as comfortable as I’d hoped, but I can tolerate. Also the reviews on the T12 said the touch censors were horrible and that the T10 had buttons and were awesome… my T10’s say “upgraded” on the box and have touch censors… and I hate them, I can’t feel anything when I’m touching, there’s no indication of what you’re doing until the voice says “max volume “ or “ powering off”. If you’re trying to make the earbuds fit more securely they power off or turn the volume down, even though I’m being careful and trying to just touch the rims. All in all, I prefer the wireless with a cord around the back of the neck from the Dollar General ($12) over these, they were loud, had buttons on the neck piece to control them and I never accidentally turned em off, they had actual noise cancellation, comfortable, and they lasted 6 months before the cord shorted out and they quit. I don’t recommend TOZO T10.

title: Not great.
body: Update: I've been using these for about a month now. Bought two pairs and my wife has bow stopped using them because they fall out of her ear. Battery life is not what they said it is. Especially true if you are in the heat. I got two hours out of them before having to recharge. Below is my original review and still true.I have plenty of other headphones to compare to include wireless dre beats, wireless jaybirds and base, but don't have experience with off brands. I purchased two of these for my wife and I to mainly be a comm asset when participating in online meetings.The good: First, they did the intended purpose of sounding clear on the line and meeting participants heard me loud and clear. Amazing for the price...considering the fact that I almost bought the pair that I actually wanted at $185 x 2. Another great feature is the wireless charging which comes handy for us. Music came through better than dre beats, but worse than jaybirds with nice clear sound and decent base.The bad: My biggest gripe is how big and how much they stick out of your ear when wearing them. Its awkward to see it sticking out so far vs some of the others I have seen at the gym. Wouldn't be a big deal but the heaviness makes them slightly bounce when you run with them, therefore making them fall out. I tried all of the buds that came with them but didn't find a solid fit. My wife was even worse, they all either fell out of her ear or were way too big! I purchased an $8 set of new buds with wings and they worked a little better, but you have to pull them off every time you put the headphones back in the charger.Overall, I think I made a decent purchase for the price but with all the negatives probably wouldn't recommend to anyone. I like the sound, case and look, but the design features fall short vs some of the competition. I honestly didn't want them sticking out that far, but now I have to deal with it. Kind of looks and feels like I'm wearing a Bluetooth headset vs an Avenger's or Navy Seal communications piece.

title: Horrible for calls, slow to pair, primitive for the price, but music quality is okay
body: When I bought these, I only used them for music and they seemed fine. As I was unfamiliar with a variety of earbuds, I did not know that these are actually very limited compared to other ones. Many competitive products like the $59 TaoTronics SoundLiberty 77 Earbuds are also waterproof but provide controls to change volume, track, play/pause, and even use Google Assistant/Siri directly from the earbuds. That alone makes these earbuds very limited and and not a very good value, but I had a much bigger problem with these.The audio for calls would only pick up voice at about half volume or lower. If I get really loud, only then can the person on the other end of the call understand me. Since getting these, I've purchased several other earbuds for different purposes and when tested with my phone or my spouse's phone, they all work perfect for calls except the TOZO earbuds. The issue is not with my devices or setup, it is specifically the TOZO ear buds. I contacted support and after 2 weeks they responded asking if I would accept a 20% refund asking if I could please not return these. At the end of the day, why would I want a slightly lower priced pair of earbuds that still do not work?Additionally, pairing is extremely slow with these. When I take the earbuds out, it takes 5 seconds to pair with each other, and then another 10 seconds to pair with my phone. Basic SoundPEATS earbuds or the TaoTronics SoundLiberty 77 earbuds, for instance, instantly pair with each other and your device. If my phone starts ringing or my laptop has an incoming business call, I simply put those in my ear and can casually answer the call by the second ring.Horrible microphones, horrible customer service. Do your homework and find a better product. I originally purchased the TOZO earbuds based on the quantity of reviews thinking it must be good if thousands of people rated them 4/5 stars. This product falls short in so many areas and only has Chinese based support.

title: Connectivity is the Absolute Worst
body: When I first tried out these headphones I was amazed. The sound quality is amazing, and while listening to them that night the battery life was great too. But, besides those two things, they are absolutely awful. For instance if you have your phone in your pocket and the earbuds in, they will constantly be blipping in and out. If you are sitting by the pool and your phone isn't basically a foot away from your head you at almost the same height you will never have a consistent signal. If you're working out and lay your phone down on the ground, your connection will slip in out still. If you're running and happen to carry your phone in the same hand that your fit bit is on, you won't be able to listen to anything for all the ins and outs. I had initially been extremely excited about these, water proof and bluetooth. Perfect for working out or floating in the pool with. I was unfortunately extremely disappointed in that after several attempts to use I have found out that they hardly work at the pool at all. As well as besides that, if the phone they are connected to isn't within 5 feet with an unblocked "line of sight" between phone and head phones, as well as you not being that active, they don't work. Now to clarify what I mean by not working or blipping out, etc, is that they cut out. They don't disconnect from the device, you just lose the music playing in the earbuds. The song does not actually stop, so when the earbuds allow you to keep listening you're at a completely different part of the song. Most Bluetooth headphones/earbuds only do this when you walk out of range. These do it all the time. In fact I'm a tall guy and they do it if I'm standing still and the phone is at my feet. On that same note while they do fit in the ear pretty well, they easily fall out if you're working out. Very disappointed, and very unhappy since I have to now wait on new ones so I can have music while working out, or at work.

title: "Water resistant"
body: I got these headphones for when I am at the spa. I don't submerge them, but I wanted waterproof headphones for when I'm sitting in the hot tub/steam room or showering.The issue is that these are TOUCH headphones (not push button). Anything that is touch sensitive is also water sensitive. This means that water can change the settings (ever get a drop of water on your touchscreen phone and it will act like that was your finger? same thing here).Out of the box, they were spectacular. I loved the battery pack box, the way they fit in my ears, how easy they were to set up, and the sound quality. The magnetic case was also great.So I put them in, and go to the shower. I didn't point water at them, but I did rinse my hair. A SINGLE water droplet contact will CHANGE THE SETTINGS.My phone is in my locker as I'm listening to Dr Phil, and suddenly, the volume goes all the way down. Then it goes all the way up. Then it fast forwarded to the next youtube video. Then it was a fun game of play/pause as I wrapped my head. This is because these headphones are touch sensitive, and water is conductive the way your fingers are.I fixed the volume and video, and went to the hot tub with my hair tied up. Their performance was terribly unreliable. They'd be fine for 5 minutes, then suddenly the video would do the same exciting adjustments. THEN THE VOLUME PERMANENTLY LOWERED BY 50%, and I could hardly hear my video over the (quiet) tub jets. Even with the volume up 100%, I could barely hear my program. I turned them off and on, and became completely frustrated, because the dampness made these headphones unresponsive and crazy.I do not recommend for any damp environments. These are not good for showers, jogging in the rain, wet areas, or even if you put them on with wet hair, your hair will change the settings.Ridiculous. Returning.

title: Rip off, plain and simple.
body: First off, let me say that I was tricked into making a review for this product.I bought this after checking all of the many reviews on Amazon saying that this was a great product. There were a ton of favorable reviews. After receiving the product, I opened it up and saw that there was a 1-year warranty that you need to activate. Upon activating, I received a note from the company saying "We'll give you a free wireless charger if you post a review on this product. But, shhh, don't publicize this." Should have known that was BS. I followed the directions given, posted the review, and then provided proof via pasting the review title into a field and submitting. Never heard anything back and never received my wireless charger.Aside from the shady business practices, I'll post my honest review here of actual product. The sound quality is great-there is no doubt about that. The fit is not great, and the ear buds hang out of my head at weird angles and look goofy. Not a big deal. They don't fall out or anything, and like I said, they sound great...when they can connect. Pairing is easy enough, and the battery life isn't bad. The problem is with the wireless receivers. They have interference ALL THE TIME. Even when my phone is in my front jacket pocket (which is less than three feet from my right ear bud), if I turn my head to the right just slightly, the connection is disrupted. I assume that this is due to the lack of direct line-of-sight to the right ear bud from my phone, blocked by my shoulder. This is a huge design flaw indicative of very under-powered receivers in the product itself. To compare, I have a very cheap wireless speaker that will not lose connection to my iphone if I walk all the way across the yard.In short, don't be fooled by the reviews or the shady business practices. It's not worth the time or money.

title: Dysfunctional
body: I’m sorry I can’t sit by and see these be the most highly rated after suffering with these for so long. I began my search for a new pair of earbuds just now and encountered this problematic pair as the top result. I bought these in December of 2018 and essentially gave up on them.Firstly battery, the battery life is less than 3 hours, on top of that they don’t charge properly on the ports, either it doesn’t charge one earbud at all despite it being in properly, or it will leave one with drastically less charge than the other despite leaving them there until “fully charged”. What makes this annoying is that now you must sit through having the device beep at an obscenely loud volume and yell in your ear that it’s low battery every 5 seconds until it shuts off, that’s totally unnecessary.“Can work one bud at a time” is also a lie. I’ve never had the left one work independently from the right one. If I try to use just one it will not connect to my device until the second one is turned on as well, if I shut one off it shuts them both off. I lost the right earbud for about a week and tried using only the left and it would not work, it refused to connect at all and not until I found the right bud did they work again. The only way one earbud works at a time is if the battery died on the other one after it already connected and it leaves you with just the one with charge left. Now what design flaw exists here? It continuously beeps to inform you that the second earbud isn’t connected anymore so it totally interferes with what you’re listening to or watching, so even if only one is on and working, it beeps throughout the entire time completely ruining your listening experience.Either I got a defective pair or this product is horrible and I cannot understand why it’s so well reviewed, but people need to know these flaws.

title: Company wanted 5 stars before resolving my issue...
body: First off, these are supposed to come with different sized ear cups. The ones that came with it were way too big for my ear canal, and no matter what, they would not stay in. I contacted the manufacturer and their response was, give us a 5 star review and send a screenshot and we will send you a replacement. Why in the world would I give a 5 star review on something that didn't have what it was supposed to, included. Therefore, I feel that many of the 5 star reviews you see are not really what the experience actually was. I just gave them to my husband to wear while mowing the lawn. Well apparently I didn't miss much, because he wore them twice. He said that after about 5 minutes the first time one stopped working, then it would fade in and out and the second time it done the same thing and then just stopped and only one worked. I had the same issue with the fading in and out on one side when I tried to wear them, but I couldn't stand trying to keep cramming them in my ears. So, I thought it was just where I had to keep pushing them back into my ear. But apparently that's just how the ones I bought work. Anyhow, I don't appreciate that a company wouldn't try to fix the issue before expecting a 5 star review. To me, that's misleading for other people who look to reviews to help decide on a purchase. Unfortunately, I know there's lots of misleading and fake reviews on here, that's why it's important to me to always leave an honest review on my purchases. I may have just got a defective or returned pair, since it was missing the earcaps. These may be fine, if you get a pair that comes with what it says, and works longer than 5 minutes. But if you have an issue that requires contacting the manufacturer, plan on having to send a screenshot of your 5 star review before they address it...even if they do. I should have just returned them. :/

title: Adjusting to earbuds from LG Tone Infinim headphones with neck collar
body: I love how small these are!This is the first time that I’m using Bluetooth earbuds that don’t have a wire or collar hanging around your neck. Most recently I’ve been using LG Tone Infinim HBS 910 for about 2 years, so I’m adjusting to just having earbuds as compared to a collar around my neck with buttons.One thing I liked about my LG headphones were the buttons, which allowed me to turn the unit off/on, adjust volume, navigate tracks, answer/hang up calls, and get Siri’s attention. However, I don’t think I’ll miss these buttons too much.I’m getting used to having just one button on this device. Fortunately I have an Apple Watch, so I find myself changing volume and navigating tracks on my Apple Watch instead of having to pull my phone out of my pocket and use it.I also don’t really need a button for getting Siri’s attention - I can just say, ‘Hey, Siri.’I love how small these and the box are. They fit in my pockets easily whereas my old LG Tone Infinim headphones did not. Therefore, when I needed to remove my LG headphones for a short time, I often forgot where I’d left them. I wouldn’t have had this problem if they just fit in my pocket like these new T10s.Features that would be really nice in future models:1. Each time you pair, the device tells you if it has low, medium or high battery.2. Work on reducing lag between laptop video and laptop sound. At least with my HP Elitebook, when watching the same video with my wired Apples headphones and these wireless earbuds, the wireless earbuds had a slight lag whereas the wired headphones had no lag.Update on October 16, 2020 - I’ve had these for six months and now the left one no longer works. I followed the trouble shooting procedure and it didn’t work.

title: Kinda Mediocre, But I Can't Get Them to Fit Anymore
body: These were my first every wireless earbuds, and frankly, their price shows.First off, the sound quality, though I'm not an audiophile by any stretch, was kinda mediocre. I've heard better audio come out of a computer monitor's speakers, as the sound from these sounded just a little bit muffled. There's also supposed to be noise canceling, and I can notice it happening.Secondly, the touch controls are just overly sensitive. You could wear them in the shower, get some bubbles from your shampoo on them, and then they think your trying to increase or decrease the volume. The controls also sometimes get triggered when you're putting them on or taking them off unless you make absolutely sure to only be grabbing it from the side. Sometimes, when you want to raise the volume, it'll skip songs, or reverse/go back to the previous song when you want to lower the volume. Very inconsistent.Third, the battery life wasn't that great. I could listen to music for a few hours, and then it would beep at me, telling me the battery was low. Although, I guess that's what you get for earbuds at this price point nowadays.The last one is something I ran into recently, and is more of a personal thing (I have had this problem with other buds of a similar shape). I can't ever get these to fit decently. Sure, there are changeable gel tips for these, but none of them work, and they either leave the buds feeling loose, and/or the buds cause physical discomfort for me. Again, this doesn't go for just this particular set of earbuds, but it's still something I dislike about them.I would say, save up for something better. These aren't worth your time or money, especially if you have issues with this particular style of earbud like I do.

title: Not worth the trouble
body: I bought a pair of wireless ear buds from Amazon about 6 months ago. They were good and I liked them but I gave them to my wife when she started her new job and found she could listen to music at work. So I went to order the exact same pair but they were out of stock. After searching I found these and since the reviews were mostly positive I ordered them. To my surprise they actually sound better than my original pair that I gave away. They were about the same price so I was expecting the quality to be similar. These sound great and the batter life is about 4hrs which is acceptable for me because I never use them for more than a couple hrs. I have only 2 complaints and they're both minor. First, my original pair had skip functions for music so u can skip tracks without pulling your phone out. This pair doesn't have any phone control other than play music, pause, answer calls, and redial last number. Play and pause is expected but no volume control or track skip. Minor complaint. My second minor gripe is the size of the charging case is bigger than my original pair. Slightly too big for pockets although they make up for it by including a strap for wearing the charging case around your neck like a necklace. Not the most ideal way to carry but its better than trying to pocket such a hefty little case. I would recommend these to anyone looking for good sounding earbuds on a budget*update*After 6 months I've started using these more regularly and they honestly suck. The battery life isn't great for the earbuds AND the charging case. They also randomly disconnect from my phone or just "power off" for no reason at all. I'm goin to order a name brand pair because I hate these.

title: They "Upgraded" these from the "10-R" version, but they're a MASSIVE DOWNGRADE
body: I had bought a pair of these early last year and they turned out pretty good. I bought a new pair for my sister because she liked mine so much.Turns out the ones I bought last year show up her phone as "TOZO-T10" where as mine show up as "TOZO-T10-R".The case was changed to be made much cheaper (feels lighter too? maybe they thinned the plastic on the case??).The hinge on the case changed to be much cheaper and easier to break.The earbuds themselves no longer have actual physical "buttons", but some weird pressure sensor that has no feedback whatsoever to what you're actually doing.They also changed how the earbuds work by putting both the POWER on/off feature on the same "HOLD" action as increasing/decreasing audio. This means you're going to constantly be accidently changing the audio CONSTANTLY when trying to power off the earbuds, etc. You're very likely to blow your ears out next time you power them on if you're not careful!!!!The pressure sensor they're using now is EXTREMELY sensitive which means putting them in your ears or taking them out is going to be a constant struggle of pausing/playing music.Overall I'd highly recommend NOT buying these and find something else. TOZO decided to make their product cheaper, less intuitive to use, and overall a more frustrating experience.Massive waste of money. On top of that they replaced the previous listing for the TOZO-T10-R with this one without saying anything. This means we can't even order the "Good" product anymore!!!TLDR; Find something else, TOZO is shady and made their product cheaper, worse to use, and hid the old version.

title: Amazing sound with terrible quality control.
body: So this is my 3rd pair of Tozo headphones that I have purchased. The first for my nephew, the second for my roommate, and this pair for me. It has been a roller coaster of emotions.Let's start with the positives: First off let me say how amazing these headphones sound. The bass is thick, the mids don't get lost easily, and the fit is fantastic. The sound cancelling is top notch and I have to actually take the buds out to have a normal conversation. The battery life is pretty solid, and the charging case is compact, well built, and fits nicely just about anywhere. Wireless charging is solid and the extra tips will guarantee a nice fit.Now here's the bad: The quality control with these is terrible. The first set was fine, no issues. The second set had a faulty charging case and had to be replaced. Now with my set the bluetooth is barely functional. The left slave earbud will not consistently stay paired to its partner no matter how many times things are synced and resysnced. The bluetooth range is also non-existent. With a direct path to the phone, the range is about 10 feet. If I put my phone in my pocket or in my backpack that range drops to 2-3 feet. If I turn my head too far the buds will just unsync.I've not yet reached out to Tozo for a replacement, because I mostly have been using them at work to listen to music at my desk. However these were purchased for gym use and in their current state I don't see how they would be able to function.I really want to love these, because Airpods just don't fit in my ears comfortably. Unfortunately I'll probably have to return them and pick up a pair of either Sennheiser Momentum or Bose SoundSport.

title: Does not work independently and has a long recharge.
body: So I want to preface this with a statement saying that this company, Tozo, attempts to bribe people to leave good reviews, so keep that in mind with all those 5 star reviews. Now for my actual review. It sounds good, Takes way too long to get a full charge when a full charge only lasts like 2.5 to 3 hours. You know those old RC cars where you charge them as much as you can use them? Yeah that's what these reminded me of. Waterproof seems to work fine though. They are comfortable in my ears at least. Won't work without both being connected to each other and if one is on the charger they both disconnect. One will not work independently unless the other is in your pocket, which is not independent functionality, so dont listen to those questions that are answered that say they work independently. The low battery warning is about 10 seconds before they die, and interrupts whatever you are listening to with s voice telling you. Should just be a low beeping in the background, maybe on a future product? Also, true independent functionality, with the water proofing and a full charge in about 15 to 20 minutes, I'd pay a little over 100 bucks for if you guys had it. I tested these and the link between the two can be spotty, seems to be rare but it happens. One loses signal from the other and you hear the beeps as it tries to reconnect and then they work again. With that, I have maintained a connection between the two from about 50 ft away, more and they disconnect. I do really enjoy the design, and their charge pad, nice quality charge pad and gorgeous but I'll go write that over there too. The case has a very strong and satisfying snap as it closes that is nerd asmr.

title: Maybe 4th brand of ear buds will be the best...NOT
body: UPDATE Nov 2021: After a year the buds only keep a charge for one hour before recharged need. I bought another brand to replace them. FIRST REVIEWED Oct 2020: Amazon Prime Day got me shopping and I landed on ear buds. The buds I had before this one wrapped around neck and then plugged into ears. I didn't like the cord around neck and potential to catch on something and be pulled out. And there were other problems. The price for these buds is very competitive with other brands. Also 4.5 Stars with 115K reviews on Amazon convinced me to get the buds. Only 4 of 5 stars due to some limitations. 1. One can't increase/decease volume on buds as you must use phone. 2. You can't place a phone call/text with a button push or voice command on buds. Again use phone. My previous wireless ear buds allowed me to change volume and place calls/text. The Bluetooth pairing with my Samsung Galaxy phone was quick and simple. The buds come with medium sized rubber fittings installed but you also get small, large and extra large fittings. This allows you to use the size that best fits your ears. I experimented and went with the extra large set as they provide a more complete seal in ear canal keeping out much surrounding noise. This is important to me as I use the buds almost only for listening to audio books and blocking out ambient noise lets me hear the spoken words. For users who listen to music this wouldn't be as important. Recharging is very simple as magnets grab the buds when you are about to put them in the recharging case. Watch the blue indicator lights on carrying case to know when to recharge the box. When the buds need to be recharged you will get an audio prompt.

title: Sound Quality Important and All Functions Work and They Stay In EARS
body: UPDATE: Day 2: First let me say that customer support was great; asked them about the tips problem I reported yesterday, that was a Sunday and got a response within a couple of hours. Did not expect to hear back until today Monday.Now for today: Had to reset the earbuds as they would not increase/decrease the volume level. Holding the buttons would only turn the earbuds on or off, no other functions so followed the instructions to reset them. Did so, and they worked OK for a short period of time than back to the only function being to turn them on or off. Reset them and again had control for only a few minutes and then experienced the same issue. So, I am returning them. Disappointed as they seemed to be the best earbuds I have tried, sound was great. I need earbuds that function so I can use them primarily as hearing aids. Really like by Apple earbud pros but they tend to fall out of my ears. whereas these stayed in even when eating.***************************************Just got these today and they came 3/4 charged. Sound quality is excellent for music. Easy to pair to iPhone. Used wife's phone to call me and one tap answered the phone. holding the right earbud increases volume and holding the left earbud decreases the volume, wide volume range, I use about 30% level which is comfortable for me. I also use these as hearing aids and they seem OK for that purpose. Only issue was that the "kit" of ear tips was incomplete, sizes missing and some appeared to be missing the "red" part that holds the tip to the earbud; I've contacted the seller and will update after I hear back. Other than that issue, I am initially satisfied.

title: Worked Well for a very limited time then became useless
body: I am so disappointed. When I first got these headphones I could not help but rave about them to friends and family, but less than 2 months later and they are practically useless. The headphones stopped pairing with each other and no amount of troubleshooting has allowed me to get the left bud to connect to the right or vice versa. I can listen to one headphone at a time which, to me, is useless. What is the point of noise cancelling headphones when you can only use one.I should've read the other one star reviews instead of seeing the large quantity of positive reviews and assuming there would be no issues.Additionally, I don't think the headphones are very user friendly. Forget any other headphone controls because the button controls are different, and I found myself thinking I would be skipping a song and then redialing the last number that called me (not that I could talk to them with the microphone quality of these headphones). I found it very frustrating that the headphones would randomly turn on when in their case without me realizing. Forget having it paired up with multiple devices (phone, laptop, etc.) it just doesn't take into consideration all the things people could use them for.My last complaint is that there was very little volume range. It seemed like I turned up the volume one notch (on the iPhone) and it was already at half the volume other headphones would normally be but then increasing the volume at all would have little effect until you had the volume all the way up.I contacted the seller to see if there was anything they could do, I will update this review if they respond.

title: Update: Great Customer Service Poor Quality  - Great Quality and Sound
body: Update: these stopped holding a charge and the left ear bud died completely, these are not good quality. Update 3: after my review and 1 star rating the company contacted me to offer a new updated pair of headphones. This was weeks after the bad review. They could have just left me hanging but they went above and beyond to make it right. I still wouldn’t recommend this pair but the new version is sleeker has super fast charging and has good sound quality. The sound is a little echoey but it is surround and the quality is good. Also there are more ear pads for a better fit and an 18 month warranty.Update 2: The sound quality is now horrible. I cannot hear what I'm trying to listen to. These only lasted a few months. Do yourself a favor and buy a decent pair. These are cheap and will probably die way too early. I cannot return them and so they are in the trash.Update: So I took off a few stars. The battery life for this is not good. If I don't use it for a week. It is almost assured to be dead when I put them on. The charging case dies too quick and the headphones drain without use. It is disappointing and I would not recommend these any longer.These are great. The sound is excellent. The battery life will last a couple of days. The charger is nice and convenient. There are several adapters for a solid fit.The only complaint is that the R and L do not light up so it is difficult to see which is which. However, these are really good other than the minor lighting issue.In fact, my first pair was stolen and I liked them so much I bought another pair immediately.

title: Left Ear bud cuts out (Updated 8/8/19)
body: Truly a 1 Star experience with these, Left ear bud cuts out very frequently even on the replacement set.8/08/19: Went on a 30 minute walk this morning with Replacements... Cut out about once everyone minute or two. Super frustrated and upset. Returning for a REFUND and will look else where for a quality pair that works as it's supposed to.8/07/19: Received replacement set. Went for 45 minute walk, seems to be promising, only cutout once or twice.8/04/19: Day 2 of trying to use Earbuds.... Still having cutting in and out issues with Left ear bud, getting super frustrating. Tried many different pairing tricks others suggested with no luck.Just submitted a Request for replacement from Amazon. Will update once replacement arrives.8/03/19:Just got these earbuds today. Was easy to pair to phone and started listening to music within seconds.... that's about the end of the positives.Put phone in pants pocket and started my walk... 30 seconds in I notice the left cutting in and out every 10 seconds or so. I stand still and it works fine... start walking and left bud cuts in and out time to time.I turn buds off.. unpair and forget device from phone. Turn buds on they pair to each other and say "right channel", "left channel".. i then pair to phone and all seems well again... until i start walking. Left cuts in and out every 10 seconds or so....They seem to work only if you sit still but that is NOT what I bought them for. I NEED something for walking. Will try few extra tips and if no resolve, will return for replacement or refund.

title: It’s not terrible, but it does suck and isn’t worth your money. Find something else.
body: Sound quality is alright but the battery life sucks. I had a different brand of wireless and those could last 32 hours+, I could sleep with them one night and still have significant battery, these are dead before the day is over!Another con is that they fall out so easily! When I want to jog and jam to my favorite songs they slip out and ruin my high that comes from music. I also have to keep adjusting them to get the best sound quality and so they aren’t muffled by being directly pressed against the wall of my ear; it’s hard to get it perfectly positioned.The worst thing about these is the charging. The wireless case they rest in is super cute and portable but it does a terrible job of actually charging the buds. For some reason, it only charges one bud at a time even if you put both in, and even if the charge light comes on for both. It confused me, when after a full nights charge it would say half battery and within 10 freaking minutes one bud would say low battery and shut off (calling this bud #1). Then after another ten minutes, if the other bud is still in (bud #2), the empty battery will go to full (showing the charge of the 2nd bud). Point is: they can’t charge at the same time for some reason so you have to alternate which bud gets charged then find time to switch to the opposite after an hour or two.My experience with these buds was disappointing, and I got them at a lower cost because of a sale; I still say NOT WORTH IT. Because of these issues. I’m not sure if it’s a defect with mine specifically, or if this was the reason they went on sale, but I can tell you this sucks.

title: Uncomfortable, heavy, and poor battery life.
body: "Amazon's Choice": gives me the impression these are just overstock items that have reached the end of their life.Feels VERY VERY deceptive for them to be labelled as anyone who would recommend these over the other competitors who offer much better quality and over-all value.Amazon would only recommend these if their profit ratio is much higher on selling these over others.All of the exhange-able ear-bud caps this comes with, have a rigid hard plastic circle ring around them.It might be to ensure there's a snug fit, however it's hard and uncomfortable and will probably be irritating for most people, like myself.The battery only lasts two to two and a half hours. Which is very poor in comparison to similarly priced competitor's.The recharge time feels pretty poor for the battery life as well.The case was kind of nice, about it really.The earbuds are kind of bulky, and more importantly fairly heavy for what they are actually. (sounds a bit odd, but they're definitely quite a bit heavier than other brands, for whatever reason.).I would NOT recommend these at all.These should be $10-$15ish at the most in cost - in some dollar style bin at office max or office depot for a "back to school section" or something along those lines.Curious stocking stuffer or otherwise. Save your money, spend the extra $15-20 for a much much much better value and pair of wireless earbuds. Avoid these. I doubt the batteries will have much of a charge after awhile, so you'd probably have to replace them within a year as well.

title: Misleading information. Only can use right earbud alone.
body: I really wanted to like these headphones after seeing all the reviews. I am not sure what the expectations where for the other reviewers but these headphones did not even come close to my expectations. Below are my issues with the headphones1) Made for only one size of ear. It did not come with any extra ear pieces for different size ears.2) This was the biggest issue with me, you are only able to use the right earbud alone. The left one (or slave one as stated in the instruction manual that came with it) cannot be used by itself. No where does it state this by the way in the manual or in the item description that I could find. This means that when the right one runs out of battery life, you have no headphones. This is very misleading not including this information in the description or the manual.3) I am not sure as to what the 4 blue lights are supposed to indicate in the charging case but it showed 4 blue lights when I put the drained earbuds in the case and 1 blue lights when I removed them 2 hours later. I see where people are saying they can get 4 charges out of the case but not sure how that works exactly.4) Left earbud sounds louder then right one when they are both in. I have never noticed this difference with any of the other earbuds that i have purchased in the past.I hate having to write this review but now I have to go through the trouble of returning these and purchasing another pair. If the sellers of these earbuds happen to read this, it would be very helpful if you would please put that the left one is useless without the right one. Thanks.

title: Hyperactive Touch Sensitivity is the Worst!
body: I bought these b/c of the waterproof rating and I like listening to music in the shower but unfortunately my shower is one thin wall away from my spouse's home office desk. Not wanting to be disruptive, I usually skip the music. Needless to say, I was so excited to try these out!They are waterproof, but extremely frustrating. The buttonless touch is way too sensitive. If ANYTHING touches the earbud, it triggers an action.The one time I used these, my hair brushing over the bud, as well as the shower water hitting the bud, all triggered an action. To make matters worse, there's a voice confirmation (ex: "Volume Maximum") with each touch.So I was listening to music and water droplets hitting the buds had the buds interrupting my music to say "volume maximum volume maximum volume maximum volume maximum" with each droplet.Even when I momentarily popped out 1 bud and its touch surface rested on my counter it kept repeating "volume minimum volume minimum volume minimum volume minimum volume minimum" until I moved it!I was in a car with the windows down and my hair strands gently swirling around my ears triggered it, as did laying against a pillow with the buds in my ears.Not only that, but I have smaller ears I guess, and these stick out. Not just the part that goes in my ear canal, but the main part that sits in my outer ear.So basically, as long as you're sitting perfectly upright and NOTHING goes near your ear these will work as intended.Extremely disappointed. Getting a refund.

title: Mediocre Earbuds at a Low Price-- Buyer Beware
body: These were great in the first month-- They charged properly, played sound at equal volumes, and were pretty well what I expected. The in ear pieces that span the ear canal are oddly wide, and it's hard to find comfortable replacements from other ear buds I've had. I've used them almost every single day for 4+ hours while working, exercising, and doing yard work. They have a habit of coming a bit loose in the ear and need an adjustment in somewhat recurring intervals,. It may be my ears and how they fit, but I put in the smallest ear pieces to get them a bit more snug and that hasn't helped either. The larger ones were always pushing out of the ear every minute or two if I was active.The second month is when it went downhill. They wouldn't charge properly. After picking them up and putting them back in the case multiple times, the left ear bud finally is detected and begins charging. Sometimes it'll charge all the way up, sometimes it'll be connected for a bit then not charge. The contacts were cleaned, there's no junk on them preventing a proper connection.Then the volume in the right ear bud drastically dropped. It's barely half the volume of the left. I tried turning the volume all the way up and down to see if somehow they got paired with differing volumes, but this did not fix anything. I tried to un-pair them from my device and pair them again, but there was no change. The right driver is dying after 2 months.Bought these in mid July, and by September I am having to find another pair. Find a better solution than these.

title: No better than the T6 and still has a lot of issues.
body: I was having issues with the T6 earbuds with battery and connection issues, so I bought these hoping those problems was fixed. But they are still there and there is additional issues. If it wasn't past my return date I would return these and not buy another Tozo product.So the issues...1) battery life, battery life is shorter than my T6, these last for about 6 hours with continous play. I use these at work to help pass the 12 hour shifts. I got to the point I have to use one at a time to prolonge usage due to the battery.2) connection issues, sometimes when taking out of the charger it won't turn on. Not even holding down the button will do anything. Only way to get it to turn on is to put it back into the charger and then take it out.3) touch sensitivity, when wearing a large hat like a beanie, the contact surface will trigger the play/pause without me even touching it. When I do touch the controls it will sometimes not recognize the correct amount of taps. The T6 was a little better in regards to this4) included ear pieces. Even when using the best fitting ear piece depending on what I'm doing it will fall out.The only upgrades this offer over the T6 is none.... It has a bigger charger which is more awkward to fit in your pocket, the touch button graphic is sightly different, and there is a different voice.Only thing different with the voice is the "low battery" prompts is very quiet now where you hardly can hear it. The T6 was loud and interrupted you when you was trying to work.

title: Good Headphone but a little big
body: Updated: these units are highly frustrating. When I use then for voice calls the person on the other end isn't able to understand me. Also if I am slightly active they cut in an out to the point that at times I can barely understand what is being communicated over them whether it is music over Pandora or listening to a podcast. I have had to fall back to my Bluetooth receiver and headphones. Boo!These are a good set of headphones with nice battery life. That quality of sound is pretty good though a little on the brass side not quite enough bass. They are big and don't quite fit comfortably into my ear so I can't wear them for extended lengths of time.
