Also I using one (1) headphone is irritating, when I remove the right one from it's base it connects fine but if I don't take out the left one as well I get this constant beep in the right one, would be nice as I am sometimes in areas that I need to be able to hear in one ear. It would be nice if they had an app I could install on my phone to see battery life and maybe other controls. I like the ability to charge on a Qi wireless charger so I don't have to fumble for a chord all the time and the headphones charge quite quickly too. They stay in the ear well and I haven't had one fall out yet. Voice seems to be clear as my wife didn't seem to notice when I was wearing them and talking to her or talking on my LG G6.Over all decent sound for the price with decent battery, a little big for my ears, would like an app and the ability to only use one ear piece at times.

title: great value, amazing sound but won't stop skipping
body: I really like music and usually spend $$$ on equipment. The over the ear wired headset I was using was a high-end unit is a Bowers and Wilkins P5 ($185) which has sound that is hard to match. I purchased Apple earbuds last year but didn't like the sound. These appear to be made quite well and fit my ear perfectly. I think the bass is fine and natural sounding. If you like a ton of bass for listening to rap music, these probably will disappoint. The key here is NATURAL sounding bass and this it has. I listened to them for about 90 minutes in the first try and they stayed connected throughout use. They were very comfortable to wear and although they provided 4 other ear tips, I found the ones they arrived with were perfect for me. They might not match the sound reproduction of my Bowers and Wilkins P5s that cost $185 but they are close.*** UPDATE*** I've changed my rating from 4 stars to 1 because after about 3 weeks the left ear bud began to skip, meaning the sound breaks up for a second as the BT signal fails to keep a consistent connection. This got worse so I returned these and got a new set with the same result. This seems to be a design issue since ONLY the left bud is ever effected and this happens on both sets I bought. When you read other reviews, they have complained about the same issue. FYI- I use them with and IPHONE 12 running the latest software and it makes no difference whether the iPhone is in my pocket or 2 feet in front on me. It's a shame because the sound is really very good for the money.

title: Defective Left Earbud (2nd update)
body: Sound kept dropping out in the left earbud. Not sure whether it was a bad Bluetooth connection or a short in the earbud. Battery life was right at the 3 hour life as advertised. I am sure this would have gotten worse over time. I returned the product after using two times.07/24/19 update: Vendor contacted me and offered to send me a new set of earbuds free of charge to test and review. I am revising my original one star rating to three stars for good customer service. Hopefully the new earbuds will deserve a much higher rating.08/04/2019 update: I received the new earbuds on Tuesday, July 30th. After fully charging them and pairing them to my Samsung Galaxy S8 phone, I used them on my exercise walk the following morning. Unfortunately, within less than a minute of starting my walk, the left earbud started losing the signal and continued to do so for the next five minutes of me trying to get them to work. After 5 minutes, I gave up and turned them off and put them in my pocket. For those that are wondering, I wear my phone in an armband on my left arm, so distance to the phone is certainly not the issue. Just to be sure that the problem was not related to my phone, I paired the earbuds to my wife's IPod and tried again to walk with them while listening to music. The problem continued to happen with the iPod, so I know the earbuds are defective. Because of this, I am reducing my product rating back to one star. I appreciate the company trying to make me happy, but I am unable to endorse their product.

title: My reasoning for 4/5 stars.
body: Sound is great. It's clear, you can feel the bass, changing the equilizar settings are definitely noticable.Noise cancelling is great. I have a hearing range above a normal person. To give you an idea, I can hear electrical currents and air separating when a door opens. These muffle out everything around me and people talking sound like blurbs. If your hearing is normal, then you shouldn't hear anything. If your hearing is below average, consider an alternative since the bass can drown out the treble.Battery life sucks. I charged these for a full 24 hours when I received them, but they still die after 2 hours. I work a shift of 5 hours unloading semis and don't expect these to last a full 5 hour shift. However, considering it is advertised as lasting 4 hours I'd expect it to do as such. Also, the constant reminder of battery low is really obnoxious.Hold up well against sweat so far. As you can imagine, semi truck trailers are extremely hot and we live in Florida so heat is trapped. Making sweat a must. I've had headphones stop working because of my sweat, but these don't. So far anyway.Controls are a bit iffy, but easy to get used to.Update 5/26/21I had left this a 4/5 stars before, but now it's a 1 for me. It's been a little over a year since buying them, but they have completely crapped out. I only used these for meditation or calls. About 5 months ago, a bad humming started coming through the headphones and making it very difficult to enjoy anything.

title: completely unacceptable battery life and random 1-2 sec cutouts
body: I got these so I could use them at work (warehouse job), I only use one of the two earbuds, with the volume at the lowest setting my phone can do (so i am not pumping the volume at all), essentially just background music, so I can still hear what's going on around me or listen to others talking.However even with the volume at near silent levels, these earbuds suffer from piss poor battery life, I literally can only get about 1 to 2 hours of playback before the music stops and i hear "disconnected" and the earbud goes dead and wont turn back on. On top of this, over that time, they constantly suffer from random brief spontaneous short cut-outs every 10 minutes or so, even though my phone is in my pocket and on me always (so not a distance or line of sight issue), it doesn't stop the music or whatever, however the audio cuts out for a second or two then resumes, and I am not streaming music, rather playing music off my phone's storage (so isn't an internet connection issue).I have used these for less than 1 week and already I am ready to return them for a refund.So in summary, the battery life is unacceptable, considering the fact that I am playing music at whisper levels, one would expect extended battery life, but only getting about 1 hour of use (2 at most) then stuck with no music until i can charge them during break, then get another hour or two of use then forced to wait to charge during lunch time, etc, yeah these are going bye bye asap after i finish submitting this review.

title: Connection issues, defective, and poor sound quality. Unbelievable.
body: [[VIDEOID:3bc961cf742490411006b80e5469d6d5]] I was excited to get my first pair of wireless earbuds for working out. These earbuds were a good deal and looked nice. I purchased them because there are so many reviews and seemed to have a good reputation.When I turned them on and used them for the first time, I was disappointed by the sound quality. Doesn't sound rich and full of bass, but rather flat and bland.I used them for a 5 hour workout and had many connection issues. My phone was less than 1 meter away from the earbuds, but if I turned my head to look behind me, the connection went out. I had to reconnect more than 3 times. I was disappointed by this. The left earbud kept on disconnecting over and over and it was super annoying.Eventually, toward the end of my workout the right earbud stopped working. I took it home charged it, but it still wouldn't connect after being on the charger for over an hour.I followed the manual's instructions to reset the earbuds, but the right earbud still wouldn't connect. It has a red light constantly blinking and it won't shut off. It won't power off or power on, and keeps on blinking with a red light. The manual doesn't say anything about this, and there is no information to be found anywhere.I can't believe how many problems this product had. Bad sound quality, consistent disconnections, and now the right earbud doesn't work. Especially considering how many reviews this product has, I am especially disappointed.

title: Very Frustrating, waste of time and money!
body: Let me tell you, I bought not one set but two sets. The first one stop working at the one-month mark. When I first got the package, they were a lot easier to set up and get connected to my android but not so much my iPhone. After one month of gentle use, I start to notice the statics, the mic wasn’t good for phone calls, the person on the other end of the phone call couldn’t hear me. I couldn’t return them back since it has been over a month already. I felt stuck dealing with a defective product; especially; after reading all the positive reviews. I thought I was the exception. And coming from dealing with Skullcandy Indy (Skullcandy were a Total nightmare they never worked not even for a week the right couldn’t link to the left nor to the phone, a total scam) these were already much better. I truly wanted to love them. So, I told myself let me get another set probably the one I have were just a defective batch and the price was affordable/ encouraging.I received the second set of these earbuds and they acted the same way. Except these ones had issues connecting to the iPhone, but they were easily connected to the android. After about 2 months I had the same issues again. With the right one having major statics while listening to music or phone calls. A week later the right one completely stopped working regardless of how many times I charge it or reset the connection. I can see the red light on when charging the turning blue but nothing else. It’s totally dead.What a waste of money and time.

title: Stupidly Designed Wireless Earbuds for the Price...and FRUSTRATING!
body: Edit: I'm changing my review to one start after being unable to get them to disconnect from my phone even when they're in the charging case. I had to uninstall them. Stupid design.........................When I first got these earbuds, I was impressed. Setup was a breeze. Fit (once I found the right earpiece size, which was supplied in the packaging) is perfect. Sound controls were intuitive and easy to operate. Most importantly, though, I was really impressed with the sound quality. If you're looking for top of the line, best sound quality earbuds out there - these aren't for you. But if you're looking for good earbuds, with very good sound quality, and a fantastic price, well, now you know why I bought them.So, why did I only give the earbuds two stars, you might be asking. They won't disconnect or turn off. Mine might be faulty, but they stay connected to my phone at all times - even when returned to their case for a recharge. Inside the case, they stay on until they've ran out the entire battery life of both the case and the earbuds. I expected once they were in the case and the case closed that the buds would turn off and disconnect from bluetooth, but so far, I've not been able to get them to do that. I have read and followed the instructions; and, like I said, perhaps mine are just faulty. But it's very frustrating.I'll update this review if I figure out what's going on and determine it was user error, which could be the case. But, this ain't my first rodeo. :-|

title: Annoying in multiple ways
body: Ok so these are cheap, and at first I was happy with them. For running, they are pretty ok. Battery life lasts long enough for a half marathon and they stay in place. Two major annoying 'features' make them incredibly frustrating. Firstly, they automatically connect to your device when you take them out of the charger. Fine for most people, but I have 3 devices I use with them. There is no shortcut to disconnect them via the earbuds, which means you have to find the connected device, unlock it, go to bluetooth settings, and disconnect from the earbuds, then reconnect with the device you want to use. The other baffling 'feature' is that when the battery gets low, it repeats "Battery low, please charge." Like once per minute. Until they die. Which usually takes a few minutes to happen, despite the phone still indicating 20% charge. So you never get a full charge out of them, and the last few minutes are infuriating.Edit: Company also tries to buy the deletion or revision of negative reviews. They offered replacement of the product (which is unchanged and didn't fix the issues of this review) or an Amazon credit in return for deletion of the review. Paying for an artificially high user rating instead of fixing the product just serves to waste resources and further dilutes the trust of Amazon reviews. Dropped from 3 to 2 stars for this behavior.Edit 2: Dropped to 1 star since they repeatedly reach out via email to offer a Amazon gift cards in exchange for deletion of this review.

title: They're Fine-ish
body: Updated again: Headphone's volume randomly cuts in half and starts to crackle (with no change in use). These have become more of a headache than a help in some cases.I've ordered a couple pairs of these, and ordered a couple more. I LOVE them. Overall, great sound and quality for the price, and they actually stay in my ears longer than most other brands I've tried. Drawbacks- They don't fully cancel noise. While my walls are thin, I can hear people talking in the other room while wearing both ear buds. It's faint, but noticable. WORD OF CAUTION- I really loved that I could use one ear bud at a time, leaving the other one charging, until I realized that the volume was different between each ear bud and impossible to fix. I ended up ordering more ear buds because of this, as the left ear bud ended up so quiet I couldn't hear it on full volume. I will update again in a few months to see if this is a trend among all of them, or if the volume dofference was due to my previous use (which I have now changed, and use both at the same time). Highly recommend these ear buds if used correctly.Update: Dropped my review down. No matter what I do or how I use these, they seem to lose volume over time (the loudest isn't as loyd as when I first purchased). I confirmed this because I still had a new pair never opened and the volume is better with those. This has happened before and I thought I was at fault. Seems to be the ear buds. I'll be switching brands after this pair wears out.

title: Edit: NOT Very good quality
body: Edit: I made the below review on March 10, 2020. Call it the curse of the year 2020 or call these bad ear buds. Either way, they’re not good anymore. I feel that if these are going to be the same price as the official Apple wired headphones, then they should at least last a quarter as long as them. I’ve had the same Apple wired headphones for 3 years and they’re still going strong. These wireless earbuds lasted 3 months before they started going haywire. After 4 months, the left one completely stopped working. After 5 months, the right one has to be turned to full volume just to hear anything. I’m now in month 6 (yes, I’m trying to hang on till the bitter end) and the right one now connects only about half the time. I’ve followed all the directions for when one fails from the manufacturers page. First off the fact that they need to put those instructions up front is crazy. I’ve never dropped them. They’ve stayed completely dry. The case charge used to last 4 days. Now I’m lucky if I get 2 full days.So all in all....changed my review to a 1-star. Anyone want to suggest some good wireless earbuds that last but don’t cost hundreds of dollars??Old review from 3/10/20: I've just received these 3 days ago and used them daily since. So far everything is fabulous! Great sound quality, great sound, great feel, lightweight, stays in place. I literally shook my head for 30 seconds straight (ouch by the way) and they didn't budge at all! I'm happy with these thus far.

title: Laughably bad...
body: Not a single good thing to say about these earbuds... well maybe they look alright? The primary use case I bought them for is running while streaming music saved on my Apple Watch gen 3- First thing I noticed, these sound ridiculously bad. I am admittedly not an "average consumer" of consumer audio products, I work in the industry and I have 'audiophilic' tendencies. That being said, these sound awful. Zero bass response, zero high frequency definition, these sound like crappy plastic headphones you got with a budget portable CD player in 2000.- Second thing I noticed, they don't hold a charge, earbuds or case. The units I got, when sitting unused, drained battery in one day, so I put them in the case that I had charged up the day before only to find out that this was also out of charge.- Third think I noticed, they just flat out didn't work. When running, one side would cut out completely, then come back out of sync, then the other side would cut out and come back out of sync, rinse and repeat for the entire run (some walking in there too, I must admit). This was exceptionally frustrating, but then I couldn't change the volume. I'd update the volume on my watch and nothing would happen for a minute, then out of the blue they'd jump in volume and wouldn't let me turn them down.Simply not usable for their intended purpose, and seeing as how that purpose was simply listening to music via bluetooth, not acceptable. I am returning these.

title: UPDATED REVIEW:  T-10 earbuds have changed! 🙄
body: UPDATE (Summer 2021): I ordered another set of the TOZO-10 earbuds and discovered the company had “upgraded” them. Though some features might be considered improvements, one big change, which was annoying, was replacing the button controls with touch controls.🙄 I tried the new version, and I RETURNED THEM!! The new version doesn't work well in the shower, and if I adjust them, or if something else touches the controls, the earbuds pause, change volume, etc. I contacted the company to see if they still sold the previous version of the Tozo-10. They don’t. ☹️ I will say the sound is still good with these, if you don’t have issues with the controls; HOWEVER, you might prefer one of the newer TOZO products. For example, the T-6 is smaller. All of them have touch controls. I don’t know why they changed the T-10, when the T-6, T-12, and other newer, TOZO earbuds have the touch controls! (I have tried several of them.) Not everyone likes the touch control, and, especially with my long hair, I personally find the touch control annoying. Since NONE of the TOZO IPX8 earbuds, at this time, have button controls, I have changed companies and updated my review.PREVIOUS 5-STAR REVIEW of T-10 with the button controls:These are great IPX8 (waterproof) earbuds and case! Since I lost my first set, while on vacation, I purchased a second set of T-10 earbuds. I tried other similarly-priced, waterproof earbuds, but I wasn’t satisfied, until I purchased these again. Some of the pros of these earbuds: 1. The earbuds can be used together OR each one independently!! When used together, they pair with each other and one other device. If only ONE earbud is used, it will pair with two devices, such as your phone and an iPad. For example, when you answer your phone, your iPad will pause. Also, when using one earbud, the other can charge in the case.2. Because of the easy, button control, these work great in the shower. (I’ve had touch-control earbuds pause, turn off, or change volume, due to water dripping on the controls, especially with my long hair!)3. Both the earbuds AND case are rated IPX8, which is great for swimming or beach areas!! (Of course, since Bluetooth signals won’t transmit underwater, the earbuds will only receive the signal above the water. However, water won’t damage them, so showering, bathing, or swimming is possible. Check definitions of IPX8 ratings online for more information.) Always dry earbuds after getting wet, before charging them in the case.4. Easy pairing! Once the earbuds are paired, they pair automatically to the same device. However, it is also easy to change the pairing to other devices or to switch from using both earbuds to a single earbud.5. Better sound and quality than other similarly-priced earbuds I’ve tried.6. Though these are a little bit larger than the T-6, they do fit securely, if adjusted according to the included written and illustrated instructions. (Select ear cap—four sets included—that fits best and ROTATE earbud until resting securely in your ear.) I prefer these because of the button controls. These seem to be the same size as or smaller than most other similarly-priced, IPX8 earbuds; however, they fit better and more securely than some others I have tried.7. There is a volume control, as well as many other functions. (Some functions work better with both earbuds in use.) Check instruction booklet for list of other functions. (Tip: Adjust the volume either with your device or with two earbuds. When using one earbud, I recommend adjusting the volume using your paired device or using the volume previously set, while using two earbuds.).8. The LED charging indicators on the case and earbuds are very helpful. If you are looking for a good, lower-priced set of IPX8 earbuds, try these! They are great for the shower, hot tub, etc!

title: You get what you pay for
body: I'm reviewing the $29 TOZO T10 headphones (w/o noise cancellation). There are multiple problems make it hard for me to recommend. The first pair I returned because they just didn't work. The company replaced them without issue so that was good. I got the replacement pair quickly after I purchased it on April 15, 2019.The headphones have mostly worked good for the last 18 months, but to be honest they are not my go to headphones. I use my Sony WH-1000XM2 noise cancelling headphones if I want to listen to music, but I thought this inexpensive pair would be great for working in the garden, riding the bike, and shopping around town. The problems I faced are:1. Sometimes difficult to pair2. They don't keep a charge as long as I would expect3. They don't disconnect from my phone when charging4. They slip out of the ear too easily.5. If you only want to use one ear bud it gives you an annoying beep for ~ 2 minutesAs far as pairing, it is anything but automatic. 9 times out of 10 I need to go into my bluetooth settings and select the Tozo R channel to pair (choosing the L channel is not helpful). Even then it doesn't always connect the first time. Usually I can get it working within about 30 seconds though.Once paired it does not want to let go. It gives you an annoying beep when it is low on power. The beep is fine, but you only have about 1 minute to power them and the beep and message to power is constant. Not the best warning.Then, when you place them in the charging case they continue to be connected!!! I have to turn off bluetooth to get it to release. That wouldn't be terrible if sometimes I wanted to charge one headphone while listening to the other. In that situation the headphone that is supposed to be charging seems to still be playing music while in the charging case! This eats up the battery of the charging case quite a bit.And maybe that is why the charge time seems so bad. It is really hit and miss. Sometimes it seems like the headphones keep a charge for hours, other times they will last 30 minutes even though I am confident I had charged both the headphones and the charging case. It has been extremely frustrating since multiple times I have disconnect the charging case from the charger and gone out to do yard work, or a bike ride, and after 30 minutes I get the warning that I'm low on power. It makes no sense since other days it works for hours. Sometimes putting the ear buds in the charging case will charge them other times the case seems to not have a charge.Another big issue is that they are not that comfortable and they fall out far too easily. I'm not a huge fan of ear buds, so this might really be more personal to me, but it is so bad that often while gardening I switch to Apple EarPods which feel better and they don't fall out- unless the cord gets pulled. If I don't want the cord, or if I want better sound, I switch to using my expensive Sony headphones for gardening work- not the best environment (but a much MUCH better sound).The last issue was the use of one ear bud. I often want to do this when shopping, and/or listening to podcasts. It allows me to continue listening to the environment around me, and in theory should give me double the battery life. If you only want to use one ear bud it gives you an annoying beep for ~ 2 minutes while I assume it is searching for the other unit. Often it has paired with the other unit that is sitting in the charger- why?!?It would be silly to compare a $29 pair of headphones with a $200+ pair, but if you are looking to save money you should know the realities.It's been ~18 months since I've had these. I still use them, but I'm not overly happy with them, but I think I got my money's worth out of them. I would have given more stars had the frustrations not been so annoying. You get what you pay for.

title: Won't Stay in Ears
body: I bought these for the purpose of using them while doing workout/yoga vidoes on my tablet. Pairing them was a little challenging, but once I got the hang of it, I could switch between using them on my tablet, laptop, and phone easily enough. Sound quality seemed okay but not terrific. There would be a lot of bass for some songs that I wouldn't consider to have heavy bass normally. The connection would lag every once in a while, and sometimes the audio between the left and right ear would be out of sync. Oddly enough, none of these were deal breakers.The thing that was the deal breaker is that they wouldn't stay in my ears. I used them to watch movies on my laptop, and they stayed in, though I did have to occasionally push them back in as they would eventually loosen. This wasn't horribly inconvenient. It was horribly inconvenient though when they would fall out of my ears during workouts. I'm not talking about high interval training, jumping around workouts. I'm talking about little movement, staying in place, planking types of workouts. It's super frustrating to be holding a side plank and have an ear bud fall out. They wouldn't stay in for yoga either. I can't imagine going for a run with them.I didn't buy them for the waterproof feature, but since the manual said they could be used while showering, I gave that a try as well. They really didn't stay in during the shower. They may be able to still work (i.e. play music) when exposed to water, but they will slide right out of your ears if there's any moisture.Also, they didn't have any noise cancelling features that I noticed. Since they fall out of the ear easily, they didn't create a tight enough seal to block out noise. I could still hear the downstairs kid trying to play instruments while I was trying to find my yoga zen. Pretty disappointing.I don't recommend these and will be looking for something better myself.After posting this review, the company did reach out to me directly to try to find a solution to my problem. They were very responsive and accommodating and even sent me a new pair of earbuds thinking maybe I had received a defective pair. I very much appreciated their high level of customer service and willingness to work with me. Unfortunately, the second pair (a TOZO T6 IPX8 set) had the same problem of not staying in my ears.I can't recommend this product since it didn't work for me, but I can recommend the company. Hopefully they are able to fix their issues in later versions.

title: Don't use at busy gyms!!!
body: When I first looked at the reviews and how many of them I thought, this looks like a great cheaper alternative to Apple airpods especially since I have a Samsung device. When they came in the mail they were very sharp looking. The case looked good and came in a nice sealed plastic covering. I really liked the wireless charging feature since I have a charging pad for my S9. I charged them for the time suggested (around 4 hours) and proceeded to use them in the house. They were a little bulkier than I anticipated for being in your ear but they stayed in my ear pretty well. Don't try to compare them to Airpods as they aren't as sleek and the case isn't either.Pairing was easy and worked well. You pair the R earbud to the phone and the L earbud pairs to the R earbud. Walking around the house with my phone in my pocket the sound in my L earbud fluctuated a bit but I didn't think anything of it. I then went to the gym during the week at a busy time. I had a pair of LG headphones ($100) that fell apart after about 1 year and I didn't want to wait for warranty.When I got to the gym, the L earbud started to cut out constantly the entire time I was there. It was very frustrating. This happened to my LG headphones so I thought maybe its just my phone or my mobile data. To figure out if that was true or not, I switched to the gym wifi and still same issues. I held the phone close to the earbuds and it got a little better but still cut out constantly. Very much a distraction. I then thought maybe it was my phone even though its a Samsung Galaxy S9. I used my wife's Samsung S9 and also my dad's Samsung S10 and they all did the same thing. Cut in and out during every song and the R earbud would sometimes fluctuate or cut out because the L one was doing it.I unpaired and repaired the earbuds several times while at the gym to no avail. I know this can't be a problem with other headphones because I don't see other people fooling around with their headphones the entire time at the gym, just me. I read more reviews on these and found the same issue with other people at a busy gym. I'm not switching gyms just to be able to use these earbuds so I proceeded to return them.I purchased the Mpow 059 bluetooth headphones which are cheaper and according to them older bluetooth technology than these Tozo earbuds. I didn't want to switch to over the ear headphones but they work so much better than these Tozo earbuds. They had a lot of potential but so disappointing that they didn't work.

title: So Far seems fine.
body: UPDATE 10/09/2020 change Rating to 2 starsOkay after having been using these for a week and a half and feel the need to update my review.Pros--good sound quality.-Battery life seems to be okay, have not tested it for to long but have not had the die on me.Cons- Won't stay in my ears, will put them in and before to long I can feel them slipping out or they will just fall out, even when not moving around they just won't stay in no matter what I try and I have tried all the tips to keep them in.- very bad/soft mic. Every time I am using them for a phone call I am told the other person can't really hear me, often times having to turn them off/disconnect them and just use the phone itself.- Charging case seems to drain quickly, I can be using one ear bud for just a short time less than 5 minuets, put it on the charger and before I know it the charging case is down to less than half it's stored power only charging one after having only used it for a short time.-Sound will randomly cut out for a second or two on one ear bud or the other as if it was loosing connection or something, but the phone will be just in my pocket and it won't disconnect all the way just sound cut out for a second in one or the other.So why the sound quality for them is great for listening to music, and the battery in the ear buds them self's don't seem to be bad. With the bad quality mic making it hard/impossible to use for phone calls and falling out even when I am just standing/sitting not moving around I can not say this product is wroth the money I paid for it.And kind of dirty the only way to activate the warranty that is supposed to come with it you have to leave a review before you had a chance to try them out and get a chance to be able to get a feel for them and leave a honest and informative review. I am guessing there betting on people figuring it's not worth the time and effort to go back and update/change there review and that is why they have so many 5 star ones.______________________________________________________Original reviewJust got the earbuds in the mail today, so can't say to much about them. The sound is nice and loud so I like that, had no problems connecting them to my phone (S8+), shipping was fast and came with no visible problems. Can use one or both at a time. Will update after had them a while to say how they are doing after some extended use.

title: So many new problems...
body: I bought these to "upgrade" to the Boltunes bluetooth earbuds (that were connected by a cord with a remote along the cord) - I didn't realize what a hassle they'd be, some things to consider:1. Lots of interference - I don't know what it is about these pods, but they constantly have interference when I'm walking around (but the same downloaded songs or podcast episodes are perfectly fine when I'm indoors), they pause, fade in & out, get choppy...I do live & work in NYC & find that particularly high traffic areas cause interference (when I go visit my family in the suburbs...no problem!)...I have no idea why that is, but I've never had this issue with my Boltunes or any other pair of bluetooth earbuds.2. Easy to lose - I had gotten use to wearing my Boltunes as a necklace (end ends were magnetic so it connected easily) whenever I was taking a break from listening while out & about - admittedly, I REALLY miss this option because I've fumbled around many times in the past few months searching for one earbud or the other....I'm always paranoid about dropping or losing them if I need to take them out & find myself with pockets or a bag. I realize I could wear the charger as a necklace...but with the size of that thing...no thanks!3. The touch controls are more of a hassle than convenience - I hate that everytime I put in or remove the the pods or accidentally touch them, it's inevitable play/pause/skip/or adjusting the volume. Also, while it turns on & pairs pretty seamlessly, turning it off is a different story - often, I have to put hem in the charger to force it to turn off/disconnect it from my phone, sometimes they'll even continue to play while they're in the charger so I have to go into my phone's bluetooth setting to disconnect4. The charger is tricky - the "battery life" indicator in the charger is tricky...it never seems to get below 3 out of 4 blue lights - it will suddenly die out & I only find out the next day when I put in the pods to hear "low battery"All that said...the pros, when there's no interference, the sound is great & noise cancellation is quite effective, there's no tangling with the straps of a mask (a minor problem I had w/ the Boltunes), & these stay in the best of any earphones/pods I've ever had. While I'm sure there's work arounds or a matter of getting used to for some of my issues - the interference is hard to avoid & reason enough not to recommend or repurchase.

title: BLUETOOTH CONNECTING FUNCTIONALITY is BUGGY
body: tl;dr - if you care about ease and proper functioning of bluetooth connectivity, these buds are NOT for you. at best, they are a decent gym/run earbuds that I would not listen to high-quality music on.CONNECTING TO THE PHONE:I see reviews applauding how the Tozo automatically connects to your iPhone when you remove one or both from the case.I want to speak about the other side of the coin: the Tozo will connect to my phone even when I do not remove it from the case - the one time when it absolutely should not. If my Tozos are "connected" as a bluetooth device to my phone (iPhone X), I take them off, and I put them in my case, one would expect them to disconnect while building charge. However, in several instances, I have found that they somehow automatically end up connecting to my phone ANYWAY. The only way around this is to manually disconnect the phone from the L and the R headphone separately.This ALSO happens if I make sure to turn the headphones off before I use them. Normally, if you turn one side off, both will turn off. I have tried putting them back in the case after turning them off in that manner, and yet they reconnect. I have tried turning both off and putting them back - same story. This to me is a bug alone to not purchase this product.CONNECTING TO EACH OTHER:99% of the times that I have used the headphones, be it on runs, at the gym, or working while stationary, the bluetooth connection between the headphones will falter. This is noticed by the absence of audio in one side and sometimes it will toggle between each side - an experience which is incredibly noticeable and slightly disruptive.THINGS I LIKE:The sleekness of the design is extremely nice. The hardware design of the case and the buds seems like there was a lot of thought put into it:- The way the red lights turn on (one for each side) when they are charging properly is useful.- The light-up four-blue dots that indicate battery progress is nice.- The semi-translucent material of the case lid that allows you to see these lights even with the case closed- The weight of the case is perfect (for me); they might be a bit weighty and less sleek for some (esp if you're used to the compactness of AirPods).- The magnetic clasp (three magnets) of the hinged lid to the case is also really nice- Colorways are great too (I have the slightly muted pigeon gray ones)

title: Great sound, but some weird issues with turning on, recharing, and connecting
body: PROS: These earbuds (air buds?) have surprisingly great bass and sound quality overall, and does a good job at (passively) blocking out external sounds. When it's in my ears snugly (using the extra provided silicone adapters), I don't feel like it's going to fall out. I use it for running and they actually stay in my ears better than my previous bluetooth earbuds that wrap around the auricle. The case able to be charged wirelessly is also a nice touch, which is especially nice considering the flap that covers the microUSB charging port is ridiculously difficult to open.NEUTRAL: Personally, I am not a big fan of the buttons, they require too much pressure to actuate. When I want to pause a song, I have to press so hard on them that it feels like I'm jamming it further into my ear canal. Either that or pinch it with two fingers but that moves the ear buds around and I'd have to readjust them, and so on. I would recommend going for touch-sensitive ones.CONS: There are some weird issues with this device that ruin the fun. Not sure if I got a bad device, but I am reviewing what I got. Often times, the battery pack/case will simply be out of battery when unused for 2 days. I kept these in my office over the weekend, fully charged, and when I came back to use them again, one of the earbuds was dead and the pack only had one light (out of four) lit up.Also, usually when you remove an earbud from the case and put it in your ear, you hear a voice say "power on... connected!" Well sometimes, that doesn't happen with the right earbud (the left has worked just fine every time). No matter how many times I would put it back in the case and take it out again, the right earbud just wouldn't connect or turn on. When this happens, I have to hold the power button until it says "power off," and then hold it again until it powers on again. THEN it usually connects just fine, but after a couple of days I would have the exact same issue again.And as far as the buttons go, most other headsets have an option to change the song and volume, along with play/pause. This one, you can only play/pause. a double-press of the button isn't skip song like other ones do, but redial.CONCLUSIONS: All in all it's a decent package for a decent enough price, but the issues I've been having with the charging an connections would make me hesitant to recommend it to other people.

title: Right side doesn't charge, case doesn't hold a charge
body: Edit: I wish I'd returned these. I tried to make these more comfortable with tiny tips and ear hooks and use these but the case has never held a charge. To add insult to injury the right side won't even charge. No indicator light goes on but something is registering because the 4 "charging" lights in the case turn on, then 3, and 2 and … dead batteries all around. I wouldn't expect that if I'd been wearing them as much as I expected to but to have these problems, even with attention to caring for the batteries is really frustrating.I'm sure most of these won't have these problems; I just got "lucky". 👿 I've never even left a one ⭐️ review before so yeah, I'm pretty unhappy with them.Original ⬇️⬇️They FLY! out of my ears and hurt! I'm disappointed in the battery life of both the buds and the charging case and I just don't know what to do about that. I didn't expect them to recharge every time I had to put them down (in the case) or risk losing them (not in the case). I've had them- well, I've used them for 3 days and recharged the case twice. After the second full recharging - oh, that's not right- I got a day out of a charged case the first time, a day and half the second time (?!)- I decided they were flawed. I don't think the model of "true wireless" is flawed; I bought these so I wouldn't have to worry about my batteries when I was out. (Now I have to worry about batteries and earbuds flying out of my ears in the dark!)This brand has a bizarre thing in a style I find comfortable but it has none of the advantages of true wireless- it's the one with the wide bar and ear hooks (~$20) but no charging case; the main reason I want true wireless.Obviously fit is another thing. I have very small ear canals so I stock up on extra XS earbud tips but those only help a little.. I have to carefully place and mess with them all the time. The right side stayed in, but hurts after just a little while.If you have hard to fit ears, I'd suggest finding true wireless in whatever style is absolutely the most comfortable style for you and then increasing the comfort factor even more. Don't compromise. It's awfully annoying to return things, even if it were just the comfort thing.They sound pretty good when I can stop worrying about them and nobody has had any issues with phone calls even in areas that are often iffy.

title: Stopped Working After Getting Wet
body: This is my second pair. I originally had the Tozo 6. At that time I had tried multiple pairs of “waterproof” headphones (including 3 types of jaybirds) and either the sound quality would be subpar or the headphones would go bad because they weren’t truly waterproof. The Tozo 6 stood alone at the top! There’s a reason it has so many good reviews. It’s a great headphone and out preforms other headphones that are 3 times the price. I did upgrade to this model, the Tozo 10, because my charging station was only working on one side, but that was after over a year of constant daily use. I am a person who sweats a lot during a workout and these have lasted 1-3 hour daily workouts and rinsing them off in the shower afterwards. I have put them through the washer accidentally numerous times as well as fully submerged them in water with no loss of quality or performance. They are seriously an amazing head phones.This newer version feels even better than the previous version. They feel more heavy duty, the charging station also feels more durable (although a little larger). The new version has a nice coating on it that provides better grip of the headphone. The older version was very slick and almost slippery when wet. For the money I honestly don’t think you can find better. The only cons of this headphone are that they sometimes pause/play or move volume up or down if they get too wet on the area where you tap with your finger, and for some people they may be a little large. I have smaller ears and they’ve never bothered me or had issues of falling out, but they are bigger than some headphones out there. For me the sound quality and overall durability outweigh those drawbacks, especially when you consider the price.EDIT: Everything above stands true, but the right side (new Tozo 10 model) stopped working for me after getting them wet. I never had this problem with the 6's and maybe its a fluke, but I ended up returning them and I will likely try another pair or the older Tozo 6 again. I guess they aren't invincible and although listed as waterproof, still worth avoiding getting wet. It's a disappointment since the earlier version did so well for me, but for the price and sound quality it's still worth a second chance.Hope that helps you with your next headphone purchase.

title: Terrible Battery Life
body: I've had these for about 8 months now and well I did like them at first they're a couple flaws that just drive me absolutely crazy.The connectivity is fairly good although every now and again it will just drop the signal and then reconnect a couple seconds later even if my phone is in my pocket. Occasionally they will also start to cut out as if I'm starting to get out of range even when I'm not. There's no rhyme or reason to this but it does it every couple of days.When I first turn them on the second earbud that gets powered up regardless if it's the right or the left, the voice that says power on, is in super slow motion. It doesn't affect the functionality but it is pretty annoying.The biggest flaw with these is the battery life. Compared to my last set that for much older these are terrible. The worst part about it is once you hear "battery low," she'll repeat herself every 10 seconds about 3-4 times and then they just shut off. They might as well just shut off. The charging case also has a pretty dismal battery and will only charge these things two and a half times. I'm not sure how many times they are supposed to charge them but my last set, the case would charge them five times.Another big issue of mine is the sensitivity of the "touch" button on the side. If I am working bending over doing anything my shirt can just brush these things and pause whatever I'm listening to. Another issue I have with the button is when they are on and connected, you have to turn off Bluetooth on the device you are using to switch devices. There are no way to shut the earbuds off. So if I forget to turn off my Bluetooth the earbuds will stay connected and die.As far as the hands free is concerned this is where I have another issue with these. It seems that every time I try and use these to talk on the phone whoever I am talking to cannot understand me. This is a major problem because I get a lot of calls for work during the day and again I can't just shut them off I have to go into my phone and turn off Bluetooth to get them to stop.I did give them two stars because the sound quality when they do work is decent. Not spectacular but for the price I didn't really expect spectacular.

title: The sound cuts out way too much
body: I wanted to like these more than I do. I really really did. First to be diplomatic, I'll give what I like first.1. They look great and they are very compact. They are wireless, so no more cord knots!2. I like the charging case, so you can take them with you on the go, and you can know they will be ready for use.3. Easy to pair and use.Minor issue with service: I ordered black and I was sent an opened box with white headphones. Looks I was sent a previous person's return. Not a big deal, Amazon made things right and sent me a new replacement with the correct color. Mistakes happen so I let this one go, meaning, it really didn't account for my star rating.So why did I give this two stars?The sound quality when they stay connected to my bluetooth device is pretty decent. That said, I cannot walk 10 seconds or turn my head to the left while my phone is in either pants pocket, without the connection dropping and the sound cutting out. It really is such a reoccurring situation that the headphones eventually get put in a drawer and I turn to my trusted wired headset. I have another pair of bluetooth headphones, SENSO Bluetooth Headphones, which are connected by a wire to both ear buds, and while the sound has cut out once or twice, they seem much more reliable. The Senso headphones, I can at least jog, walk or mow the yard without the sound cutting out every few seconds. I also wish they had a skip function or volume function on the headphones when listening to music. My cheaper Senso headphones have this.At first I thought it might be my device that was causing the headphones to cut out. But then again, my other BT headphones do not have this bad of a problem. I also have BT headsets for work and they do not cut out when walking 15 feet away from my phone, so I don't think it is my phone. I simply think the range on these earbuds is not that great. I suspect that it would be better if I used an armband case for my phone, but I do not want to do that. If I cannot have my phone in my pants pocket and turn my head to the left without the sound cutting out, then I need to find a product that can meet this minimal requriement.

title: Great headphones at first
body: I purchased these in August 2019 to use while running. I run 8-10 miles three times per week. My comments are from my overall experience:(1) excellent sound quality when working appropriately.(2) After a few months, my left earbud is not as loud as the right so I have a very off balanced sound.(3) After a few months, occasionally my left earbud would not produce any sound.(4) Sometimes there are sounds inside the earbuds that seem to match the rhythm of my gait.(5) Music will sometimes cut out for random reasons. Over the months I realize it cuts out at same locations on my running route - I think it is picking up bluetooth or wifi from a private home or car and that for some reason takes precedence over my iphone.(6) After a few months, the earbuds struggle to pair and then struggle to connect.(7) After a few months, one set (I have owned several, see below) would pair and then connect to my phone and then immediately disconnect and then power off. It would do all of this in immediate sequence and no combination of pressing buttons or resets would work.(8) After a few months, my current set automatically turns on when I plug them into the charging base. And therefore the charging lights do not come on. I have reset the item several times and it does this every time. This is why I am here now doing this review and shopping for a new pair.(9) Be careful with the 5 star reviews. I bought mine in August 2019 but as of Jan 5 2020 I am working through my 5th set. The company emails those that give poor reviews and offers to send a new set free of charge if they are willing to remove or change their review. I have now received 4 new pairs from them after giving 3 star reviews. I will not remove this one. I'm ready to move on.Conclusion: if you are looking for a nice sounding pair of earbuds that will give you about 3-6 months of use then these are probably going to work out well for you. If you are looking for something that will work flawlessly and is durable enough to hold up then you should not consider these. I paid $35 for my set in August 2019 and have run through 5 pairs in 17 months. I'm looking for something better and more longlasting.

title: Meets Basic Needs
body: After using it extensively for over a month I can say that this unit is a great starter set for anyone trying out their first BT In-ear set. Works right out of the box, no Issues pairing with a range a devices. Given that similar sets are much more expensive and equally easy to loose, one should moderate expectations.I am still happy with my purchase (and hence the 4 stars despite key features being so so). I am reluctant to spend 2-5 times as much given how easy the in-ear BT devices are to loose. At my Gym I have already returned 3 devices I found in the parking lot (all 3 apple) back to the front desk. That made buying this device a no-brainier (has all the key functions of acceptable quality and not a hefty price tag in case you end up loosing them.Sound quality and noise cancellation are acceptable and though not music aficionado great. The phone calls were clear sounding and the other end could hear clearly. I tried it for late night movie watching and listening to podcasts while running satisfactorily. The big con is limited battery life in continuous use sometime just a couple of hours on full charge. They are also OK fit for my ears and I have had to look for them under furniture or retraced my steps back to a room to find the missing one (all too easy for this genre of devices). Luckily in each case I was able to find the missing piece.Update - I guess the ear buds were waiting for me to finish writing the review. Something changes quickly and my right ear buds became almost inaudible. No amount of playing with it (hard resets and trying different devices) including reaching out to the manuf. helped. So I have a choice of listening very loud left and nothing from right or using it as a mono earpiece. I also find not being able to control the volume from the ear piece a major usability issue. Many devices do not have obvious or easy Bluetooth volume adjustment and not having controls on the earbuds is an irritant anyway. I would no longer recommend this pair. I think ponying for name brand device like Apple, Samsung, JBL is hindsight is better if all you are going to get is a few weeks of function.

title: **Update** Stopped working after a couple of weeks.. was: Surprisingly Great earbud find
body: **Update**I really like the product and the concept. I got real bad news.. it stopped working for me :(. Amazon and Tozo have great customer service which is why I cannot give it one star because they are really trying to take care of me. However, I returned my first earbuds when the left earbud stopped responding. I sweat alot, and while I was working out lots of sweat came into my ears and the the left bud stopped responding. I contacted support and researched how I can fix this. They were helpful in trying to reset it, but no response from the earbud. I tried to recharge it in the case, and still no response. Tozo and Amazon then sent me a replacement and I sent back the non-responding pair of earbuds. Fast forward 2.5 weeks later and my left earbud of the replacement died again. Same situation, same attempt to resurrect. I really want to love this product with its nifty wireless recharging feature, decent sound, and comfort for my ears, but the earbud is not working. I am torn if I should get a 3rd replacement or just give up with my head down. I hope I can update this review with putting more stars back up and say how great the product is and the third time is a true charm.Original PostOn a whim, I purchased these earbuds so I can go in long walks and listen to my music or news. I didn't expect much for the relatively low price I paid.That said, it was one of the easiest things I ever paired with my very complicated bluetooth on my Samsung s9. It took less than 2 mins. Following this initial setup, connecting to my phone has been and continues to be seamless to the extreme...like it was made by Samsung.When I first listened to a wide variety of music, the sound was a little metallic which is why it's 4 stars. But when I was listening while taking a shower, WOW!!! Suddenly the bass was deep and crisp. The higher frequencies lost their metallic sound and is almost like real high quality speakers. This is when I realized I need to spend time teying the different fittings to achieve that perfect sound that these earbuds are capable of. Do I higjly recommend? Absolutely.!

title: Is it worth it?
body: When I first got it, I thought the product was wonderful because I wanted to be optimistic about it since it was the first time I ever spent so much money on headphones. However, after a few days I noticed quite a few things about these earbuds.They are not made for small ears (even if you change the silicone buds to the smallest size they have): It was very uncomfortable to wear and it did not fit comfortably in my ears. I think it's b/c my ears are so small (even though I'm sure it's normal size), but still it's good to be warned. I could not keep them in long, it was uncomfortable to sleep with and it kept heating up in my ears.Battery life is not that long: I had to keep charging the earbud every hour even though the description said it would last 3 hours. The box that charges it lasts a while though, so that's a good thing and it doesn't take too long to charge for both.Has limited capabilities: You can only press the button for play/pause and answer/end call. I don't think it says anywhere in the description about what it does exactly but I had the impression it could skip a song, turn up/down the volume, and have a great microphone (people can barely hear me when I call them).The connection is not great: If you turn a certain way one of them cuts off continuously. It's usually the one with just a blue blinking dot. It does a little better at long distance but for some reason it can cut itself off or sound like static even when your not far from the connected devive.Good things: The sound quality is great. It is noise canceling but I bought earphones that could do the same thing that were cheaper ($11). It has portable charging station that you don't have to charge too often. I'm not sure if it's waterproof since I've never tried it out.Overall, I don't think it's worth the price you pay for it if it doesn't do it's most important function which is play sound (as I've said before it cuts on and off) so I am sending it back right away. If you can handle all of the things wrong with it then try it out for few days to see how it is.

title: Unexpectedly Bada$$ Headphones - UPDATE - 5-Stars to 2-Stars unfortunately
body: I was hoping these would be at least as decent as my JBL or Apple headphones - but, wow was I underestimating these. Never heard of this brand (TOZO), and I'm always skeptical of overly positive reviews, but I literally can't find one thing wrong with these. While you can't control anything physically on the ear pieces, they do have built in mics for bluetooth, handsfree talking, and you can control the volume and sound FX when connected to your phone or device. What's most impressive is the bass these little things produce. I feel like I have a subwoofer in my head. They fit super snug, and cancel ambient sound better than any headphones I've owned. I truly hope these hold up. A+++++++UPDATE REVIEW 7/31/19:While these do sound pretty great, they do manage to have a few irritating qualities. You can't control the volume on them, but that doesn't especially bother me because you control it on your connected device. What bothers me is there is no on/off button, so these things are on and auto-connected the moment they come out of their charging case.The headphones have to connect to each other. You'll hear a voice say "second device connected" - at first I thought that meant I had connected to someone else's device, but no... connection 1 is the left headphone, connection 2 is the device. When the headphones aren't paired to a device, they have a little chirp that goes off about every 20 seconds, which is extremely annoying. What's worse, is now mine won't stop chirping at all, whether connected or not.Now to the absolutely biggest design flaw in these - the microphone. I bought these for hands-free convenience. But if you are outside in the slightest bit of wind, or in a Starbucks or anywhere not in a closed, quiet space, no one can what you're saying. The microphones are on the outside facing part of the headphones, so they aren't even directed towards your mouth. This is a huge issue and I am returning these as this particular flaw completely defeats the reason I bought them in the first place.

title: Uncomfortable, and constantly fall out
body: Update: After 8 months of ownership, the case died. It won't accept a charge, nor will it charge either set of earbuds (the ones that came with it, or my old generic ones from a different case). The last 4 or 5 months the earbuds were finicky about charging when placed in the case, and would fall out of their spots and not charge while riding in my pocket. I think the magnets were becoming demagnetized. Either way, this was a bad purchase, and I'm glad to move on.These are among the most uncomfortable things I've put in my ears. I've tried all the tips, and none of them work for me. I don't typically have this problem. I was looking at earbuds with wings, but decided to take a chance on these because my old set did okay without the wings.These are constantly falling out, and I constantly have to push them back in. Yes, I put the tip in my ear hole and then twist the body into place. They just pop out during normal activities like cleaning the house, mowing the lawn, walking, or riding my bike.The controls are touch controls, which I personally hate, but you almost can't find things with simple, reliable buttons. They do okay with sweat, but I accidentally touch the sensor and something happens every time I'm putting them back in my ears, which is a lot. The noise isolation is practically nonexistent for me, but that's to be expected with a poor fit. I like the multi-tap functions to skip forward and backward and change volume. There is a right and a left bud, and if they don't go in the correct ear your tapping will do unexpected things. The right and the left can also be accidentally swapped in the case.I have discovered that the case will charge my old buds, so I have mostly abandoned these earbuds in favor of my old ones with their new case. The sound and battery life (~2.5hr) on these are just okay. They stick out of my ears a good bit, so they stand out more than other earbuds. Overall, I'm not impressed, not even for the low price. I really wish I had bought something else.

title: Phenomenal (but only previous version)
body: Updated model, updated review:To preface: I loved my old version of these. I had them for 2 years before their battery started to go, at which point I ordered the new ones.The new earbuds: the new design is a lot like the older, but the case isn't backwards compatible. I thought that I would have 4 earbuds that would work with one pack, when I reordered this. The new earbuds are smaller and only sort of fit in the old case and the old ones don't fit at all in the new case.This is a massive annoyance because I prefer the old earbuds. They have a button (rather than touch) and they can redial and play within control. The new ones are touch, so that everytime I adjust them in my ears, they pause or play. The redial feature is unreliable and they tried to make them fancier with volume control, but this just means it's tougher to power them off. I frequently have one earbud in at a time, and this also means I dont care which is right or left, but the programming I'm the earbuds matters for volume.The new case: Here's the only improvement, USB C. That's all. Otherwise, they removed the port cover that keeps the charger dry and safe (see old review.) As previously said, it isn't even backwards compatible due to sizing.FINAL OPINION: The first model was more than good enough to guarantee that I bought these again.... this new model is over designed and found wanting. They used to do something simple extraordinarily well, but now I would need to be assured that they backpedaled to buy them again.I am still using my older model earbuds exclusively, and just keep the case plugged into a battery pack.My Original, Glowing Review: These headphones are phenomenal. I dropped them in the toilet by accident and they worked the same after getting rinsed off. I even dropped the entire case in the snow and couldn't find them for a week. When I found them, they were open in the snow. I let them dry out, and it's like nothing happened at all.

title: For $50, Buy Something Better!
body: I’ve had these earbuds for about three weeks now, and I must say, for what I paid for them, they are terrible! The “sound quality” that’s so highly bragged about, just isn’t there for me. I’ve tried every combination of earbud covers they come with, and they still sound like $5 dollar store earbuds, with a very tinny, weak tone. I’ve changed my equalizer on my iPhone a dozen, or more times and still can’t fix it. Just a small nitpick on them, and I would have said this even if I had liked them, they look absolutely stupid in your ears! Not low profile, whatsoever. If you lie down with them in your ears, you will have trouble laying on your side, as they protrude around half an inch out of your ears. Now for the real reason for the negative review; THE BATTERY LIFE! The battery life on these earbuds is god-awful! They claim 4 hours on a full charge (if I remember correctly, I didn’t check the listing before writing this) and when you unbox them, the features list on the box claims “a 3 hour battery life”. (Again, I did not check before writing this, however actual product claims hour less than listing here) Instant lie right there, folks. I have seen a MAXIMUM 2 hour life with mine on a full charge and that’s on volume click 4... Not impressive at all. And the “fast charging” feature, is not that quick. 45 minutes to get a full charge. For example, my old $12 Walmart earphones had a max life of 6 hours on a 90 minute charge! What. The. Heck. Problem is, they are very fragile pieces of junk, that crap out from on drop of water. That’s why these aren’t only one star. The water-resistance is really good, so far, and the 4 charge case is really nice, as well.Bottom line; is if you asked me how much I’d say these are worth, let me use them for two days,then said name the price, I’d tell you $20. Fifty dollars for these is absolutely ridiculous. They are nice, in a couple of ways, but definitely not worth fifty bucks. There’s plenty of WAY better options on here, for that price.

title: this is an UPDATE to my previous comment
body: I am leaving my prevoius comments but today's date is August 19,2020. Well Since I purchased these earbuds the right one has stopped working and actually instead of sounding like a lady it now sound like a deep voice woman. It will not connect to a phone and if it does it is only for a few minutes and then it disconnect. I would contact them to replace them but they never replaced the one that was broken that my wife has. Their gimmick was for me to give them a posistive review and they would replace her earbud and that was in maybe April or May and we still haven't received that earbud replacement. Like I said in my prevous comments they worked well but after about 4 or 5 minths of consistent use they gave out.When I was looking for a total wireless headset I needed something that would control two phones at the same time. This feature was key to me because I work in a hospital setting and I have to carry 2 phones. So having a regular Bluetooth that could only answer one device was not for me. I tried a few and wasted money on some that said that they were a multipoint Bluetooth but really did not do the job I needed. Some only came with one earbud and was not as relievable for my application but they had good reviews so I tried them and they didn't live up to my standards. The TOZO T10 Bluetooth 5.0 Wireless Earbuds worked great for my application. I can answer either phone, I can connect them to my MS Surface when I working to listen to music or watching TV on my phone when I go off on trips. I haven't tried it yet but I think I can even pair the left one to one phone and the right one to another phone. What I do know is that I can pair both phones to the same pair of earbuds and they work with out problem. They worked so well I even bought my wife a pair. She don't use them for the same purpose that I do. All she do is listen to her music in stereo or her school lectures and that is it but I put a pair of earbuds to the test.

title: Weird issues when used with PC/Laptops
body: PC issues:Weird audio issues - *managed to fix*mic issues - no fix found yetI'm a weirdo and wanted this for computer use, not phone/music use. I cannot give any accurate feedback regarding how it works with phones.On the other hand, with my PC and laptop, this has it's issues.When you pair it with a PC there are 3 things that appear in your sound settings.(2 under playback)TOZO-T10 Hands-Free AG AudioTOZO-T10 Stereo(1 under recording)TOZO-T10 Hands-Free AG AudioIn order to get the audio playback to work correctly I disabled ALL other playback devices and left ONLY TOZO-T10 Sterio enabled. Before I did this it had very strange issues. There were times that the audio seemed to be at it's own levels regardless of what I set the computer to. (I could turn everything on volume mixer to 0% and still hear everything at 100%). However, seemingly randomly it would swap back to regular audio that was properly controlled by volume settings. I may have done a blanket fix, when in reality I might have only needed to disable the Hands-Free AG Audio portion. But it's working now and I know what I did, so I'm fine with it.Second issue:The mic is so quiet that people can barely hear me in discord. I was really looking forward to being able to use this mic and after reading peoples reviews about it it sounded good. There is no mic boost or anything on any of the systems I have tried this on. I have played with all the settings I can think of and nothing helped.I use these because I regularly use earbuds paired with my laptop, but can only have 1 earbud in at a time. I was annoyed at the wires so I decided to go wireless. With the playback sound issues fixed I'm still fairly happy with these, I just can't rely on the mic. But it still doesn't deserve more than 2 stars for giving me the issues I had, and a mic that won't work with a computer.

title: Not worth the cheap cost
body: The quality of these headphones is something that is pretty awesome, in that fifty dollars can get you something that will in theory, do the job. But my concern with these headphones was three decent sized problems.The headphones themselves are not very comfortable. I have average ears. I have not come across many sets of earbuds or headphones that feel awkward or difficult to wear. These earbuds do not feel good though. They also have a huge problem of sliding out, no matter what position I place them. I attempted the different size inserts, and they also had similar effects. This has not been a problem on other brands. I have since switched to the JBL Free X and they fit without any issues and have thus far been extremely comfortable.The next issue is the quality. The bass seems to sound very distorted in these ear buds, while the left one specifically has a very tinny quality to the highs. It's jarring and forces me to want to turn the volume down, even when listening at low volumes. Overall, the quality of the audio is mixed, which is odd for something that you would think would be similar between both sides.And last, the drop out. These earbuds do not stay attached across any of my devices for a constant session. Every few minutes, they will have a few seconds of audio loss and then come back. This is not a case of getting notifications. It's interference or just bad quality. I've used these buds on apple and android products, and none of them can keep these things attached without audio loss.Overall, they aren't worth the money. I understand they are cheap and the technology is still being innovated. But overall, if they sound different from one ear to another, don't fit well and can't play without feeling like your audio is censoring itself, then they simply aren't worth the money invested in them. I would move on to a different product, probably something a bit more reputable.

title: Randomly connect, charging case does not hold charge
body: Update 3/12/20: The case and left earbud have technical issues. I keep them in the case, and it can be fully charged. Next thing I know when I need to use the charging case it’s down from 4 to 1 light indicator, even though I haven’t used the earbuds at all.The left earbud randomly connects to my iPhone constantly. While it is plugged into the charging case. Very annoying.When these work correctly they are great. But there are technical issues with this item that need to be corrected.Received on 12/27/19. So far these are great. Very easy to use. They pair seamlessly with my iPhone 6s. The battery lasts long enough that I don't feel like I'm shortchanged on battery life. I haven't used them till the battery has drained completely though. The sound quality is good. I sometimes use both, or one at a time. There is no issue with using one or the other.I used them on a conference call today. Some previous posters mentioned that the mic quality wasn't good. I had no issues on my call. I did ask specifically if people on the call had any issues hearing me. They did not. Full disclosure, I was in a house where it was quiet, so I can't say if these would work well in a public place with a lot of ambient noise.For the price, I am very happy with them. The case is good. It is good for 4 full charges of each earbud and there are very clear blue indicator lights in the case to let you know how much charge you have left. This item comes with multiple earbud sizes and a micro-usb cable for charging the case. It also has a lanyard that you can thread through a loop on the case if you want to wear it around your neck. So all amenities are included. Only con I would say is that the case is larger than I thought it would be. But it's not like a brick or anything.For $50 I don't think you can beat the quality and ease of use.

title: Earbuds that fail on just about every metric besides price.
body: I was looking for an inexpensive pair of Bluetooth earbuds to use while running and watching videos on my phone before bed. I did no research, defaulting to the tried-and-true method of finding the product in my price range with the most/best reviews. This has served well in the past, but alas, this time was a massive miss:Really, the only PRO I would bring to attention to is price, but that seems irrelevant now that I realize the "Bluetooth Earbud Market" is completely flooded with brands selling $50 models.So here is my exhaustive list of CONS:-Sound Quality is pretty bad- and that's saying something as someone who only listens to spoken word content (podcasts, audiobooks, movies/tv shows). I think this I more an issue of fit though, which leads to my next point.-These earbuds feel massive, stick way outside of the ear, awkward center of gravity so they feel like they're always falling out, and the only way to keep inside your ear canal, once you pick the right sized numb is raw suction. This is extremely uncomfortable, as you essentially have to create a vacuum to get a tight fit for decent sound and keep them in your ear. Very invasive and nothing like I've ever experienced with any other audio device.-Push controls (not touch). No volume control. I had no idea how much better the control features are on competitor earbuds. Do yourself a favor and look around, you'll see these are basement level.-They blink constantly when on. This is very annoying when I'm trying to watch a video in my bedroom at night while my wife sleeps. I continuously see a blue light flash up the room and reflected in my glasses. Completely unnecessary and annoying.Maybe I have bad ears, but given the exhaustive options on the market, I have no reason to keep these earbuds and not assume some other company has a better product.

title: Bluetooth connectivity issues between earbuds...
body: **Update 5/10/19**The exact same issue is happening with the replacement earbud set. Right ear stays connected to the phone and has no issues what so ever. The Left earbud drops signal every 1-2 seconds. No amount of resetting fixes the issue. Very disappointed.🙁RMA requested. They’re going back.******************************************************Update 5/8/19**I’ve just received the replacement set of the Tozo T10 IPX8 earbuds. They were new, in a sealed box. Pairing with my phone and the two buds went without a hitch. Initially upon first use of the replacement set the left earbud did disconnect from the right earbud a couple of times so I performed the factory reset and there have been no issues. I will put them through the paces over the next 24-48 hours and update again. Still have not heard from Tozo. 🤨 Thank You again Amazon Customer Service! You’re the BEST!!****************************************************I liked the look of these earbuds. The reviews sold me so I pulled the trigger on this purchase.I like the fit and sound. Really like the dedicated charging case that can be wirelessly recharged.Pairing with my iPhone 7 was easy and the pairing process between the buds was just as easy. They didn’t or haven’t fallen out of my ears. Used them for about a half a day and the Left bud started to lose connectivity with the right ear bud.After trying the reset recommended in the owners manual I still had the same issue so I contacted Tozo’s customer service via two separate e-mails. Waited 24 hours and heard nothing from them. So I escalated my issue to Amazon’s customer service team who came to the rescue. They’re sending me a replacement set and will edit my review after I receive and use the replacement buds.

title: After A Month, The Reasons I’ve Decided NO
body: I realize how difficult it can be to purchase new earbuds off without trying them out first. Here are the pros and cons and why I ultimately would not recommend these earbuds because I would not buy them again.CONSI like to think that my ear canals are typical. I have tried all of the included options for sizing in a multitude of combinations. I appreciate that there are so many choices however I absolutely can not get these earbuds to stay in my ear (and yes I have made sure I am putting them in correctly).With that said, running with them in and eating with them in is nearly impossible.After a month of use, the right earbud will show that it’s charging and will power on just to tell me “low battery, please charge”. It turns off right away and has yet to function again.These are obviously deal breakers.CONSWhen functioning, they did charge and stay charged for a good amount of time. The case holds a good charge and I would say that it is capable of fully charging the earbuds at least twice.As far as sound goes, for the price and considering they aren’t headphones, no complaints there. The tones are even and bass is decent.When I pull them out of the case they are able to automatically detect and connect to my iPhone 11 without an issue.Haven’t fully tested the waterproof capability but with such a good resistance rating I can confidently say that I wouldn’t be afraid to run with them in the rain or even in the shower.For the price, I think they are great. There are many other options however and the fact that they wouldn’t stay in my ears and that one ultimately just stopped holding a charge is why I don’t see it worthwhile to replace them and rather to just try something different next time.
