title: I was really hoping I’d be able to give them ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️...
body: So, I’ve had these Bluetooth “waterproof” headphones for less than 1 week. My initial opinion was a positive one...once I figured out how to fit them properly they worked great! The volume capacity, bass, noise cancellation and overall sound quality was awesome! I got 48 hours worth of enjoyment before I finally put them to the real test (the main reason I purchased them): wearing them in the shower. I’m one of those people who like to listen to music loudly while I shower. My roommate doesn’t mind the sound of my singing, but apparently the bass is more than a little distracting. So for the first 10 minutes of the shower, they worked just as well as they had in dry conditions. But when it came time to wash my face, I decided to take them out because I figured they’d probably fall out in the process. I set them up on a shelf in the shower, literally the furthest away from the shower head as you can get. After washing my face the earbuds wouldn’t stay put in my ears. I guessed it had something to do with my ears being dry when I’d first put them in and had them stay successfully. Upon stepping out of the shower the first thing I did was wrap them in paper towels cuz I figured they’d need to dry. I then put them back in their case to charge and didn’t attempt to use them again for several hours. That’s when I discovered that the left one had stopped working. Completely. It will blink it’s light while in the case, but as soon as I take it out it’s totally unresponsive. The right one still seems to work, but I didn’t spend $50 for 1 working waterproof earbud. And I haven’t showered with that one because I’m afraid it’ll die too. I purchased the recommended extended warranty at the same time, I’m hoping they’ll get me a pair that will work consistently. And I’ll come back and update/edit this review once it’s been addressed.

title: A year later, not as impressed
body: UPDATE: 8/26/2020 - While the headphones allow me to listen to music and take calls while I am walking in the morning, I have been dissappointed that I have never been able to use the wireless charging function. I have tried several different charging "plates" over the past year, including one from TOZO, but the case will not charge except with the usb cord. While I have tolerated this over the past year, I do feel the need to make sure others know of this discrepency. The initial review was based on the earbuds themselves. For a basic music listener, they seem fine, but I will admit over the past year that they do disconnect from my phone, but then reconnect. The battery life has been okay. I can get about a 3.5 hours out of a charge. They buds are a little bulky, but not horrible. I did use my own size earbud tips as the ones that came with it were all too large.These are incredible! I bought these headphones to replace a different brand of earbuds that stopped functioning. The earbuds arrived quickly and in a great carrying case. They pair with each other quickly and then quickly pair with my phone. The sound quality is incredibly clear with a rich sound. The base is very strong. The noise blocking feature is an added bonus, I hear very little environmental noise while outside. These will be great for my next flight. Customer service has been superb, checking to make sure I am satisfied with my purchase, unusual in today’s world. I would recommend these to anyone looking to buy a good quality affordable pair of earbuds.My only problem is that they are not wireless charging. I have tried them with diffferent Qi compatible chargers, and they will not charge. It will work with a regular usb cord, so thats fine. I just wish they were able to perform as marketed.

title: Do not recommend
body: I was really excited for these earbuds. I checked the reviews and they looked promising, so I caved and ordered them.Once my package arrived, I noticed it was substantially larger than what I'd expect for a small box of earbuds. I picked it up and my heart dropped as I heard my brand new earbuds just sliding around, slamming on the sides of the box. Once I opened it, there was absolutely no padding inside. Just this tiny box of earbuds. So right off the bat I wasn't too happy due to poor packaging. Luckily, the earbuds didn't look damaged. I charged them for a bit before I paired them to my phone, which was quick and easy.I wasn't a big fan on the sound quality. I wouldn't consider myself an audiophile, but I do like to enjoy my music with decent audio quality, and this just wasn't cutting it. The bass? It just felt off. I'm not sure what it was, but it sounds "flooded" if that makes any sense. These are also not comfortable in the slightest. I probably wore these for about 30 minutes total and I tried every size that it came with. They all just hurt. Bad. My ears were so sore and I felt a huge sense of relief when I finally took them out.The biggest issue I had overall was the mic quality. I made a phone call with these earbuds and I had to basically yell so they could hear me. After my call I recorded myself so I could hear what it sounded like, and I could barely hear myself even with the volume on max. I thought it was maybe my phone, but I tried it with another pair of bluetooth headphones that I've had before and it was just fine.I realized that this just wasn't worth keeping. I spent an extra $16 and ordered a different pair and couldn't be happier. They're just all-around better compared to these TOZO T10's. I'm going to be returning them soon.

title: Who's ears do these even fit?
body: I really wanted these to be the ones that worked since they didn't have a cord, but they were not at all good. I thought maybe changing the earbud tips they provide would make a difference but they don't. They just dangle in my ear, about to fall out and the sound sounds as though the earbud isn't in all the way. Then when I push the earbud in further, it is a larger part of the earbud that finally makes contact with my ear enough to hold it in. I just don't think that the part that holds the earbud in my ear is the part that is suppose to. So after having the earbuds in my ear where I think they should be so they don't fall out, I can't have them in longer than 30 to 45 minutes because they hurt my ear.Then the button on the earbud is not as easy to push as it should be so when you need to push it, you do so pretty hard which hurts your ear even more.The pairing and playback was actually pretty good. I wanted to use these between my phone and desktop at work and they worked how I thought they would. I get up to use the restroom and they unpair but when I come back they automatically pair with both devices. Switching from one device to the other is pretty decent also but listening to video messages in Marco Polo still had the skipping in the audio which really bothers me.So I returned these and found a pair of cheaper $20.95 bluetooth headphones with a wire and an around the ear type of earbud thinking that I'll just use them for working out but they are the best pair that I've found yet! They feel great in my ear, I don't notice the piece that wraps around me ear, they pair so easily with 2 devices at the same time, switching between the devices is seamless, the audio in the marco polo app is perfect with no skipping or pops. I would've paid $60 for a pair like these easily!

title: Don't bother.  There are so many similar ones, I recommend trying a different one than these.
body: These are not usable headphones, it will cut out on the left ear if you press your phone into your left hip. I suspect the Bluetooth reception may have trouble reaching the 'master' right side earphone through the human body. I also see it cutting when I've held the phone with my right hand (to stay on side of the master receiver), and turned my head left or right, similarly cutting out reception when my phone orbits my head. Not sure why, but imo definitely should happen and not even a possible limitation to Bluetooth itself. Bass also sucks, out of the box, the treble is predominant (tinny).On a live test run, I hiked about 90 minutes over 4.5 miles. Rather than focus on positives (all things which should "normally be expected), here are the additional negatives that convince me to return the product:-Preparing my morning, I had the earphone playing while having a quick breakfast, the left earphone fell out due to my jaws chewing. The next size larger was uncomfortably pressuring against my canal. They are heavier than other ones I just ordered to compare against.- While on my 4.5 mile walk, if I held the phone in my left hand (natural usage), on the slave side ear piece, the stream consistently cut in and out. This alone was enough to warrant a return and poor review.- If I had cupped either ear piece with my hand, the left side (slave) would cut out and not return until I removed my hand. Again, enough to warrant a return.- When I unlock and lock my screen, the stream would also hiccup. Annoying enough considering you don't want to accidentally operate the phone in your pocket but there are not enough remote navigation capabilities, you have to unlock your phone to change songs, read random articles, check messages.

title: Extremely Disappointed!
body: How disappointing!With over 14k reviews and 4.5 Star rating, it was based on those reviews and that rating that I purchased these earbuds. So unless I got a lemon those reviews are either misleading or expectations of a product are very low.Initially, the sound quality was awesome listening to music but when I used it for voice conversations over mobile, the person on the other line asked me to call them back when I'm in a less than noisy area. Then more people told me to not use 'whatever i'm using' when speaking to them because there was too much noise coming in. The sad part is, I wasn't in noisy areas so you can't imagine how disappointed I was. So I purchased another earbud for talking and used these Tozo's only for listening to music at the gym and they were worked well until... One morning less than 2 months since I purchased them the left earbud would not connect.I've had the lack of connection issue about 3x since I purchased it and that is not acceptable. Considering I have used these earbuds about a couple dozen times in less than 3-months, this is extremely disappointing.Oh btw... the range of the 5.0 Bluetooth SUCKS!The sound quality is very good and the noise cancellation is average. If the left earbud wouldn't go out anymore these earbuds would be awesome for listening to music ONLY. NOT PHONE CALLS!PROS-Nice design-Great for listening to music-Sound quality is very good-Cool carrying case that charges the earbuds-Fit comfortably in my earCONS-Not good for voice talking-Bluetooth range is horrible-Left earbud issues. Didn't turn on a few times in less than 3 months-You can't control volume from the earbuds

title: Great while they lasted
body: I've been listening to a lot of Podcasts and Audiobooks lately. While I think this is having a positive impact on my life, my roommate will likely tell you otherwise - he was probably pretty tired of having me blast "Principles" by Ray Dalio or "Catholic Stuff You Should Know" on my speakers.I knew cords were a thing of the past, but I wasn't about to shell out $140 for another hyped up piece of techwear. The Tozo T10s were just what I needed! Great battery life, good sound quality, and ergonomic design! I started wearing them on my walks, in the living room, in the shower (they're waterproof!) and before I slept.Imagine my horror when one morning at the gym, just a few days after purchasing, I put them in my ear, only to find that there is no sound coming out of the left earbud! It happens that the left earbud, the "slave", is meant to pair with the right earbud, the "master", which then pairs with the phone, allowing you to listen on both ears.Since then, I've tried everything: disconnecting from my phone, resetting them, putting them in the charging case and taking them out - all to no avail. The reset function acts really weird: you're supposed to turn them off, and then press for 10 seconds, at which point they should flash a purple light. And yet, whereas the left one will reset if you do this, the right one will reset only when it's on...I don't want to entirely discourage you from buying these. I was in contact with Amazon support and they'll file a warranty claim on my behalf, which means I should quickly get a replacement pair, with which this hopefully won't happen. Until Tozo disappoints me again, they get the benefit of the doubt. And for the price, I think you should give it to them too.

title: There are better options at this price.
body: TL;DR - Do not recommend buying unless the only thing you care about is waterproof, loud bass.Bought two pairs of these. Returned one thinking it was faulty. All Pros and Cons below are based on both sets so you can pretty much expect the same.Pros:Bass. If you love it, you will like these. Very strong profile compared to other sets. Likely due to the 8mm driver, most others are 6mm or smaller.Loud. Much louder than comparable sets. Rarely need to hit the volume limit. Consider if you need the extra volume headspace.Waterproof. You can submerge these in shallow water with no ill effects. Swimming, showering, etc are no problem.Connection. When dropping out of range, the left disconnects temporarily while the right usually stays connected. Reacquires very quickly.Cons:Sound. Noticeable distortion at mid-high frequencies on left earbud only. If you own these, don't listen to Thievery Co- Sweet Tides, you will never unhear it. Stereo sound oscillates back and forth from left to right earbud through center-stage while listening to music or on calls instead of staying at the same volume level.Drops. Loses connection at random times. Complete drop outs only correctable by turning off and on again or putting back into case. If you take critical phone calls, you can't depend on these.Call quality. Sub par compared to others. Voice transmitted sounds very distant. Voice transmitted cuts in and out constantly.Case. Buds occasionally do not power up when removed from case or only the right powers up. Large size for the amount of recharges you get compared to others. Battery should be bigger for a case this size.

title: Good while it lasted... for 4 months.
body: I bought this back in January so I've had a good four months of using this at this point. The battery life is still great. I can watch two movies with this on without the battery beeping on me - so I'm guessing around 3 or 4 hours of use per charge? The charging case is great to keep at hand. The earbuds are comfortable to keep on for the whole time and don't fall off for me. The noise reduction feature is still good and sound quality is generally great (see more below). The one feature I really wish it would have is the "skip songs via click" function or something.But that all aside, there are two problems that started a couple of weeks ago:1) connectivity between Bluetooth-enabled device and earbuds problems. Sometimes the connectivity between my laptop or whatever other device sputters and I'll have to wait a moment for it to reconnect. Or try the off-and-on again trick. For ex: I was watching a movie and forwarded it a bit, the bluetooth connectivity with the earbuds sputtered for some reason and there wasn't sound for a few milliseconds. Or when I'm taking a walk and have my phone in hands, the connectivity will sputter.2) fading connectivity between earbuds. Sometimes only the right earbud will connect with the device, or sometimes the left one will sputter only. Not sure what the issue is, but again sometime it just reconnects by itself or I do the off-and-on fix.These are my first earbuds so I am not sure how long these are supposed to last, but I would hope for something longer than a couple of months. Unfortunately the two problems above likely won't go away so I'll probably start shopping for another one soon :/

title: Does not work with long hair
body: This is a great example of why diverse voices are needed in STEM product development. When using this product in the shower or while swimming or in wind, or moving a lot without ballet tight buns in my hair, the touch pad reacts to my sweaty wet hair hitting it. How it reacts is random. Sometimes it skips or rewind the track, sometimes changes volume, sometimes power off. Very annoying. I bought them specifically for swimming so they're useless for me. There's no way to lock or disable the sensor, or even decrease sensitivity. It's another product clearly by a team that either does not respect their female's input, or did not hire enough of a diverse type of voices. This world was built mostly by men, and by result for men. They can remain blissfully unaware there's hundreds of ways that existing while presenting in any other form than traditional male means the experience you have with products is not going to be the same- to the point that without any heads up or warning it's virtually impossible to use the same tools equally. Some designs won't work because sexisim still exists, and if you bring it up the expectation is on the woman to adjust, adjustments the architects and people that look like them aren't expected to do. For them things just work as designed much more often. If there were an equal voice of diversity in the design and test, then this would've been caught and had a fix before it hit the shelves with great reviews. Now a substantial portion of the population has a less than optimal experience that could have been prevented just by asking and listening to someone other than people with short hair, which coincidentally are traditionally men.

title: Probably find better ones elsewhere
body: You get what you pay for.I do experience the connection issues others have mentioned in their reviews. I drive a lot for my job and experience “deadzones” here and there but this is ridiculous. The earbuds disconnect from each other for like a microsecond and play sound again. It’s not annoying to me yet, but it is rather disappointing since it happened a mere day after receiving them, and it happens very frequently, at least 2-4 times an hour.Sound quality is ‘alright’, it seems to me it tries to amplify certain sounds like people’s voices among a few others, and it makes it to where you think your volume is really high when it’s really not so I find myself constantly turning the volume up and down. Kind of an inconvenience since there are no volume buttons on the devices themselves so you gotta grab your phone, if you have it set up on a car carrier or something, and manually do it. I know it’s intentional and part of the design, but I feel they could’ve tested the sound quality more and made some adjustments.Comfortability is great, they snug nicely in my ears and don’t fall out like I feared they would. They don’t bother my ear holes after extended use like other earbuds do.I like the sleek design and portability of it all. And it comes with a 1 year warranty coupon that’s easy to set up so I’ll probably be using it soon to replace the pair I have and see if I had a defective pair or not.I’d give it a 2.5/ 5 because of the issues coming straight out of the box. Didn’t expect issues to happen until a few months later which I could excuse to the conditions I’d subject the earbuds to because of my job.

title: Easy pairing - poor connection stability
body: On the positive:Easy pairing makes it easy for anyone to operate and the fit seems secure, although I have yet to workout with them. A few walls gave me connection problems at work, but that's to be expected. I'll be testing the range at the gym today and battery life on the trails this weekend. I'm looking forward to a solid wireless experience!Update (all negative): Battery life was substantially less than expected, lasting only three hours (fully charged when starting). The connection was substandard with the signal fading out on the left earbud and the bluetooth source less than two feet away during a hike.However, the poor signal issue seemed to go away as long as I wore my brand new magic hat (a basic hiking hat purchased at REI). I tested this theory 8-10 times at random intervals during my 10 mile hike - as long as I wore my hat, the signal was solid, rmoving the hat immediately resulted in a lost signal. I fully expect to be able to communicate with aliens using this hat, although I'm not sure if the earbuds will be required. At least I'll be able to use my tin foil for other stuff.I'd fully recommend the hat as a companion item, except that when I sweat, it drips orange/red drops of sweat from the visor, so I'd only give three stars for the hat, itself.As far as all the other 5 star ratings for the earbuds, those seem fully related to the seller relentlessly pursuing and bribing people by offering a wireless charger for the non-working earbuds in exchange for a 5-star rating. I'd buy another magic blood hat before purchasing another pair of Tozos.

title: Drop in quality from previous models.
body: On the front, this seems like a great set of ear buds. Battery lasts for 6+ hours in one sitting of continuous use (playing music), water proof makes them slightly more rugged. Sound quality is good as you would expect for wireless earbuds.BUT the ear buds quickly lose their luster when used in any manner you'd want a set of wireless buds for.For starters the earbuds are programmed setup as separate objects. The right earbud is primary and seems to have to mirror/relay all sound to the left earbud. This means a lot of times the left ear will cut out for no apparent reason despite being mere feet from the bluetooth source.And this gets even stupider if you only want to use one earbud (leaving your other open to hear things around you.) The one active one will beep insistently for about 5 minutes every time you turn it on regardless if you have sound playing or not. And there is no option to turn this infuriating feature off.The signal strength is awful. Regardless if I'm using my phone or laptop to broadcast to them, walking more than ~30ft from the source makes the audio cut out intermittently, and just forget it if you break line of sight for any reason. For reference, my 2021 TOZO earbuds did NOT have this issue and could go 60+ feet or around the corner of a brick wall without any noticeable drops in audio.TL;DR if you only ever use these while within arms reach for your audio source, these are fine. If you ever dare to stray further than that expect music to cut out, and god help you if you need to move to the other side of a solid object.

title: These almost burned down my house
body: I purchased these in February of 2020 and was overall very happy with them. I didn’t use them daily, but most weekends while doing yard work or projects. Over time, the battery life shortened, but it was still always plenty for what I needed.Then, on December 20th, 2022, the case EXPLODED while charging on my desk and caught fire. The ear buds were thrown out of the case and the case itself was burning and melting, spewing toxic smoke and fumes into my house.Luckily, my wife happened to be walking in the immediate area when she heard the POP and was able to grab the case by the sides and rush it to a nearby sink where it continued to smolder and create a fog of choke-inducing smoke.By this time, I had heard my wife’s screams and made it to the area. I was able to use a towel to grab the case and get it outside in the 10-degree (Fahrenheit) weather to cool off.I’m currently trying to air out my house with open windows and doors in the middle of a Wisconsin winter night.This all happened about 30 minutes before we would’ve been asleep, so in that regard, we really lucked out. There’s no telling what would’ve happened had the case been left to flame and smolder on my desk.In short, I’m sure this is rare, but it’s certainly not something I’ve ever heard of happening with reputable, household brands of Bluetooth ear buds. These random Chinese brands don’t seem to follow the same quality and safety standards.Good luck out there, but I’ll never buy anything from anything other than a major manufacturer again.

title: I don't understand the hype
body: I really don't understand the hype with these earbuds. I guess because they are cheap? Compared with a $45 pair I had previously, these earbuds are really sub-par. Granted these are quite a bit cheaper, say 70% of the price of my other pair, but way less than 70% of the quality.My three biggest gripes:1. Bluetooth range is laughable. Frequently, I'll be working out on my back with my right earbud in and my phone on the ground to the left of me about 3 feet way from my body. Whenever, I do this the Bluetooth starts to cut out. I literally need to move my phone to the other side of my body, just to maintain connection. With a clear line of sight to the phone, I'd say this earbud works reliably from 15ft, but I wouldn't try any further.2. Battery life - Maybe my previous earbuds spoiled me, but I used to be able to do probably 4 or more charges of an earbud before needing to re-charge the case. With these headphones, I need to charge roughly every other day. I would guess (but have never really checked) that I get about 2 charges out of the case. Considering how bulky this case is, it should really last a much longer time.3. Disconnecting Bluetooth - Replacing the earbud into the case will not reliably desync Bluetooth from my device. Maybe this contributes to the battery issue from #2, but it is not an issue I would expect to have after using the earbuds for a month.Overall, I'm just disappointed in these. I don't expect magic from a $30 pair of earbuds, but these buds are clunky and poorly engineered. Find something better

title: Powerful bass? 4 hours charge? I don't think so!
body: I'll start with the good:1. Charging is easy. Just drop them into the case and put the case on your wireless charger.2. Pairing them is easy too. They automatically power on and pair as soon as you remove them from the case.Now the bad:1. SOUND QUALITY: Personally, I love bass. I was excited when I read that they had "powerful bass performance" and oversized drivers. I was further excited by the reviews that said that these had great bass. I don't know if these are defective or what, but I was dismayed to discover that the bass is nonexistent - just pure treble. If you hate bass then these are the headphones for you!2. BATTERY LIFE: I was in a 4 hour Zoom meeting this morning, and had charged the headphones all night right up until the beginning of the meeting. I knew it would be tight, but I had faith in these little headphones since the description stated "over 4 hours' playtime from a single charge." They lasted 3 hours and 6 minutes before they started yelling at me every 5 seconds that the batteries were low.3. COMFORT: They aren't all that comfortable. I have tried several of the included ear pieces, and have yet to find one that fits good in my ear and doesn't have me on my hands and knees trying to find my headphone on the floor. The fact that you have to put them in your ear with the heavy part on the top doesn't help; they naturally want to tip out of your ear.I'm giving these 2 stars because they actually physically work, even though they are DEFINITELY not as advertised.

title: earpiece beeps every 10 seconds for 2 full minutes after successful connection
body: PROS:1. 1 year warranty (needs to be activated, instructions are included in package)2. comfort: good3. charger: functional for the 30 days i had it. its nice that it comes with a 1 year warranty. make sure you register your warranty to "activate" it.4. showerproof: functional for 30 days I used it. i put a small strip of packaging tape over the mic hole though. just to be safe.5. bluetooth range and connectivity: max range is 30 feet for me. which is better than most v4.X bluetooth. no drop outs in media or phone calls.6. video audio lag: noneCONS:1. sound: treble is a too high. an EQ setting: "treble reducer" can remedy this somewhat. bass is ok, not good or bad. high treble is annoying.2. THE BEEPING.if you use just one earpiece and connect it to your smartphone, it will continue to beep every 10 seconds for 2 full minutes. this is to notify the user that the earpiece is searching for the other earpiece. which is dumbest design idea ive ever seen.just tell the user in the manual/online that the earpiece will search for the other earpiece for 2 minutes after successful connection. also you can just change the pace the LEDs flash to indicate this searching process instead. i only use one earpiece at a time. the battery for each earpiece only lasts 3 hours. so you can imagine how annoying this gets on a daily basis.conclusion: i am returning this product for the extremely annoying beeping "feature". knocking off 2 stars for this, and 1 star for the high treble audio.

title: Awesome for some people - not a good fit for my ears.
body: To start with the good - These ear buds came packaged very nicely. They seemed to pair up quickly. I've had them back in the case several times today and every time I take them out of the case, they pair up with each other and my phone almost immediately. The fit and finish of the case seems very nice. I like the magnetic dock for the buds and the lid of the case. The sound is reasonably good. More than adequate for listening to podcasts.So what's my problem with these things? They just won't stay in my ear and it doesn't seem to help to change to a different size of rubber / silicone tip. No matter what size tip I use, they just aren't angled to properly go into my ear canal and as a result, the main body of the unit does not get into the main part of my ear (auricle) where they are supposed to rest. They just hang precariously there until they eventually fall out. I'm a guy, 5'10" 180 lbs, with what I thought were regular sized ears.One of the main reason I bought these was because while doing dishes and other chores around the house, the headphone cable would catch on things and they would get ripped out of my ears. Well, today, I got these in the mail and started doing dishes and 10 minutes later I'm fishing it out of the sink disposal. I'm impressed that it still works after sitting under running water for 30 seconds, but I'd be even more impressed if they fit my ears.BTW, I have never had any problems with the standard iPhone shape of earbuds. These were so cheap - I really wanted for these to work.

title: Not Great.
body: I have a number of earbuds that I use for different activities or devices. Honestly, just a tad addicted to earbuds. I own the larger Sony and Bose noise cancelling earbuds for working out (Sony) and travel (Bose)(the Sony just are a bit more secure). Both are amazing in their own way in terms of noise cancelling (Sony) and sound (both) but they are REALLY large. Too large to sleep in. In addition, the battery life is very limited.So, for sleeping (listening to music or audiobooks) or watching TV, I had purchased earlier versions of the TOZO buds or Kurdene buds because they are so small that you can sleep or watch tv with no discomfort. The sound, not great, the noise cancelling...non-existent...but AWESOME in terms of comfort and size. The battery life is poor however, especially the Kurdene.The T-10 -- not great for any use. The sound is poor; the purported noise cancelling is minimal (compared to the Sony or Bose)(I, honestly see no difference with any non-noise cancelling earbuds) and the size is way to big, uncomfortable and insecure in the ear (compared to the prior TOZO or Kurdene). In addition, the connection is spotty at best. So, you get the size of and more discomfort than the Sony or Bose combined with the not great sound or non-existent noise cancelling of the Kurdene.I would note, it does have a great battery life. Way better than the Kurdene.In summary, really...not great, not good, and really, except for battery life, the worst of the alternatives imo.

title: Not that great
body: I can't recommend these buds, and have no idea why they're so popular.Let me couch my review by saying I am reviewing these based on my expectations based on product claims and user reviews. But given the price, my expectations aren't exceptionally highThe good: battery life and fit are decent. Sound quality is alright for the price, but dot expect it to be as good as a decent pair of dual driver buds. Case seems well made. Kinda bulky and plain, but does the job. Range is about middle average. No one has complained about the voice quality in calls and they come thru clear. The power and control buttons are click type, not touch sensitive, which is good for me if wearing gloves or have dirty hands (can knuckle it).The bad: there is NO skip track function or volume control, which sucks, esp when your 30ft from your device. Play/pause, call/hang up, and voice assistant is all the buds can control.I'd love to have an extra notch or two of volume.Wore them in the shower once and one of the buds wouldn't shut off or charge for several days. Working normally again now. Just don't count on them being waterproof beyond a bit of sweat, light rain, or a small splash.All in all, for the money, as far as performance goes, they're okay but nothing fantastic. The lack of media control seems self defeating. I am very disappointed in the water proofing, or lack of. Figure I'll give them to my niece and find something else, so basically a waste of money.

title: Can’t skip tracks/advance? I can’t go for that! (No can do.)
body: First attempt: purchased the color called “khaki” bc in the photo it looked like it was more of a rose gold. Nope. It’s like the Suntan colored pantyhose my grandmother wore. Returned without even attempting to use.Second attempt: ordered in black. Those looked better so I tried to put them to use. Pairing with my iPhone 11pro was more difficult than expected but really only took a few minutes. (Odd that it attempts to pair both headphones separately but the directions explicitly tell you to pair R only!?) But then I realized that you can only play/pause audio with the controls, can’t advance or go back. I use this feature ALL the time and I can’t let that go. Shame on me, it’s right there in the Q&A, I just didn’t see it. Worse still, if you do accidentally double tap in an attempt to advance (like most earbuds) it will callback your most recent call. I accidentally ended up ringing my neighbor and I can see myself doing that time and again. I’ll keep these and gift them to my daughter for Christmas. She’s never had a pair and wouldn’t be accustomed to skipping tracks by double-tap, so she wouldn’t know what she’s missing. If not for her, these would be going back.Note: The sound quality was initially terrible and I was shocked how bad they were... but then I put in the smaller size earbud to actually fit my ear and it was fine. Not stellar, but adequate for the price. So if you find that the audio is exceptionally poor, make sure that you have the right size for your ear.

title: Really wanted to like these.. sent back.
body: I own a pair of the Tozo T8's, had them for a few years and wanted to upgrade to something with more volume and better sound quality. I purchased the T10. The build quality was impressive. The charging station, and earbuds in general were larger than the T8's, maybe something to do with the waterproof nature of them, maybe bigger drivers than the T8. They did protrude from my ears much more than the T8, which I did not like. The sound quality was excellent. Very loud, good bass, good overall equalization, nice and balanced for such a small device. The battery life was good. I was VERY disappointed in the poor Bluetooth connection. The sound CONSTANTLY breaks up (doesn't disconnect entirely), to the point where I had to keep my phone always within 2 feet from where I was. I couldn't even put my phone in my pocket or move my head from side to side. Just constant , unnerving interruption.. killed my workouts! I reset them as per the website instruction, I forgot device and re-connected, I shut bluetooth on and off numerous times. Nothing stopped the interruptions . I have 3 other headsets and none of them do this. My T8s are better, my Jambuds (which hurt my ears) stay relatively breakup free within a decent range, my Vmoda Crossfades I can walk away 15 feet without an issue.. I literally had to hold the phone 1 foot in front of my face to get a decent constant connection with the T10.. even then it would sputter. Sent them back, which stinks because I liked the sound just fine.

title: A BENTLEY WITH NO TIRES!!!!
body: The size and overall functionality of these earbuds is great!• Sound Quality *****(Bass, Lows, Miss, Highs all hitting!!!)• Fit *****(Snug, adjustable, comfortable, stays put while active)• Battery Life *****(Excellent battery life, mine last for an entire 8 hour work day with continuous use. When they get low on juice I just drop them in the case for 15 mins and I’m back at it!)• Connectivity *****(Very easy to connect, simple to use and I have mine connected to multiple devices)• Range *****(Great range, I work in a large warehouse and I can walk to the other end with no problems)• Microphone *(Herein is my biggest issue, when I talk people say “I cant hear you” or “you sound really muffled”... this is a huge issue for me because I want the sound quality and freedom from cords but I NEED to be hands free and I can’t be at work, gym, jogging, etc and have to stop, grab my phone and switch from my earbuds to hold my phone because if not people can’t hear my responses clearly!!!)• Overall **(I rate these low because I need a truly HANDS-FREE experience from my earbuds, when the mic does not work properly, it severely inhibits the the purpose of my purchasing this device in the first place!)SUGGESTIONS:• FIX THE MIC• Create a simple app to tweak the sound quality, troubleshoot the mic & speakers, check battery life of the L & R earbuds, etc

title: if it doesn't stay paired, either one bud to another AND/OR to phone right next to your person...
body: CAN IT BE WORTH KEEPING?----------------------------------------<preface this review noting the following was gleaned with over 1 month of usage, on 2 different units, as the inital order was VERY susceptible to cutting out/losing phone pairing. Hardly worked at all, actually – just enough that i liked the potential and opted for a replacement to see if something got past QC...whatever form QC takes for Tozo><second context provider: this is the only pair of wireless ear buds i've ever used. it's easy to see on Amazon, as well as crowd-funded sites, that this form-factor is the currently, predominate iteration. case/dock that serves as recharger; buds very close, if not exactly the same, shape and general layout; claims of Bluetooth 5.0 and typically IPX6, 7 or 8, although these T10s are the only IPX8 rated i recall seeing.><CAVEAT each and every one of us really should keep in mind as we happily shop away online, wherever that may be: WHO or WHAT centralized agency, reputable or not, is testing and certifying these products?by exactly what measure is it that products are found to actually be and do what the things "marketing" indicates is so? Hmmm???...lets think on that for a moment...instead of blindly swallowing whatever hook is cast before us and then complaining that we've a hook embedded in our mouth.that said – IDK where to go to get reputable answers to the obvious questions regarding online (only, typically) product in 2019. if someone does, please post under this review cos I damn sure would like to explore that further.moving along to this item...>-------------------------------------------it is unfortunate, really – as otherwise, there is MUCH to like about this ear bud set up:* the price was outstanding, at least for this purchase as it was made during a lightning/gold box – whatever Amazon calls their countdown-timed deal opportunities. in my case, it was $29.99. what i paid is actually the teetering point for me as my inner-monologue vacillates between "when they work, i really do like these...and they were astoundingly inexpensive" to "when they cut-out or lose bud-to-bud pairing, no price compensates for resulting escalation of irritation to aggravation..." but as far as evaluating on price relative to sound, feature set and (mostly present) functionality +2* surprisingly good sound, incl much more low-end than was expected, particularly as one ought remember, they're wireless, fairly small form, ear buds. think about that for a moment...personally, i'll put them firmly between a good set of cans and any of my wired buds, of which i have a half-doz, incl some decent Sony's and a set of Bose sports – granting the Bose are not in-ear buds; which makes a CONSIDERABLE difference in sound quality, as well-fitted in-ear buds both better block outside noise and seal desired audio's path, directing toward the eardrums. +2*voice calls show well, both from the send and receive side. i have one colleague who routinely complains about calls made either through my car's bluetooth-connected system (mic is in steering wheel) or the now out-of-production Jawbone Icon – yet he's made no comments about my use of these buds, which tells me they're doing the job and then some. i have also made calls to other parties, for the purposes of testing call quality, and no one has observed any issues. my ability to hear and communicate effectively has been surprisingly good, although i haven't yet tested the buds on a call driving down the road with the windows open, or through a call standing outside on a windy day. the Jawbone did a pretty nice job with purported, military-grade noise-cancellation; not sure what/if considerations are employed to that end in these T10s. +1.5* fit/form is comfortable and stays in place – albeit w/o any running around. my most active conditions involve wearing while operating a residential, riding mower over uneven terrain, getting on/off, sweating were the most vigorous, operational conditions. they can droop some, and they do get a bit uncomfortable after a couple of hours, but then, show me some ear-buds that don't. +1* battery life was good (roughly 3-hrs) and the recharge time (via an unplugged, but charged, case/dock) seemed to be about 45-minutes in very generally observed times. IOW, i did not run down the buds, break out the stopwatch, note start/stop times; just looked at the clock when initiating recharge and then again when lifted. +1.25* i get that this form-factor—charging a case/carrying buds in case for on-the-go, cord-free recharging, IS the way these many, many iterations of wireless, BT earbuds are presently made—can't really be attributed to Tozo, but the functionality of the "case as charger" has real value. specific to this case, Tozo appears to use rare-earth magnets, resulting in small form and commensurately strong magnetism. very good for keeping that lid closed and your buds in the case where they belong. i've had my case hanging on my technician's toolbag for a couple of weeks now and have yet to find the lid opening on its own, or of course, my buds out of the case other than when i pull them out. the buds themselves are magnetically drawn into the form and onto the charging contacts, once you've got them most of the way inserted. +1.25SUGGESTED IMPROVEMENTS:*most necessarily, and glaring, is the frequent and random connectivity drop: some times it was the Rt-ear (phone pairing) bud from the phone, despite the phone's proximity sitting next to me in my mower's cup holder or in my front shorts pocket, ALWAYS on my right side. other times, it was Lt-bud connectivity loss to Rt-bud, despite the buds having recent charge. this happens FAR TOO FREQUENTLY and does so while they're worn – making it an obvious engineering/technical flaw. Surely the distance through a human's head is so similar that nailing down electronics appropriate to afford solid connectivity across this distance and through incredibly similar material (flesh/bone/brain/bone/flesh) or around the same, should be made certain before this product hits market.add the pairing drop that i easily—with about 90% consistency reproduced—initiated by tightly cupping an ear with one hand...now that i type this, would seem the bud-to-bud pairing is made with shockingly short-range/weak electronics. so, forget going through the head, these have trouble going AROUND the head and are interrupted by nothing more than the composition of an ungloved, human hand and some of the time, nothing more than...air?back to the drawing board or lab, Tozo... -5.5presuming that deal-breaker gets corrected, next suggestion is* add a feature that makes it clear that the buds are fully charged. update the voiced component to include current battery-life status, or put a meter on the case that provides visual status of the same, or change that LED series (essentially serves as a meter, but with 4-LEDs lined up vs a numeric display) from blue to green, or even better, have the earbuds' go from red to green? there is no way to know how much life left the buds have, other than the voice prompt that kicks in and repeats 3 or 4 times that the battery is low. the auto power-off function comes pretty swiftly thereafter, which is fine, but outside of this, the user has no way to know the buds current, charged state of being. this is kind-of irritating, really.as it is, appears the display times out. If the buds are lifted from the charging case with a full charge (?), the buds change to blue. Put them right back in the case, they revert to red and sit that way until timing out – unless the battery life is so erratic/poor that a full-charge isn't really a full-charge, or is until lifted from direct charge contact, or...see what i'm saying, Tozo? -1.5Nit-picky suggestion here, but valid all the same:* make the charging case/dock's molded loop a wee bit larger. not sure what Tozo expects would be looped through this, but it is too small—without modification—for anything other than a string. i have several cables w/screw-together, end crimped connectors that are typically used for/found in; keeping together keys. the 1/8" threaded end of this cable would not fit through the loop. i took a very small, sharp utility knife and shaved off little bits of plastic on the case-side (not the loop-side) of the loop, until i was able to get the threaded end of my cable's connector through the opening.my workaround now ensures i can easily and securely attach the buds' case to a carabiner, a tool bag, a belt-loop (although haven't done that), etc. -1RESULTS: with this highly unscientific and also highly arbitrary summary of my Tozo T10 observations, based on just a a couple of week's sporadic usage with the initial purchase, followed by several, much more intensive weeks now with the "replacement" unit...it's a wash.would i say to someone who doesn't already have a strong interest in wireless, ear-buds, "rush out and buy these"?i would not.would i encourage someone to try this or a different maker to see if another has defeated the paramount and absolutely necessary condition of consistent and persistent pairing to both phone and bud-to-bud? sure, why not – return them in within the appropriate time frame if they're no bueno.what's to say that should i go to and through a third set of Tozo's T10s they'd not work splendidly? seems unlikely, but...or that another manufacturer has nailed some technical shortcoming and consideration that appears to have eluded Tozo's grasp? w/o going down that road, can't know.for me, relative to my specific instance and most definitely considering the price paid and approximately 80% up-time in which the buds work as expected, coupled with already having backup, alternatives if/when the T10s get too exasperating, i'll probably just keep them. reach out to Tozo–if that's even possible w/o further time-suck as has been already been spent in grappling the issue, contacting Amazon multiple times to correct some bizarre-to-me SNAFU in which reviews for this product were disallowed due to (my paraphrased summary here) some goofiness with reviews and legitimate account, product purchases and now the subsequent, even-further time-suck of typing out this loooong overview...but roll the dice and pay full price ($49.99 as of this review's post)...nah.i suspect the technology is suuuuper close, but just isn't.quite.there.yet to pull off what is trying to be done with the wireless ear-bud market.

title: Comparison: TOZO T10 vs BEBEN 5H
body: I purchased both the BEBEN 5H wireless ear buds and the TOZO T10 wireless ear buds, for use with my Samsung Galaxy S7 Android phone. Prior to these purchases, I have been using Anker bluetooth (wired-to-each-other) ear buds; the Anker buds have served me well for a couple of years, but I was looking for truly wireless for a couple of reasons (I won't waste your time with those reasons--I suspect if you read them you wouldn't care).I checked out reviews and ratings for several brands, and settles on these two:-- BEBEN 5H Continuous 25H Cyclic Playtime IP68 Waterproof 5.0 Wireless Bluetooth Earbuds with Wireless Charging Case (here: https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B07PN2FK2W/ref=ppx_yo_dt_b_asin_title_o04_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)-- TOZO T10 Bluetooth 5.0 Wireless Earbuds with 【Wireless Charging Case】 IPX8 Waterproof TWS Stereo Headphones (here: https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B07J2Z5DBM/ref=ppx_yo_dt_b_asin_title_o06_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)I purchased the TOZO first, after reading many positive reviews, and seeing an average of 4.4 stars from more than (at the time) 9,000 ratings; the price was $49.99. Afterward, I noticed the BEBEN, getting an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 1,000 ratings; these were slightly less expensive, $45.99. I liked the controls advertised for the BEBEN better than those advertised for the TOZO.Both sets claim IPX8 waterproof certification, which means they can be dropped in 3 feet of water and should be good to go. (Interestingly, IPX8 *does not* mean all of the lower IPX ratings are covered. For example, IPX6 means it can handle a spray of water at some amount of pressure; being rated for IPX8 does not mean it passes the IPX6 test.) Both are bluetooth. Both advertise good music sound quality, and good use for phone calls.When I received them, I decided they must have some common manufacturing. While the shape of their cases is different, there are some notable elements in common: Both have four small LED lights in the case to indicate the case's battery level, both cases close magnetically, and they come with identical graphics and text materials to communicate they're good for swimming (surface, not underwater) and good for showers. The TOZO advertised that it came with 5 pairs of different sized silicone cups to fit different sizes of ears, and it did; the BEBEN advertised that it came with 3 pairs, but it came with 5. Both cases charge wirelessly, and both cases can recharge the ear buds multiple times before needing a case recharge.I put both sets through the same test routine. I listened to similar music, and I made phone calls. With each set, I ran 4.5 miles on a treadmill, then showered while wearing the ear buds and listening to podcasts. When I was in the shower, the ear buds were connected to my phone which was about 25 feet away with 2 walls in between. Here are my findings:SOUND QUALITY:I found the sound quality to be very good in both. I'm probably not as demanding in this area as some, but I would guess I'm as demanding as most.Score: tieSOUND BLOCKING:Neither set has active noise cancelling. They block noise through their physical design; the silicone cup which fits into the ear canal physically blocks a lot of environment noise. I think both do brands do a fine job of blocking environmental noise, so the music sounds good.Score: tieCONNECTION STABILITY:One complaint I'd read a few times regarding the TOZO was dropped connections. When I tried the BEBEN, as I got around 25 feet from my phone, with 2 walls between me and my phone, on a couple of occasions both ear buds disconnected, then reconnected very quickly; each disconnect-reconnect lasted maybe half a second. It was a little annoying, but rare, and pretty understandable given the distance and walls. The TOZO did the same thing. However, the TOZO did the disconnect-reconnect routine many, MANY times, including when the phone was only 3 feet away and there were no obstructions between the phone and ear buds.Score: BEBEN 5H winsCONTROLS:The BEBEN 5H's buttons offer more controls than the TOZO T10's. The TOZO's controls include:- Answer phone- Hang up phone- Redial last number- Play/pause- Reject phone callThe BEBEN's controls do not offer 'redial last number', but otherwise have the same controls plus:- volume up/volume down- next/previous (song, podcast, etc.)Score: BEBEN 5H winsFIT:The fit was very similar between the two. The TOZO are slightly larger (as seen in one of the attached photos). Both stayed in my ears while I ran 4.5 miles on a treadmill. Neither the shaking nor the sweat led to ear buds coming loose.Score: tiePHONE CALLS:For me the sound was good. I was told by my daughter that on her end I sounded good. In the initial tests, I found no difference. Since the initial testing, I have used the BEBEN for phone calls on many occasions; on a couple of occasions I have been told by the person at the other end of the call that they could not hear me well. I don't have enough information, yet, to decide whether that was because of the environment I was in at the time, or if there's some flaw in the BEBEN mic.Score: tieWATERPROOF:Both sets survived my shower with flying colors. They performed fine. It was nice to keep listening to music or podcasts while I was showering. In each test, I removed the ear buds while I was in the shower (so I could clean my ears), set the buds on the bath tub rim, then put them back in my ears about a minute later. No problems with regard to water.Score: tieOTHER:These two things are small, but I notice them. First, the TOZO case lid is slightly translucent. Because of that, when the battery level LEDs are lit, they can be seen through the case lid, which is nice (this is shown in one of the pictures); the BEBEN lid is completely opaque, so the LEDs cannot be seen without opening the case.Also, as seen in one of the pictures, the "L" and "R" on the BEBEN ear buds are small and low-contrast with the surrounding plastic. For a 54-year-old who needs reading glasses but who otherwise has good vision, I wish they would have made these markings easier to see.Score: TOZOSUMMARY:The BEBEN 5H are very good. I'm glad to have them and will make great use of them. The have the added benefits of offering additional controls over the TOZO, which I am glad to have, and they are slightly cheaper.The TOZO are pretty good, but that issue with disconnecting and reconnecting is very annoying. This is something they should fix. As can been seen in other reviews, I'm not the only person to find this problem. This set will be going back.Oveall Winner: BEBEN 5H

title: 🤯Fair Warning!!… Sound may turn up to FULL BLAST on its own!
body: I ripped my earphones from my ears and uttered some very choice words as they flew across the room announcing “INCOMING CALL”!! I was enjoying some calm music recovering (or trying to!!) from a rather intense day. That all ended when my husband called and Ms Tozo10 decided to SCREAM FULL BLAST, “INCOMING CALL!!”.Needless to say, if you value your hearing you might want to reconsider these earphones, as there seems to be a glitch that auto adjusts the volume—-never lower, always higher. …And these things are FREAKING LOUD!!! This isn’t just happening when people call—albeit that wins the prize for the loudest they’ve turned up on their own!! The volume just seems to have auto adjustment issues in general.Setting aside the near heart attack this evening, I will write a few PROS & (a few more Cons) as to not completely destroy their reputation, bcs these are actually pretty good earphones.*On a side note, on my own I am comparing these with Tozo T6, so I’ll make some notes about T6 alongside my eval.PROS:✔️Decent sound quality (not near as good as T6). T6 ARE A NEWER MODEL✔️Works with iPhone X, as do T6✔️Love the dots indicating remaining charging case battery power✔️In the week I used these they had PLENTY of battery power (better than my 10 month old highly overrated AirPod Pros). I have read a handful of reviews saying the battery power on the T10 & T6 does not stay this good for very long, but I cannot vouch for that at this point.✔️Earbuds are slightly smaller/lighter weight than T6. Slightly more comfortable to wear. (I still prefer the airy AirPod Pro type buds as far as breathability).✔️Touch control pad is less sensitive than T6 (Good thing!!)✔️These stay in my ears well, as do the T6’s. Both stay in better than my AirPod Pros.✔️Can use one earbud at a time, as you can with T6CONS:✔️VOLUME auto adjusts (AKA AUTO BLASTS to FULL VOLUME!) at random times giving victim a potential heart attack.🤯✔️Case feels a little cheap (not a dealbreaker).✔️Earphones cover a majority of the ear and can get a little sweaty, as do T6✔️Battery case needs a spring to slow down the lid closure (not a deal breaker)I’m trying several pairs of Tozos, and so far I’ve used T6 and T10. Overall, I prefer the T6, with the exception of the ultra-sensitive touch pad. I don’t move around much so it’s not usually a problem. Both models have some Volume control issues, however T10 is worse when comparing volume LEVEL glitch. T10 buds are slightly more comfortable bcs they’re not quite as big as T6, but it’s not a big difference.If sound quality is a priority, T6 are *hands down* better than T10. If you move around a lot, T10’s are less sensitive to touch, thus better for movement, but know that the superior sound quality of the T6 is just not present with T10’s.☑️I’m now trying Tozo NC2’s. Will return here to add more to my evaluation after I’ve had more time to evaluate them. So far;✔️Sound quality is excellent, love the “surround sound”✔️Comfort level is the best of all 3, but shape needs some softening on edges. Not a dealbreaker.✔️A little problem I noticed, as have others…. when you adjust the L earphone it is too easy to toggle the NC feature. That touch area is too sensitive and should be moved down to the stem.✔️Also, if you don’t have a really good fitting bud, it can automatically activate the voice assistant! The buds moved around and made my phone automatically dial a woman who interviewed me for a job!!, my health insurance company, Instacare, and my dermatologist!!! I’m hoping with a better fit it will not do this anymore. This is a dealbreaker if I cannot lower this sensitivity. No way to remove VoiceAssistant feature as far as I can tell. I don’t want that feature.., I sleep with them on. Holy moly can you imagine how many accidental calls I could make?***Side Note: I am REALLY missing the SOUND QUALITY of my T6’s, but I may or may not have showered with them on🤣 (and no they are NOT waterproof, despite the claim) and I’m letting them dry. Day 5 post shower, the volume is actually returning after letting them sit. Time will tell if they sound as crystal clear as they once did.😭Tozo has some great things going on with their earphones, but need to fix a few very fixable glitches… and they’re going to kick Apple & Bose #%%! …Especially if they can keep up the PHENOMENAL sound quality of the T6’s. (Btw…T6’s sound way better than my AirPod Pros. Esp bcs I like a base-y sound [it gives some ppl headaches tho].Tozo is a brand to watch. Their earphones are competitively priced and their sound quality is superior to much more expensive brands, esp in T6 model.

title: Bang for your Buck
body: Update: 12/2/19Downgrading to three stars. The battery case won't hold a charge. It seems to charge fully in less than 30 minutes and then only has enough juice for one charging session (with earbuds). Hoping I got a bad set. Keeping my review to three stars currently because I use them at work so it's not super-inconvenient to charge them constantly, but I would very much like to take them when I travel (which I obviously wouldn't be able to do at this stage). Email sent to TOZO support; I will update with new review if and when things are resolved.Original Review:Two things you should know about me: I'm a budget guy, and I like a good deal. And while I admittedly did spend an inordinate amount of time debating whether or not to buy the AirPods, I just couldn't do it for a couple of reasons: 1) Like anything Apple, they're expensive AF. $159 for the base pair all the way up to $250 for the Pros. And 2) I don't like the way they FEEL, man. The hard plastic just ruins my ear canal after only 30 mins of use. How anyone can describe them as comfortable is beyond me. Enter the TOZO T10 Wireless Earbuds.Confession time: I only bought these because my wife asked me to look into a new pair of headphones for her. I spent several days researching budget headphones and expensive headphones and TOZO was a name that kept popping up again and again and again. So I did what any good husband would do...I gave her my old headphones and bought these for myself! Hey, I had to try them out to make sure she would like them, right? Well, the verdict is in and I guess I'm going to have to buy a pair for her cause these things are legit. I've broken my review of the headphones into five sections below, with a rating for each based on a (you guessed it) 5-star scale.Sound quality: 5 stars.Honestly I wasn't sure what to expect since these were my first wireless (non-corded) headphones, and I've had quality issues with wireless corded headphones in the past. But these are fantastic. I actually have to turn the volume down so I don't blow out my eardrums.Comfortablility: 4 starsAll-in-all, pretty average for in-ear headphones. I had to try out about three earbud sizes before I found the one that didn't irritate my ear canal, but them's the brakes. And that's why they come with four sizes: one of them just has to fit. Find that perfect size, and you won't have to worry about them falling out. I literally shook my head like a wet dog to try to get them to come out and they wouldn't budge. Extended wear can make your ear a bit numb, but the play time is around 3-3.5 hours anyway, so it gives my ear a break.Connectivity: 5 starsSo easy to set up, a caveman could do it. I especially like that they auto-connect to each other when you take them out of the charging kit and then connect to your last bluetooth device. Less steps for me. Every now and again I'll experience a disconnect and/or a drop, but they're pretty fast to reconnect. And for $50, you can live with it.Price: 4 starsNothing inheritely wrong with $50, just a bit more than I'm used to paying. Like I said, I'm a budget guy, so I typically don't pay more than $20 for headphones, but I'm also a deals guy. In this case, $50 was well spent.Charging: 5 starsThe wireless case is heavy, and doesn't feel cheap. The earbuds drop in magnetically and release with ease. They charge FAST. Maybe not as fast as AirPods, but who cares? 20 minutes gives me about an hour of play, and a full hour gives me over 3 hours of continuous play. And the charging case only takes a an hour or two to fully charge. I recommend investing in a nice wireless charging unit so you won't have to worry about cords. I use these exclusively at work, so less cords is more.I'll see how they hold up over time, but so far I'm very impressed. Looks like I'll be buying a pair for my wife after all.

title: Remarkably Mediocre
body: This was my first set of wireless earbuds, and while I was hoping the glowing reviews would mean they were more up to snuff than the lackluster offerings of yesteryear that had kept me from going wireless, they just didn't pan out. A couple of big issues mean that if I had to advise anyone else, I would tell them to give these a pass for now.First and most important is the fit. These have small round deformable in ear tips, just like my personal favorite wired earbuds, while the rest of the oval shaped unit sits outside the ear. Theoretically, the snug fit of the buds helps by cutting out outside noise and firmly holding the unit inside the ear, lest it fall out. Helpfully, they even provide a plethora of tips, for all your odd sized ear needs. In practice however, they have a remarkable ability to completely fail to fit in the ear. I couldn’t tell you why, but for the life of me, not a single tip would help them stay in my ear once I let go of them. Something to do with the housing shape meant that no matter which size or orientation I used, the buds continually loosened and fell out. I pushed and rotated them all manner of directions, practically until it hurt and I felt like I was poking my eardrum, but as soon as I released them, they would just relax and start slipping out. This is just sitting still mind you. I had bought these intending to run with them, but I don’t dare because I know it’s a one way trip to losing them in a hedge. Not only is this incredibly frustrating to have them fall out all the time, but the fact they don’t seat correctly means that the sound is almost always at its worst, and you basically are left with a listening experience equivalent to eavesdropping on the content of a bus seatmate. Maybe I’m the oddball with strange lobes, but I’ve never had this kind of issue with other earbuds, it’s always been pop them in and away you go. These just fail to deliver.Second, the sound. Leaving aside the fact that you don’t get to experience the heady heights of their full potential unless you press them hard into your ears and sit motionless, these have remarkably underwhelming sound quality. Switching from any other headset or earbuds to these is a noticeable drop in sound quality. They’re tinny and shallow sounding. It’s not the classic scratchy though a drainpipe cheap headphone sound, but it’s like the LaCroix of audio. I can tell what it should sound like, but there’s just….less. Even listening to a podcast you lose the richness of the hosts voice, and with music half the band seems to have called out. Just forget it if you want base, percussion, or even mids honestly.Third, the connection. This was the one thing I thought they had going for them up until a couple days ago. For a couple weeks they were kinda pathetic sounding and a waste, but at least they connected well and were an okay backup pair of earbuds if you couldn’t bother to cross the room for better ones. The connection functionality was solid, impressively so. But the last couple of times I’ve tried to use them, they’ve had some frustrating connection issues. One earbud would connect and the other wouldn’t, or they would both be on but somehow separate into left and right mutually exclusive units in the phone menu. It’s not a consistently bad issue, but still, if they sound rubbish, and you can’t keep them in your ears, at least they should connect.Maybe I’m just the odd one out and everyone else has a better experience, but I just had to say, these really under delivered.

title: Perks are good, but consider the drawbacks
body: When my Bose Sountsport headphones were chewed on by a cat and rendered broken, I needed a quick and effective replacement and I was admittedly influenced by a Buzzfeed article that recommended this pair. The positives are significant - price, of course, sound quality, easy pairing, quick charing and long battery life - but there are significant drawbacks as well. Maybe this could go unsaid, but these are not an investment piece and you will ultimately get what you pay for.First, they cannot pair with multiple devices at once, and they cannot even pair with a device if another recognized device is in range with its bluetooth turned on. For example, if I’m listening to music on my phone and I need to jump on a Zoom call on my computer, I cannot just switch the pairing, I have to turn off bluetooth on my phone, turn on bluetooth on my computer (because it had to be off to be paired with my phone…), and manually connect my computer to the headphones. I have an iPhone and Mac, but this has also been the case with my work PC.Second, the fit is bulky and a little strange. I have what I would consider average size ears, and I have to use the smallest rubber cover to get anything close to a secure fit. Even so, the audio quality is significantly impacted when I am running and the headphones bounce and jiggle for lack of secure fit. Further, a bump from adjusting my glasses or tucking my hair is often enough to dislodge them, as they stick out strangely from the ear.Finally, the controls are not ideal. I do not consider AirPods the be-all-end-all headphones (I much prefer my Boses), but credit must be given to their intuitive and easy controls. On this product, one tap pauses the audio, which would be fine, except that what constitutes a “tap” is inconsistent and frustrating. Sometimes an accidental bump will pause the audio, other times an intentional tap doesn’t register the pause. Part of this may be due to the strange fit and bulkiness outside the ear - when you can’t get good spatial awareness for where it’s sitting in your ear, it’s hard to know where to tap. I thought I would become familiar enough over time, but it hasn’t really happened. Holding down on the left turns volume down and holding down on the right turns volume up, which isn’t bad in itself, but there are annoying beeps that indicate volume is moving up or down. I also wish that they paused when you take one headphone out - but maybe this is proprietary to Apple.One final note - a few other reviewers mentioned that over time they had issues with one or both headphones randomly unpairing. I didn’t have this problem at first, but just within the past day or so I have had the left piece randomly unpair a couple of times, at which point I put it back in its case, take it out, and it re-pairs automatically. Having the case nearby has made this a small issue, but if it happened while I was out on a run without the case, I’d be very annoyed.TLDR; you get what you pay for - this is a decent stop-gap product if you’re between good pairs or you need a backup to your good pair, but I wouldn’t count on it for a long term, hard-working purchase.

title: Good product, but not worth the price tag.
body: I returned this product for a few these cons:* Touch control - for something that is in your ear, you have to press the button way too hard for it respond. I used other in-ear headsets that a light press is all that is needed.* connection reliability - I had the earphones less than 5 feet from my phone with no interference in-between and the connections will still pop in and out.* Annoying blinking light - when in ear, the light blinks constantly which can be very annoying at night. Other competing earphones will only blink in ear if you are on a call which makes more sense than an constant blink.* The earphones are the exact same form factor (not a plus) - because they are the same shape, it is possible to put them in the wrong ear. While this doesn't sound like a big deal for some, I feel I shouldn't have to closely examine the earphone for the L or R notation to know what ear to put it in. Plus, only the right earphone will automatically disconnect the earphones from your device, so its important to know which is which especially when you are in mono mode... and speaking of which...* Mono mode - the earphone is constantly beeping looking for the other earphone. This is true for both the R and L ear piece, so Mono mode is a worthless "feature" in my opinion. It's incredibly annoying if you have to charge one side only but while you're listening with the other side, it is constantly beeping looking for the other side to connect to.* Large case - While this case can charge the earphone several times and itself can be charged wirelessly, it is extremely large. other competing cases have half the footprint, but can almost have the same charging capacity.UPDATED (7/23) I should have listed pros:*Well made - despite the cons listed above, you can tell the product is made of high quality and will likely last long.*Wireless Charging - The wireless charging worked flawlessly and was very responsive on the couple of different wireless pad it was tested on. Unfortunately, part of the convenience of wireless charges meant a larger case. For my purposes, while I loved the wireless charge, I would stick to cable charging if it meant a smaller case for portability. Granted, while still very portable and if you only owed this set, the case may seem small, but in comparison to other units I have used, the case was huge in comparison.*Customer Service - The customers service is very responsive and even offered a replacement, but again for my purposes, the cons outweighed the pros, so the product was returned.Overall, Tozo has a very good, well made product with great sound and (fairly unique) features but in this reviewers opinion, some there are glaring 'issues and build decisions' gives the set its current 3 star review. The 'issues' (I put issues in quotes cause it may not a deal breaker for some, but it is for me) can probably be an easy fix in a future version that would earn an easy 5 star review.

title: Not great, but not horrible, getting what you pay for.
body: I bought these to replace a halfway decent bluetooth headset I used for running that crapped out on me after a solid 18 months of use. I am pretty sweaty so I figured I'd give the wireless earbud style a shot and see what it was like given the IPX8 waterproofing certification. The price is... compelling. 50 USD for a pair of wireless earbuds is hard to ignore.So... how are they? Decent.The sound quality is decent. I am no audiophile, but I wouldn't call the sound quality to be any more than that. Acceptable range so most music genres sounds nice, podcasts come through clearly enough.The Charging Pod is convenient and keeps the lil stinkers from bouncing around in my bag and maintaining a good charge. Given I routinely loose small things, this is something I am actually pleased with. Magnets keep the earbuds fixed in the case for storage and charging. The case itself acting as a battery pack is a nice touch to avoid constant re-charging.I've not had any significant issues with connection in-and-out while running. I admittedly do not use these for just normal listening or phone call purchases. They paired to my apple watch without issue and play with only the occasional hiccup. They seem to have better connection quality to my watch than my previous running headset so that was nice to see.First mildly annoying issue are the buttons and controls. They are more than a bit on the unresponsive side. Difficult to press the button on either of the two earbuds without my finger slipping or feeling like I'm going to crush the plastic housing into my ear. Both sides have the same control options, and there is no volume control so you are going to have to use the source of your audio to manage that.The fit is not bad, but not great. There are a nice set of optional silicone sizes to try to get the optimal fit. And for a cursory test of music walking through my home, that seemed like a good fit. Then I went for a run. They just didn't 'quite' fit right, with one or the other starting to wriggle its way out every 4-5 minutes or so. Not so bad for a short run but if you go for distance, this will probably become infuriating. It very much feels like a loop or band could (and should) be affixed to loop around the back of the ear to combat this with a minimum of fuss. It honestly seems like they are on the cusp of a solid robust fit that would handle the trials of head bouncing from any vigorous exercise.I'd give it a 2.5 Star Review if that were possible. Given the price and the relative quality even to wired sets I've owned, bumping it up to 3 seems fair. But pretending these are great is being very generous indeed. A better set can be had, just be prepared to pay for it.

title: Amazing Sound Quality, But....
body: I want to start by reminding anyone reading this that wireless earbuds are still a baby technology, THEY WILL BE BUGGY. Even Apple's airpods are buggy. It's perfectly normal the first few years a new technology is released.I have had these for a month now and am writing this review based on my daily use of the product.This being said, I am a landscaper by trade and am moving around for 12+ hours a day. I needed something wireless as any wired earbuds get caught on my equipment annoyingly often. I bought these after learning they had a 10 hour play time, reading the good and bad reviews, and weighing my options. I got them first day and played them until they were dead (about an hour) and charged then overnight. To my dismay, they only charged halfway even though they were magnetically attached to their case all night. No chance they "slipped off" or weren't connected. I'm not sure if this is part of the glitches or if I have a defective charging case, but this seems to be a typical problem. I just make sure to wiggle the earbud so that it "wakes up" and continues charging. Unfortunately, they do not have a 10 hour play time. Once charged, they will play for about 3 hours before dieing. Luckily, none of my yards take longer than 3 hours to landscape and my drive time between properties can be an hour or more sometimes, so I just charge them in the truck.Another hitch I have noticed, as other reviews have stated, the booth connectivity is weak between the right unit (main unit) and the left. This means that at least a handful of times throughout the day the left earbud blips in and out, interrupting the music. Now, if you are at a desk job or work with very little movement you may not notice this issue, but since I am constantly moving around, it happens with annoying frequency.With all that being said, I love the sound these headphones have! The bass is amazing and they don't detract from the music quality. They also have a feature in both units that allow you to pause/play with a single push. They are also pretty good at phone calls. My husband and mom both say they don't notice anything. Unfortunately, there is no volume control on the unit, you have to use your connected device to control it.In recap:Pros: sound quality and bass, wireless, play/pause function, phone call quality goodCons: glitchy connection, play time less than 3 hours, intermittent charging port, no sound control on unitWould I recommend them ... No. This company still has quite a few bugs to sort out, especially the connectivity issue between earbuds. But they are good enough for me and work sufficiently for the price.

title: This was an ALMOST perfect purchase
body: When I recently purchased these earbuds, I was looking for great sound for my music, easy connectivity, water resistance, Bluetooth 5.0, and a very clear mic for calls. And while I did find that a few of these needs were definitely met, I was extremely disappointed with the mic and charging capacity.Pros...The connection was pretty easy once I added the Bluetooth name to my Android phone.
