Each time I took out the earbuds from the charger after turning on my Bluetooth, they easily connected with each other.These earbuds have GREAT stereo sound...very clear and rich sound which I think could rival higher priced earbuds.The carrying charger case is a great size, very compact and the transparent lid allows you to see the charging lights while closed.The earbuds stay put and come with 3 different size buds that should be good for any ear size.CONS...The mic...I called several different people to see how the calls sounded on both ends and ALL could barely hear me. I heard them fine on my end but it doesn’t really matter if no one can hear me. I did try putting the earbuds back into the charger and reconnecting to Bluetooth but it didn’t help. This is such a disappointment because if it weren’t for the poor call mic (and 1 other issue), I would have been SO happy with these!The other problem is that the charging lights and possibly the charger itself was very inconsistent. The directions say that when charged, the lights on the earbuds will go from red to off, but every time they went off and I removed them for a second and put them right back, they would remain red. So are they charged or not? Very confusing. Also, when using the buds, the lights weren’t always the color they should be. For example, the dominant bud (Right) should continuously blink fast (blue and red) but sometimes it would just be red.The battery/charger case....The charging case has 4 blue lights that, when charged, should be all solid. However, after fully charging the case, I used the earbuds for a short time then put them back and no light came on at all. So I’m not sure if the case was charged or not. I found myself spending way too much time being on “light patrol”. Crazy.Final thoughts, it’s possible that the earbuds that were sent to me were defective, and if this is the case, I would be very willing to try another one. I really wish it worked! I guess if you are looking to just have great music quality then you will be very happy you are willing to put up with the dodgy battery.

title: For $30-$50 bucks this is a good deal with built in wireless charging case compatibility
body: Pros:-the charging case is compatible with wireless charging pads in addition to the traditional micro USB charging method-price point (especially if purchased during lighting deal)-amazing sound for this price-comfortable with the correct fitted earbud tipsCons:-charging case continues to discharge earbuds even when they are powered off inside the charging case, depleting the charging case in about 6 days without any use-earbuds are a little large with regards to protrusion out of ears-battery charging case could have a bigger battery for longer earbud charging timesThe long version:What makes these earbuds worth purchasing is they sound good overall. There's a little bit of emphasis on the bass but that is mentioned in the breakdown of the product, so I knew prior to purchasing these. The bass isn't so strong that it drowns out the mids or the highs. The wireless charging case compatibility with wireless charging pads truly makes these wireless.I can't speak for those that have had a bad experience with these headphones, but I'm completely satisfied with these earbuds. What makes these earbuds and earbuds similar to these earbuds noise cancellation effective is fitting the proper earbud tips on the earbuds for your ears. That's not to take anything away from these earbuds because they are exactly everything I expected them to be. I didn't buy these for phone calls nor have I tested them for phone calls so I can't comment on that aspect.Update 8/4/19I noticed one major flaw with this earbuds that made me take away two stars because the charging case is a very important part of why I purchased these. It's important for the charging case to be able to hold a charge for a long period of time (at least 30 days) without having to charge the case when the earbuds are powered off inside the charging case. For some unknown reason the charging case will continue to discharge with the earbuds powered off inside the charging case. It seems as though the earbuds turn themselves back on when placed inside the charging case. I haven't actually tracked how long the case holds a charge with the earbuds powered off inside the case but I suspect the case will loose its charge in less than 7 days. This is not normal because I have other wireless earbuds that don't have this problem with the charging case discharging after the earbuds are placed inside the case sitting for many days, sometimes weeks before I use the earbuds again.

title: Great Headphones for affordable price!
body: With the current pandemic taking place around the globe, we're forced to wear masks during our outings. I work in a warehouse and required to wear a mask for my safety and for those around me. My previous headphones were wired and you can imagine how frustrating it was to get those wires tangled in the elastic of my mask. Many of my co-workers have similar earbuds like these and so I finally decided it was time to get a pair myself.I wasn't looking for any specific name brand. I wanted something affordable and would last a good chunk of the day at work. Then I came across these earbuds.Unfortunately when I first received the ear buds in the mail, they were defective out of the box. It took me two days to realize it. I've never had earbuds like these before, so for a few days, I thought I may have set them up incorrectly. Tozo offers warranty for their earbuds, so I obviously took advantage of this. It took 3-4 days for a response, but within that time, Amazon had replaced the defective with a pair that worked right away.Up until I received a response, I enjoyed the T10. They quickly connected to one another and to my phone. The distance wasn't too grand, it got static and lost connection if I got too far away from my phone.The battery life was about 3-4 hours. Working 8+ hours a day, this was frustrating because now I was left without music as I was pulling orders for our contractors and in house techs.The package offered 5 pairs of buds so you may comfortably fit the earbuds in your ears for maximum comfort. Sadly, they didn't have a small enough pair, so I did run into some issues of the earbuds popping out of my ear.When I received response from Tozo Warranty, they were helpful at trying to pinpoint the problem I was having with the previous set I received. Making sure I followed every step to be sure the earbuds were in fact defective. Even after letting them know Amazon replaced the first pair, they went ahead and decided to replace my previous defective Bluetooth with their upgrade T6 model. And boy was it an upgrade!The T10 is still a great affordable bluetooth. If you're someone who doesn't wear headphones too often, maybe only for a quick grocery trip or a quick jog or walk, the T10 would be perfect for you. But if you're looking for something to last longer in the day, look into the T6 upgrade. You will not be disappointed!

title: Cheap wireless earbuds with some issues
body: This was the cheapest pair of wireless Bluetooth earbuds with decent reviews that I could find. I got them mainly to see if I liked the wireless earbud experience before spending more on a higher quality pair.I have only used them for a few hours so I can’t comment on battery life.The sound quality is decent, about on par with other cheap wired earbuds I’ve used. Not for audiophiles, I’d say.I had no problems getting them to pair with my iPhone X.The charging case seems to work well. I have no issues opening or closing it, and the earbuds pop in and out easily. The included lanyard for the case is a bit ridiculous (would anyone actually wear the case around their neck?). I may cut it down to be more wrist sized. It’s also pretty cheap material and not comfortable if you were going to wear it.The instructions say that there are four pairs of ear tips of four different sizes, but what I got was six different pairs of ear tips in three sizes and two different types, one with orange tubes in the center and one without. There is no explanation of this in the manual. The middle sized pair with the orange tubes fit me best.The only issue I have run into is very odd. When I’m walking around outside, and I put my phone in my left pants pocket, or hold it below waist level on the left side, the sound cuts in and out and then eventually stops. When I put my phone in my right pants pocket, or hold it on the right side or above waist level on the left, there are no issues. Inside my house, I can walk around and listen all that I want with no issues regardless of what side my phone is on. Also, it doesn’t matter if my phone is inside or outside of the pocket, just that it’s held in that location. My theory is that there is more interference from other wireless signals when I’m outside, which may affect the signal strength of either my phone or the earbuds’ Bluetooth, so the right earbud (which I’m assuming is the one that connects to the phone) needs to be as close as possible to the phone. Just a theory, but it’s consistently reproducible.Overall they seem like a decent introduction to wireless earbuds, if you can put up with the annoyance of having to keep your phone on the right side when walking outside. Mostly annoying for me because I’m accustomed to keeping my phone in my left pocket.

title: Lacking several features I would have expected at this price, but overall good buds!
body: So I recently bought these TWS Earbuds because my previous pair (different brand) were going the way of all man-made products, (they were dying). My previous pair was a no name brand directly from China. I'll do a bit of comparison between the two of you're interested. My Chinese pair cost roughly $15 shipped, these cost about $30 shipped.Chineses TWS Earbuds:$15Charging case could charge your phone.Charging case showed how charged your buds were and had an LCD screen to show internal battery percentage.Audio quality was decent, nearly comparable to these earbuds.You could skip tracks forward or backward, pause/play music, answer/hang up calls, raise/lower volume, and access Google assistant, all based on gestures of the buttons on the earbuds.Battery life on a single charge was about 4-5 hours both buds on about 80% volume.TOZO TWS Earbuds:$30Charging case can't charge your phone.Charging case shows how charged the case is by 4 LEDs. No LCD showing specific percentage.So far I like the audio quality, I think it is exactly/better than what is to be expected for $30 earbuds.You can't raise/lower volume, skip tracks, or bring up Google assistant using the earbuds buttons. You can however answer/hang up calls, and play/pause your music using the earbuds button gestures.I don't how good battery life is, haven't had them long enough to test that function yet.Overall conclusion, if you don't mind having all the different gestures for media directly on the earbuds but want some decent quality earbuds these would be a great pick for most people. However, I most definitely think the manufacturer could have included at least media gestures at this price point. Other than that gripe, they fit my ears well and sound quite good. I think collectively these missing features at this price point based on what I have gotten in the past for even cheaper, is what induced me to give them a 3 star review. If these buds had media gestures like my old pair did, I would say at this price they would have definitely gotten five stars out of me.Hopefully this slightly detailed review will be helpful to somebody! Oh, and didn't mention, they are quite loud! Which is a good thing because you can always turn them down.

title: Very Meh
body: Initially I really liked these and attempted to use them frequently but started running into problems that made me think twice about ever taking them anywhere or using them again.The earbuds are actually kind of weighty in the wrong place. The way they rest, angled up, causes them to become dislodged from my ears pretty easily. I tried this with all the different earbud fittings and still ran into problems due to the less-than ergonomic design. Also changing out the earbud fittings is a pain in the behind.Another thing I realized was that even when not in use, the battery is constantly discharging into powering the earbuds. This wouldn't be an issue with frequent use but if left alone for a day or two the battery stores become depleted and then the actual earbud battery starts to discharge as well.You'll have it fully charged and ready one day, need to bring them along to a workout 2 to 3 days later, and the entire battery stores are dead in 20 minutes. I can't just have these charged preemptively for any given time I need them. I found myself constantly charging them, just to have them totally dead and discharged without even using them a few days later, which can't be good for the overall battery life.Okay marks for sound. Bass was pretty flat and treble sounds at higher volumes tended to produce rattle. Thankfully due to the noise cancelling properties you won't have to boost the volume up too loud to retain listening quality of whatever you have playing. Sound quality is just okay. The frequent slip of a misfitting earbud kind of ruins that though.I think I learned a lesson by getting these. If you do so much as jog in place or power walk, these are going to fall out on you and you'll lose them down a storm drain or worse. If you're relaxing in a seated position and just need something to drone out noise, you'll find yourself constantly adjusting them but they'll stay in place mostly. You're going to constantly have to charge them every night even if you're not using them, so about as often as you put your phone on the charger. Otherwise, you'll put them on a shelf somewhere and forget them completely after going back to use your betting fitting, better battery life, over-ear headphones like how I did.If I wanted earbuds for being active, I'd look elsewhere.

title: Decent sound, but broken after 4 uses
body: UPDATE 4/28/2023Was looking at my reviews and noticed I never updated this one. So, here we go...I submitted a warranty replacement to TOZO for these T10's, and they honored it without requiring me to send back the broken ones, so I am changing this review from 1 to 3 stars.While waiting for the warranty, I went ahead and bought TOZO T6's, and I've been using them about 3 times a week at the gym for the last 9 months. The T6's are smaller and lighter so I never went back to the warrantied T10's and they're just sitting in a drawer waiting for the T6's to break, which hasn't happened yet. I took out the T10's the compare to the T6's, and I must say the T10's are incredibly uninspiring. I would recommend T6 over T10 if you don't need the independent earbud connectivity of the T10.ORIGINAL REVIEW 6/8/2022Bought these for wireless music and podcasts from my phone in the gym. Sound is only acceptable--low on bass and the clarity isn't amazing, but for the low price for true wireless I felt the sound was good enough. One side would briefly cut out occasionally if my phone was in the wrong pocket, but again not a dealbreaker.However, I did have a major problem leading me to this poor review: On my 4th use, the right earbud does not charge properly. Instead of a solid red charging light, I either get no light at all, or a red light that blinks off briefly every 10 seconds. This condition is not addressed in the manual. Then, when the right earbud wills itself to turn on it all, it doesn't play sound from the phone; instead, it makes awful, deafeningly loud screeching like an out-of-tune radio, as well as ear-piercing dog-whistle-type squealing. The reset process does not work on the right earbud as it is clearly broken and not functioning as expected.I've just submitted a warranty claim as, of course, I'm beyond the 30-day return window and the 90-day Amazon coverage. Based on my research, I doubt the company will do anything with my warranty claim, so I'm leaving this review here in hopes Tozo will see it and do something about this situation. If not, this one-star review will remain and I'll be looking for a new set of earbuds.

title: Fantastic sound, but disappointing battery life
body: I was looking for an upgrade in buds, so came here for reviews for buds in the $50 range.Most reviewers pointed out how great the sound is, and this is very true. The bass is rich, but not boomy, and the mid-ranges and highs come through beautifully. I was really shocked at how good the sound quality is, compared to the cheaper buds I was replacing. The sound is stellar for music. I use the buds for music, youtube vids, and podcasts, so I'm a heavy earbud user. I use the buds at the gym, or just doing daily tasks, like food shopping. Again, the sound quality is excellent.The buds pair, and connect to my phone almost instantly. When charging from the case only, about a 15 min. charge will get you about another hour of use in my experience.The different sized tips for the buds should make a good fit easy. The buds fit snugly, and won't fall out even when you're active, like jogging.Now for the cons-I get random signal drops, mostly in the left bud. Sometimes, I get a crackling sound.The range isn't that great. The distance from my living room to kitchen isn't more than 15' but I've suffered signal drops at that distance. I've suffered total signal drops when my phone is in my pocket.On the 2nd day of use, the buds became unpaired, and I spent HOURS getting them paired again. I used the method on the Tozo site, and it didn't work. If there's a PURPLE light, my buds don't have it. I tried various resetting methods, then simply left the buds in the case for a few hours, and they paired properly. WHAT A HASSLE!My biggest complaint is the battery life. The description says 3.5 hours. In 3 separate tests, after the buds are totally discharged, then charged via AC power, the maximum time I can use the buds is almost exactly 2.75 hours, max. I find this really unacceptable.If you want great sound for an affordable price, these buds may be for you. If you're a heavy user like me, they may not. I bring my old buds with me when I go to the gym, as a backup, should the Tozos die, which they invariably do. I get at least 10 hours from my cheaper buds.

title: Good value, but not great
body: I bought these because AirPods Pros wouldn't stay in my ear and I wanted to give these a try given all of the positive reviews. While it's not fair to compare $200 AirPods to these $30 earbuds, I will use the AirPod Pros as a means to compare incase others are in the same situation.The TOZO earbuds sound alright, although I think they have too much bass and high-end frequencies. To make them sound more balanced (and more like the AirPods Pros), I used the Spotify EQ to boost the mid frequencies and almost eliminate the upper and lower frequencies. The result sounded more neutral, but muddy and lacking the clarity of the AirPods. However, for $30, I can't really complain on the sound.As for fit, these stay in my ears unlike the AirPod Pros. The only situations where they do not stay are if they get very wet (running in a downpour, sweat from running 10k+ in summer heat). Once one earbud comes loose from water/sweat, it is very hard to keep it from falling out again.The issues I have with the TOZO earbuds are that:1) When connected to my MacBook Pro, the sound sucks. When I listen to the same song through Spotify on my MacBook Pro with the same streaming quality and EQ selected as on my iPhone, the sound on my MacBook is much worse and distorted.2) The left earbud will randomly glitch and cut on and off for about a second. This will happen about four or five times on a 30 minute run. Even when sitting still, the left earbud will still randomly cutout for a second. My understanding is the bluetooth connection is between my phone and the right earbud, and the left earbud connects to the right earbud to get sound. So, the issue is between the earbuds, not the bluetooth connection to my phone.3) The microphone is terrible and unusable. Also, it will not work with Zoom despite some other reviews saying it will. I tried on both Windows and Mac, and neither would allow me to use these over Zoom.In summary, for $30, you are buying decent sounding earbuds that will stay in your ears. If you want anything else, pay more for a better product.

title: BAD UPDATE.....Sound quality is not what was advertised. Touch button is a problem. NEW UPDATE
body: MANUFACTURER DID RESPOND. Mailed me a new pair for free and I'll update again as I use them. 3 stars for now.MANUFACTURER WON'T EVEN RESPOND FOR SUGGESTIONS. My guess, is they know they can't fix their JUNKJUNKJUNKWorst decision I've made. These are junk. Less that 50 days after buying them the right ear piece volume has decreased by 80%.I've tried the factory reset instructions. I don't even get the proper flashes to preform it.No way to flash the firmware which can help with devices. Even flashing the same firmware can fix issues.No way to even do that here.JUNKJUNKJUNKBattery life is good. Love the charging case and works as advertised. The fit is bulky but fit tight once you figure out what direction they should be turned. You can use one at a time.The mic does work but with a echo, like you're in an empty room(well, not that bad but maybe a somewhat empty room). They do pick up background noise but I'd expect that. That's not their selling pointI have two problems. The first is the touch button on the ear piece. It's really easy to brush it and pause what your listening to. All you have to do is gently swipe to pause\play. It SUCKS. The second problem I have is the the sound quality. It also has a hollow sound to it. It has good bass and sound, i suppose, but with a hollow sound if that makes senseAs of this review, right here on Amazon, are the JLab Go Air True Wireless Bluetooth Earbuds + Charging Case which sell for $30 but are on sale for $18 sold by JLab and shipped Amazon.They sound a lot better. I wish I would have waited, to be honestI lost my old pair of JLabs and saw the great reviews for these and took a chance. I got the T10s for $20 so I wasn't too disappointedMy advice is get the JLabs if you can. The Go Air. NOT the Go POP. ..IDK how the Go Pop sound so I can't recommend them. They are the cheaper model, although as of right now they aren't on sale and are one $1 more than the normally $30 Air Go. The Go Air on sale $25 or less is a pretty good buy

title: Not bad, but not built for use while exercising
body: Edit 7/17/21I was contacted by the manufacturer and sent a pair of their newer model T6 earbuds, which appear to be a redesigned version of these. The exterior part of the earbud has been slimmed down and there are even more ear fittings for sizing, both of which are great improvements over the older model. The case has been slimmed down as well, making it immensely more portable, and I loved the charging options available. The touch tap to pause, skip or reverse is a nice feature, but takes a little effort for skip and reverse, as it is more incline to pause unless you tap just right.Again as below, maybe it is my ears, but I did feel like I just couldn't keep them in my ears. I found that I wasn't adjusting them as much as the older model, but listening while sitting, walking, crocheting, all pretty tame activities, still had me adjusting them every little bit. The more vigorous the activity, the more messing with it I had to do. Walking for exercise, I found every time I had to adjust, no matter how careful I was about where I held them and how hard, the tap function would pause what I was listening to, making me need to tap again to re-start, jostling it slightly again.Overall, they are a definite improvement on the older model, but still not for any vigorous activity.Original ReviewMy husband bought these for me to wear on my morning runs. It may be my ears, but none of the guards worked to keep these things in my ears. I wore them for the first time while doing some house cleaning, not quite as jostling as running, but active nonetheless. I was adjusting them repeatedly, changing the guard sizes, trying again to no avail. I was worried they’d fall out while i was cleaning the toilet. It could be that the weight of the outer part is just too much to be offset by the part that inserts into the ear. They work well if you’re not trying to do anything other than sit or walk. There have been a couple little glitchy type things with the sound quality, but nothing repetitive and ultimately not a big deal. They work surprisingly well at noise cancelling.

title: Charging issue
body: After having these for two years, I can say with confidence that I would not recommend these to anyone.Pros:- I've used them in the shower and they work well.- Good sound quality most of the time.- Easy to connect from one device to another (I use them with my phone and laptop).Biggest Cons:- The battery life is very short, three hours maximum. I listen to everything at a low volume because I'm sensitive to sound so I don't know what the deal is.- When I turn off the buds and place them in the container to charge, they turn back on ON THEIR OWN after charging, and then it connects to my device. If I'm nearby and it finishes charging, whatever I'm listening to just gets cut off and continues playing silently because the sound transfers to the buds. If I'm not nearby and they don't connect to any of my devices, they still turn on ON THEIR OWN and waste battery sitting in the charger waiting for me to pick them up again. When I do pick them up again, they've charged themselves several times in the case and used up all that power just sitting there even though I turned them off before putting them in the charging case. It sits on my desk. Nothing moves it. It charges, finishes, turns itself on unprompted, uses up the batteries just sitting there, charges itself again on its own, finishes, waits again, and repeats until the charging case is also out of power and I need to charge it as well, which takes a long time.Cons, but not the biggest deal:- The voice that says "low battery, please charge" only comes in through one of the buds. So if I'm wearing only the left because I need my right free, I don't hear the notification and whatever I'm listening to just cuts off without warning.- If I'm wearing one bud and I have the other in my pocket because I need the other ear free, they stop working properly. The sounds become distorted and there are interference sounds. I have to place the bud I'm not using on a table for this to stop, but then I'm not able to walk far from it which is not ideal.

title: Kept losing its charge and now won't charge at all
body: I bought these only a year ago and already need to replace them. I am happy with a lot of aspects of the product, but unfortunately I have been having issues with the battery for months. The description says the earbuds can play music for four hours and that the charging case holds an extra 14 hours. I can't confirm that. I have been using the earbuds for about an hour once a week (gym day). After fully charging the case, I would get only two gym visits out of the earbuds before both the earbuds and the case were out of juice. That means that I only got two hours of play time out of these before the battery was completely empty. It seems that the earbuds and the case just lose their charge when they are not being in use; if I let them sit around unused for two weeks, they will lose their charge, even if I don't use them at all. Is that normal? It's pretty annoying. But anyways, even if this is somehow normal, a few days ago, the right earbud just completely stopped charging. I checked the contacts and they aren't dirty or broken, so I assume that something is broken either inside the right earbud or inside the charger case. I was hoping I would get more use out of these than just a year. I did not even use the earbuds that much, and I never used them in the rain or otherwise exposed them to anything that would be a danger to electronics. Disappointed in the durability.Other than that, I liked these earbuds okay. They do not stay in my ears very well, but that is the case for virtually all earbuds I have ever had–must be something with my ears, rather than with the earbuds. They always connected to my phone well, the sound quality was very good, as was the volume and the noise cancellation. So it's really not a bad product other than that it already stopped working and that the case would not hold a charge long enough for my purposes.I would fully charge the case, then would use the earbuds for an hour at the gym once a week, then the third week, I would notice that not only the headphones were out of juice but also the charging case.

title: Good sound, bad buds
body: For the price, you truly can't complain with these bluetooth earbuds. Their light, sleek design looks nice and the sound quality is surprisingly great! But these buds are far from perfect. Let's start with some of the cons first.CON: There is a noticeable delay in audio when synced with my laptop, watching videos. These buds are not great for YouTube or Netflix as the delay is about a full second if not more. This is not an issue if you're listening to music but highly distracting otherwise.CON: These are PAINFUL after long sessions. I wore these for a 4 hour flight and I was in serious pain. The included pads for the earbuds are just cheap. You can probably get away with replacing the pads with something more comfortable but I decided against it.CON: Probably the most insignificant con is that the built in mic on these is pretty poor. Let's be honest though, if you're getting buds like these for the mic, you're wasting your money.PRO: Surprisingly good audio! Bass is not as much as I'd like but it's definitely present. I listen to a lot of Rock and Metal and these earbuds do fine with harsh guitar tones and bass drum action.PRO: Connection process is pretty straightforward and easy. You can even pair only 1 earbud exclusively if you want to just listen to audio in one ear. I wish I could modify the voice prompts but that is a very small nitpick.PRO: Case wireless charges the buds no problem but also has a Micro USB port should you wish to charge them wired. Both work perfectly fine and a nice LED indicator inside the case lets you know the charge level.PRO: Long battery life, on that aforementioned 4 hour flight, I still had above 60% battery life left on the earbuds. They'll certainly last longer than you can wear them!All in all, these are great for the price. The pads on the earbuds definitely caused the lower stars as I work long hours and need something comfortable, but as a cheaper alternative, these buds are great.

title: Decent sound, pairing ok, blah battery life. (Updates in Review)
body: ***** Update Dec 22 2021 - Still only using 1x bud at a time (I work and listen to content while editing etc. and like to have one ear free)... Right Earbud charged all night, now battery only lasting just over 3hr. Left earbud lasted just about 6hrs still before battery warning with off/on use.****Update left channel bud only (reports) 70% charge after days/weekend of charging. (See pics)***Update: Purchased 12/2019 now 09/2021 and the left earbud only lasts for 4hrs off and on use maybe 2.5hr constant time overall with it sill saying 60-70% left. Then minutes later it will give a low battery warning at 30% then only lasts for 20 seconds more, then shows 20% and more audio low battery warnings... 10 seconds later, dies. I usually listen to podcasts etc. one ear at a time so I still have the Right option but that left one is pretty bad with overall battery life/battery indicator reliability.Purchased a pair of these in December 2019. Have you stopping on at least a couple of days a week for that time. Call me the quietest volume or maybe one or two notches up. Never half or full power... I see a trend that the battery used to last about 6 to 7 hours, then 6 hours, then 5 to 6 hours... After about 8 months of regular use, they now get about 4.5?I will get the "low battery charge now" 🎧 voice over warning at that point. Even though the connection states 60, 70, 80% battery left? ... If I don't charge them and ignore the voice it just repeats until it shuts off about a minute later.So... Is the battery dead? Is it not? Glitch? ... Anyway I usually listen to podcasts, YouTube instructional videos, music etc etc. Sound quality is decent, the fit is pretty decent, for the price everything seems fine but that battery life is pretty bad now. I just alternate from left to right because I only listen to one ear at a time anyway (to hear around me while working).But if you're needing both ears to work for more than six to eight hours +? ... Nope.

title: Old ones great new ones....
body: I really appreciate the build, they don't feel cheap, the case doesn't seem cheap. Apparently it uses magnets to help keep it shut and it has an actual hinge instead of a plastic strip as a hinge which would eventually break. So far so good. Pairing is really easy. Range varies and you can get drop outs at 50 feet. Battery life is about 4 hours depending on volume levels. I like the case also charges it, it has it's own battery. I suspect it can recharge the ear pieces 4 times before needing charged itself. It comes with extra rubber ear pieces that are different sizes depending on ear size. The bigger the better, really helps with bass. Not as much bass as I'm used to but it has plenty of volume. I don't even crank my phone all the way up and it's plenty loud.UPDATE I ordered what I thought would be an extra pair, this new version has changed drastically. The sound has no bass and is extra tinny. My previous set was nicer. You could fit them in your ear more and get better bass, these do not get better, very disappointed. The one feature that I haven't decided is a good feature or not is there is no longer a button, you just tap and it stops and starts, that's a plus, the negative is you can't touch them to adjust them in your ear without shutting them off so you can't hear the adjustments cause it shut off. The carrying case feels like it got cheapened but I understand why. Another negative, the older version would identify left an right as you put them on but I understand why now. You can put in just one if you want, the older version doesn't allow this, they both have to be activated so that's a plus for the new version. Another plus, the new version uses USB-C, I didn't like the USB-C in the beginning cause it meant getting all new chargers for the car but I really like it now, it's a much more durable connection and it's reversible. So the only reasons for taking off two stars are serious lack of bass and being very tinny, however everyone has different tastes in what sounds good.

title: bought for swimming
body: First off, I knew from the previous reviews that once these go under water, they won't work as the connection can't travel through water (I'm sure I'm paraphrasing here) so I was prepared for that. Considering I keep my head above water in what one can only observe as a spastic version of a breast stroke, I thought it'd be ok. The fit is fine although after a few laps they start to wiggle their way out a little and I have to reposition - but that might be due to the fact that my cheeks are puffing/deflating constantly, causing my ears to move up/down (yeah, I swim weird). I have to put my phone at the exact half way mark of my lap pool so the distance isn't too far to pick up but no biggie. I realized it was still cutting out when I got close to the outer range unless I put my phone on a chair or something a little more elevated. problem fixed. My biggest issue is the sensitivity to the controls on the buds themselves. Holy smokes is this a pain in the wrong place. the moment the slightest 'wave' or splash of water hit the side of my head, the audio would set to an auto increase till it reaches it's maximum or decrease till I can't hear anything or stop or pause or advance. Of course as my workout continues and I get more tired (read 'lazy'), my head gets a little lower in the water and if anyone else is swimming in the pool causing turbulence it's a no-go. I spent more time swimming back to my phone to get things reset (because I couldn't get the buttons to undo the action) than I did swimming. I did find a solution, albeit not for everyone. I put on a light headband that covers my ears when I have them in. This cushions the splashing water and for the most part, stops the problem. The only issue now is if I have to adjust said headband, even it sliding over the ear buds can cause them to respond with some undesirable action. And the headband isn't a great look but considering I'm already flopping in the water like a dying fish, aesthetics are clearly not my biggest concern.

title: Has a few bugs that need ironed out...
body: I really enjoy this product! I’ve been looking around for a while for wireless earbuds that don’t make my ears sore and these are perfect! I am still adjusting to setting them in my ears correctly so they don’t fall out, but I’m hoping time will fix that. These earbuds were a bit bigger than I expected, but so far they have excelled. One feature I am glad of is the extra ear caps they send along that are different sizes. My ears are small, which causes most earbuds to not fit right. The smallest ones I found are perfect for me! I will definitely buy from this company again!Feb. 17, 2020 Update:I was extremely excited to try these wireless earbuds and wanted to try them out for a few months before giving my final opinion. It's been about 2 1/2 months since I received them and there are a few things that bug me.1 - They have great sound and block out noise, but will randomly cut out even when they are less then a foot away from the device they are connected to.2 - When I place them in their charging case, they will turn back on themselves and reconnect to my device ,,,they will literally not turn off, even when I turn them off in the case (the Left one always turns back on if not both) - which is A. obnoxious and B. I'm never sure if they're completely charged.3 - Occasionally, one earbud will die quicker than the other even though they are both fully charged at the start of use.4 - I also retract my comment of them fitting my ears. Yes, the ear caps work perfectly, but the earbuds themselves do not stay in my ears. I've used them while jogging, washing dishes (yes I know I'm tempting fate), and simply watching a movie on my computer and they will slowly slip out of my ears. It definitely seems that the warmer your body temp, the faster they try to abandon ship.All in all, I'm not as positive that I'd buy from this company again next time.

title: Mixed feeling on this product.
body: Pro-Sound. For the earbud under $50 and compared with higher end earbuds, this is good enough for every day use. Well.. being really exact not every day use and it'll be explained in cons.-Battery Life. I had no issue on batter life on both earbuds and case. They are exactly as advertised I had no problem on battery running out during my use. To tell you the truth, max I've used this for was about 2 hours and a half.-Phone Calls. People I was talking to was able to hear background noise but they had no issue hearing me.-Holds steady in my ears. Comes with 4 different sizes so it should fit you find unless you have ear canal as big as a cave.Con-Connection. At home, fine. At shower, fine(IPX7 is true). Out for a walk/run, fine. Working out at the gym, disaster. Left ear is losing connection every second on and off. It kind of drives me crazy as I was focused on lifting. It sounds just fine only with right earbud on but hello this is a pair of earbud, not one single piece. I wanted to find a solution so I've had my wife to use it on crowded area and she had same issue.-Customer Support. I've advised them what issue I had. They asked me to reset the earbuds and I've done so and issue was still the same. I asked them what the next step is and they've gone silent until now. That being said, I decided to just return afterwards.Bottom Line is 3 Stars overall rating.1 Star on Material quality.5 Star on Sound quality.5 Star on Battery life.What turned me off was the connection of the left earbud.In case if anyone is wondering what functions this one have. Both right and left earbud have buttons that clicks and have same function. Power on/off, answer call, reject call, hang up, stop and play music. There is no rewind, fast forward, skip track, volume control. For the price point, I think it's fair.

title: I didn't get what I ordered but I'm still using them. They are fine for the price.
body: I paid a little extra for the white model and they sent me the black color.Also I bought these based off of a YouTube review where they compared the older model without touch pads to the newer model with controls on the buds. I thought I was buying the older model based off of the posting. I wanted the older model because the reviewer said he liked them better.The battery life is fine and I can recharge them from the case a few times before I need to charge the case.One complaint I do have is that the charge indicator on the case doesn't seem accurate, the case suddenly dies at two dots of charge.Another problem I have had is that the right earbud will discharge completely if i let them charge while the case is on the charger. This doesn't happen every time but often i think i am fully charged and ready to go but the right earbud is completely dead and I have to pull it out of the case and reinsert it so it will charge up.The connectivity is sometimes problematic and I have to fight with them a bit, plugging them in and out of the charger several times to get them to go to pairing mode or holding down the touch pads turning them on and off.The most confusing thing that has ever happened with this product is that I once plugged in my earbuds to the charge case thinking that they would turn off like they always do but instead they stayed connected, hours went by missing calls and texts because the earbuds stayed connected even though they were in the charger.Don't get me wrong, I use these everyday and am enjoying them for the most part. My main frustration is that they sent me the wrong color. The other issues I imagine come from the fact that these are cheap earbuds. How they make these and then can sell them on amazon for this price while making a profit...they must be extremely cheap to produce and as we all know we get what we pay for.

title: What’s the point of hands free if Callers can’t hear you on the other end
body: Ok so I would have given these earbuds two stars if it weren’t for the insane amount of time they stay charged. I have a job where I’m on my phone most of the day and these lasted a good 6 hours of continuous use. When I wasn’t actively on the phone I was listening to music or a movie while I worked. Sound quality was pretty good . Pairing was a breeze and I never had any connection issues.Now for the cons, which may be two of the biggest reasons why I most likely will return them . I’ve been on the search for the perfect pair of ear buds for my needs. These came so close !!’ I would sacrifice a not so stellar battery life for these two issues to have not been issues.First, like other reviewers have said the ear buds are too big for the ears (I guess unless you have big ears) mine did come with different size earpieces and none of the sizes made the earbuds fit any better. It felt like constantly that they were going to fall out of my ears, which at one point the right one did while I was cooking and fell into a pan of chicken right into the chicken drippings. Thank God it still worked after that. The other issue which is a non negotiable for me was every caller on the other end of the phone said they had trouble or could not hear me. Which leads me to think that the fact that the earbuds being too big is what plays a part in this ; by not fitting properly in the ears. When I would carefully put my fingers to the edges of the earbuds and hold it into my ear, the caller would say they could hear me better.As a crisis intervention nurse and Having a job where I’m on the phone most of the day it is so important for people to be able to hear me and for me to have the convenience of hands free.I could even live with the issue of the earbuds being too big . Does anyone have a fix for the sound issue ? If not I’m afraid I will return these .

title: Not as good as the T6
body: I had the T6 for about two months before I lost them. I replaced them with the T10 because they appeared newer and "better". However I have found them to be worse, but only mildly.The case for the T10 is less convenient and less ergonomically shaped. The T10 also has a button rather than a haptic touch sensor built into the earbud to stop sound or answer phone calls. The haptic touch was better, the only exception being the shower. When in the shower, the water tended to activate the touch feedback and stop the sound on the T6, but not on the T10.On the T10, making this a button makes pushing it more deliberate, with fewer error readings. Also, the Bluetooth doesn't seem to function as far with the T10's as opposed to the T6's. I was very surprised at how well the Bluetooth stayed connected with the T6, and I have to make sure I take my phone around with me whenever I use the T10's.Overall these are still good earbuds, and they are a real improvement over technology from a few years ago. If you want some good earbuds, these are really good candidate, in a good price range. If you're anal-retentive about getting the exact best deal for the price point, I'm not sure what to tell you. Who can know without buying 50 pairs of earbuds and evaluating all of them? They're mostly all good for normal people and very few good enough for the connoisseur.I'm pretty happy with these, but if I could go back I'd spend a few dollars more and get the T6's again.Update: My wife took these, and feels much the same. They don't always connect easily, or won't connect when using only one at a time to listen to podcasts, talks or while driving. That little bit of hassle can be annoying for her. My 17 year old daughter used them for a day, and then bought expensive apple earbuds. It's nice not to have expenses huh? She wanted better noise cancellation.

title: Probably a better option out there
body: To start, the headphones do not have a skip track function built in. Given that I use my headphones mostly for listening to music while working out, I cannot recommend them with such a massive oversight. I bought the headphones so that I wouldnt have to keep my phone on me while working out, but these don't solve that problem.The sound quality on the headphones I think is adequate. I have headphones ranging from 25 dollar wired skull candy's to 350 dollar bose noise cancelling headphones and I can say that these headphones, realitively speaking, are the weakest in terms of sound quality. To be more specific, the headphones handle edm and rap very well, but fall very short when trying to handle cymbols and highs present in rock/metal. They seem to clip those sounds.Call quality is alright, but unless I speak louder than I normally do on phone calls, the other party tells me it sounds like I have a rag over my phone. Maybe I just speak too quietly though.The headphones certainly come with a wide range of gummy sizes so I'm certain you will find the correct size. I do find that the headphones slip out of my ears about 25 percent very quickly. This further decreases the sound quality as the bass and mids require a good seal. I've never felt like the headphones were going to fall out even while working out, but I find myself constantly trying to push them back in all the way.The build quality of the headphones and charging pack is very high and putting them back in to the charging pack is very tactile and satisfying. The headphones don't fit very flush in my ears giving me a sort of Frankenstein look, but that is minor in my opinion.Overall, the headphones are built well and produce adequate sound. However, for the money I feel I could have found another pair with more standard features.

title: Acceptable but problematic... UPDATED
body: UPDATED: The first pair I bought was ok... that review is below. I replaced them because the right side died after almost 2 years service (which I find perfectly acceptable BTW). The new "updated" version has more features like volume and shuffle control which is awesome! However, they don't rely on a button membrane like the original ones. These are induction sensitive like your cell phone screen and ANY water drop on them causes them to lock into volume changes or track changes so being in a drizzle angers them and they do goofy stuff. Forget about using them in the shower. Just the steam sets them off. They sound amazing and they connect super easy as well as having a good amount of distance from the connected device without dropping off but the inability to use them in a wet environment makes them difficult. They are waterproof as long as you are not using them in a wet environment. Bummer that I bought two sets because I now have the same problem with two sets. I am not returning them as they ARE useable... I just have to think about the environment they are being used in.This is the first pair of independent wireless earbuds I have owned and I am pretty impressed. The sound quality is quite good for the tiny package and they get quite loud as well. I love the waterproof part as I bought them for chilling in the hot tub. Have used them in the shower as well to try out the waterproof element of them. The con to them, and there is really only one I can find, is that the left side will often drop out. It is losing the signal from the right side. Doesn't take much at all for this to happen and it will often do so without any obstruction between the left and right sides except your head. Get your hand or arm or something close and it's gone. All things considered though, a very nice product. I love the charging case.

title: TL; DR. They're cheap with lots of flaws. But they're cheap.
body: I already own a pair of Jabra Elite Active 65t true wireless earbuds but... I stupidly left them at home for a long trip abroad and ended up buying these on Amazon as cheap temporary replacements. Definitely no chance of them being more than that.Cons:- Biggest flaw: Buds are a bit bulky shapewise (not so much weightwise). They don't sit securely in the ear, and I have had them come off quite easily despite trying the different silicone tips and twisting them in at whatever possible angle. This is a big issue with wireless buds that would make them so much easier to lose.- Sound is mediocre. The seal because of the unsecure fit already leaks in quite a bit of external noise which doesn't help. They can get quite loud but then you can really hear where it suffers in terms of overall tinny sound, poor extension on both ends, and muddied separation. They're ok, I guess for podcasts and dialogue-driven content but not general music listening or shows/movies with lots of dynamic range.- The buttons right on each earbud are terribly hard to press. You end up jamming the pods into your ear to increase volume or switch songs. I end up just using controls on my phone.- No auto pause when you take either earbud out.Pros:- Being able to wirelessly charge the case is a nifty feature. Works on any Qi charging mat + my Galaxy S10e's wireless powershare feature.- Pairing was pretty easy and mostly consistent. Link was also pretty OK but haven't tried walking more than 8 feet from the signal source or into separate rooms.- Cheap. For the price I paid, I wasn't expecting them to permanently replace my Jabra 65ts, but I can't escape the feeling I could have found better deals (e.g. not as cheap but with better fit, design, or sound quality) if I shopped around more.

title: Audio skips / drops
body: These headphones are ok for walking, and extremely frustrating to use while running. I originally had these synced to my phone which I carried in my hand while running. If my rhythm altered at all while running it would interrupt the audio connection and either skip or disconnect entirely. I recently purchased a Fitbit Ionic, and the earbuds did the same exact thing when paired with that device too. Although the idea of having wireless headphones was appealing, the connection is unstable regardless of which device I use. I have to stiffen my arm to prevent skipping / drops from music. I tested the connection by flexing my wrists up / down and waving my arm around with both my phone and Ionic, and the motions confirmed that is what was interrupting my wireless connection. I removed the device from my settings on both my phone and Ionic, then reinstalled it, but still had the same issues. The phone and earbuds were also fully charged. The really disappointing thing is I bought 2 pairs of these, and my husband's does the exact same thing when using the bluetooth feature.The case is extremely convenient and pretty heavy-duty. The water resistance is also a nice perk. Another thing I like about these headphones is the case automatically charges the headphones when you put them back, plus there are magnets that hold the earbuds within the case. You can also visually see the charge progress and how much charge is left within the case even when it's closed because of the LED indicators.I would wear these in the gym, and like I previously said, they do ok while walking if you do not walk briskly or alter movements unexpectedly. I'm currently searching for new wireless earbuds that I can sync to my phone and Ionic and not have to worry about the skipping or drops with the audio connection.

title: Good value but things to consider:
body: UPDATE: Something is "different" with the newer version. I ordered a pair summer June 2020 and they were great. After almost a year, the charge didn't hold as long, and I bought a second pair May 2021. The second pair I got was not the same. (Full disclosure - I use my buds for HOURS every single day)Straight out of the box - pair 2020 last 6 hours, recharged hundreds of times before loss of battery endurance was noticeable. The charge case was usb-micro and had a protective plug to protect the usb port.Pair 2021 lasted only 4 hours, survived only a few months of recharge before losing endurance (while also being rotated w/the 1st pair) The case is USB-C, and has no plug to waterproof the usb port. Also, the buds from purchase 2020 would not fit in the 2021 case (although the reverse does work).BEWARE: It would be fair to be skeptical of some reviews and the overall rating. The seller offers "warranties" and free gifts if proof of positive review is provided.The over-all quality/price-value is high, but I would not recommend if this is your first experience with wireless headphone technology. The instructions provided aren't overly detailed (but contrary to many other's complaints- the manual does include a table for basic commands including going forward/back, managing calls, etc). Unfortunately- everything besides the pictograph of instructions is "translated-to-english" and this makes understanding the written instructions very difficult. The website is the same. Even though I would rate my tech-savvy on the higher end of the spectrum, it took way too long to pair with my first device (phone) and I have been unable to pair with other devices. If you have owned similar products and are familiar with the basics, it would be a good purchase.

title: Earbud tips, Sound Quality, and Buzzing
body: This is a paraphrasing of an email I sent to Tozo Support. Unfortunately, my time with these earbuds was probably not able to capture the full experience of Tozo, but I will write a full review whenever I have given the earbuds another chance.--I recently purchased the Tozo T10 Earbuds, however, I am currently unsatisfied with them and thought I would contact the support email first.1) Earbud TipsAfter following the wearing instructions as closely as possible (including searching online for other possible solutions) and after trying every single earbud tip size available, it has come to my attention that nothing has worked for me.The seal hurts my ears, and no matter how I try the earbuds become unbearable after around 15-20 minutes (not enough for my full workout or anything reasonable). The sound quality is also compromised by the improper fitting.2) Slight High-pitched Buzzing in Right EarbudAs the title of this section would imply, the right earbud has a slight high-pitched buzzing sound in the background. There has been no damage to them from what I can tell, and I have tried resetting them following the procedure in the manual multiple times. I have even tried turning off the sound features from my phone in case that was it, but unfortunately it wasn't. My guess is that it has something to do with the Bluetooth connection, but it still could be many other things. Either way the sound is extremely unsettling and I don't want to have it constantly ringing in my ear.--Tozo Support has contacted me back to solve the issue with the buzzing, and I will continue to try to solve the ear-tip issue. For the mean time, due to the speedy customer service response, I'm giving them 3 stars.

title: They're overall just fine. Definitely worth $20.
body: TLDR: They're fine; neither amazing nor terrible. For $20 I have no significant complaints.I've been using these for about 2 months now. I jog every morning with them, and walk with my dog in the afternoon. It's quiet neighborhood, so I can't speak much to noise cancellation.They work fine and charge well most of the time. I've noticed a significant drop off in the battery life of the case though. In the beginning, the 4 blue lights that indicate charge level would stay at 4 through several headphone charge cycles. Now from a full charge, it usually goes down to 3 or even 2 lights during a single charge. This is after using the headphones for about an hour.The headphones mostly stay in my ears, even during moderate jogging (6 mph pace). Occasionally the left one becomes loose and falls out. I usually check it halfway through my morning jog.They pair easily, but the range is not great. If I'm more than 30 feet from the phone they're paired with, I can count on static, cutting out, or even no sound whatsoever. When I take my phone out of my pocket when I get back home, I tend to leave it in the center of the house now.They have a varied set of controls with taps, double taps, and holding your finger against them, but it is quite bad at differentiating between single and double taps. More than half the time I try to skip a song with a double tap on the right earbud, it pauses instead (the behavior for a single tap). The volume control is a bit confusing, too. It feels like the headphone beeps are delayed from the volume change, so I have a hard time figuring out how much I've raised or lowered the volume, and you don't want to hold too long or it will go all the way in that direction.

title: Good price, lacks good battery life
body: I purchased these back in July and really liked them in the beginning. First of all, I was very impressed with the fit of these and that I didn't feel like they would fall out at all. Some of the other earbuds I've tried just didn't work out and would drive me crazy because of how often they'd fall out.The sound quality is good, and for the price range I'd say great, but it lacks the heavy bass I like for working out.I like the case for them as well, and the fact that I can charge the case and take it with me is great. Unfortunately it's not just great, it's necessary. The battery life on these is definitely not the 4 hours the listing states, and the case provides nowhere near the 14 extra hours they say it does. In the beginning I was able to take my walks and use these the whole time with no problem. Now, I'm lucky if I make it a couple miles before the horrendously annoying "low battery" message starts. On that subject, the message starts at 10% and plays about every thirty seconds. The voice that says it sets off my misophonia so I can't stand it.Putting them in the case to charge is convenient, but they say it gives you 14 extra hours and that's just not been true in my experience. At this point I get one good full charge and then half a charge the second time giving me an extra 4ish hours of use.So for the price I can't say it was a bad deal, I got a few months of good use out of them. But I wouldn't buy these again.Pros- good price, decent sound quality, secure fit, convenient charging caseCons- battery life degrades quickly, case doesn't give as much charge as advertised, low battery message makes me want to cut my own ears off, lacks the deeper base I like

title: Good for everyday use, I think a little over priced though.
body: I still am not sold on these, but I can’t say they’re a bad product.I own everything Apple, but don’t feel I need the apple AirPods. I prefer wired headphones, but needed something wireless for my workouts. These seemed like a good compromise.First off, the sound on these is wonderful. The noise canceling is great. When you have both in and music playing you can’t hear anything else. It’s amazing.My issues comes from pairing.I usually have to go into my phones Bluetooth and manually click to pair these. The EarPods don’t seem to want to pair automatically.You cannot switch from one audio device to another easily, its a manual process. I paired them with my Mac and my IPhone, and it always pairs to the Mac first automatically and then I have to discount it and manually pair it to the iPhone in the settings. I’ve since told it to forget these pods on my Mac, but still doesn’t want to pair automatically with my phone.If you pair the left first, the right won’t pair, so you then must disconnect the left and then pair the right.If you want to use only the right earpod - you still have to remove both pods from their pack, pair both, then place the left back into the pack. Then the right earpod will beep at you for about 30 seconds while it’s trying to repair with the left pod until it gives up.I’ve had some issues of the sound going in and out while only listening to the right earpod.I can’t speak to battery. Seems fine.I think 50 bucks is too much for these. Not really sure I’m going to keep them or not. They’re def. a hassle for the way I want to use them but for everyday, pop in, listen to music, pop out, they are fine.

title: Acceptable for the price.
body: Reasons why I returned mine:- Flashing blue light on the side when on - I only noticed these when I was using them at night and saw my room being lit up at about a 5 second interval. Because the two buds are not paired at the same time, the buds flash independent of one another (not at the same time). I prefer something more subtle and would rather not piss off someone I'm sitting next to when on the plane or train.- Voice over is annoying. On removal of the buds from the charging case, there will be a woman's voice saying that the device has been turned on, right ear connected, left ear connected, etc. Similarly, when turning the devices on/off manually by holding the button on the side, it'll beep and say device on/off. Superunnecessary, things like this should be seamless - just remove it from the case and start the music.- Connection drops out - over the course of about an hour and half walking throughout NYC, the connection dropped off two times. It managed to auto-fix itself without having to do anything, but still a slight annoyance.- No volume control on the buds themselves.- Not too fond of the fit despite trying all bud shapes. You're supposed to turn these at an angle toward the back of the ear but it still didn't feel that secure. It fell out once when walking as I raised my arm and brushed my ear lightly. I can't really compare it to other wireless buds as this was my first pair. The standard corded apple earbuds are still my go to.Good points:- It'll last at least 2 hours per charge as I haven't tested it for longer.- Call quality/mic seem to be okay. I've only tested them with ~a minute long call.

title: After two weeks of use.....my honest thoughts when it comes to comfort, sounds, controls, etc.
body: Hey guys! I purchased these earbuds with two things in mind: noise cancellation and needed great quality for meditations/binaural beat sessions.Product itself: very nice packaging, great look. I ordered the khaki color and it’s ok but with them in it looks like I’m wearing huge hearing aids.Comfort: not very comfortable. There were points in a 30 minute meditation that I couldn’t wait to take them out. I tried every single one of the ear bud cover sizes included and nothing is comfortable. They don’t have that nice shape that molds into ear canal, all round and sometimes they even slipped out of my ears, even after really making sure they were snug. I followed all instructions on inserting them that came with package, etc and nothing.Noise cancellation: I can say this feature is pretty effective...if they were comfortable and actually stayed in it would be better.Sound: pretty decent sound but not great.Controls: wow, this was pretty annoying. I followed the instructions and it’s like “2 taps to forward” etc etc but you have to time the taps just right. If not, you end up just stopping the music, activating Siri, etc. Also, you can’t just turn them off! I tried by following the instructions given and nothing. You have to either put them back in the charging case or disconnect through Bluetooth on your phone all together. There’s no way to just turn the buds off and stick them in your pocket.My next recourse is either returning these and going with another brand or trying to find compatible ear but attachments that will be more comfortable, but I think I’m going to return them once I do more research and have a better replacement in my hands.

title: Good sound quality but they don't fit
body: I had a pair of Tozo-NC9 and when they wore out I bought these to replace them, mostly because of the price difference. I wear my earbuds for hours every day and the battery life on these is great - I've only gotten a "low battery" notification once, and I think it was after about seven hours of continuous use. The sound quality is fantastic, too, ten times better than the NC9. No shakiness or buzzing, crystal clear sounds, the bass is strong without drowning out mids and treble...pretty amazing for the price.The problem is the fit. I've tried all the sizes of the silicone tips (lots of size options, which would be a plus if any of them worked) and I can't get any of them to fit properly in my ears. If I just push them in my ears then the sound is muffled. If I wiggle them around then I can fix the muffled sound, but then they're in danger of falling out. Even when they're in a relatively good position, moving my face at all can muffle the sound again. I want to be able to put my earbuds in and forget that I'm wearing them, but with these I'm constantly aware of how they're fitting and worrying they're going to fall out. The noise cancellation isn't great either, although that might just be personal preference and it's my bad for not noticing they weren't advertised as noise cancelling.Overall I wish that I could like these because the sound quality is so good, but I just can't get them to sit in my ears comfortably the way I could with the NC9 and with most other pairs of earbuds I've owned. They're not bad as a backup pair but I'd recommend trying something else if you're looking for something for frequent use.

title: Good Product Damaged By Terrible Support
body: The TOZO T10 Bluetooth 5.0 Wireless Earbuds were eye-catchy and affordable. After using the product for less than a year, I wish I didn't make the purchase.I was pulled into buying this product because I wanted to try them out. The sound quality is decent. The product itself is compact. And, the buds fit comfortably in your ears.However, the right earbud went completely defective. Then, the right earbud went defective a few weeks later. I've since tossed them out for recycling because there's no need to keep wireless earbuds that aren't working.However, this product is riddled with terrible customer support. My device faced a malfunction and the purchased warranty didn't allow me to contact them to fix the product. SquareTrade advised me to contact TOZO because my product was still under manufacturer warranty.I've contacted TOZO numerous times, I'm still YET to receive a response.To anyone that plans to purchase this product, you should refrain from doing so. It's not worth it. There are other earbuds across Amazon that provides similar, same, or better sound quality with noise cancellation. If TOZO had responded back to my email and provided me with the technical support I wanted, and if they honored their warranty promise to provide support to defective products, then my review would've been different.At this point in time, the product is not worth it.I want to save consumers from rigged businesses and this is a great example of that. Please do not buy the product because the support you receive is not equivalent to the price point of the product.

title: Case loses charge over time
body: I've had these for a little over a year now... For the price they did the job just fine. No complaints on the sound quality (but I'm no audiophile)- I just needed them for running, but I finally lost one of the ear buds like I expected I'd do, and I'm not buying these again.Here's some ways this product needs to improve:#1- The charging case drains itself in 2-3 days now, so whether or not I use the buds at all, I need to charge them twice a week if I want to use them. I expected a longer life than a year before deteriorating. It's a bother to not know if the earbuds are fully charged when I'm about to do a long run. On that note, at fully charged they last for less than 4 hours. Not sure what they advertise it at, but I will be upgrading.#2- The microphone isn't great- I've had a number of complaints from my callers while trying to talk on the phone with them. They do not cancel noise well and I can't call with them jogging or in the wind.3# No touch control to skip songs. You can only press the buds to accept calls and turn them on and off.4# The fit is okay. I'm a petite person and I do find myself having to press them in with some frequency, although they have never fallen out. I will say they are fairly comfortable.5# Bluetooth only good for about 50 ft. Like all Bluetooth devices, walls are its enemy, and I can't be more than a room over without losing connection.For the cheapest pair of wireless buds on the market, I can't diss them too hard. They did fine, but I'll pay an extra 10-20 dollars for some improvements and added features at this point.

title: Great hardware marred by poor firmware design decisions
body: Firstly, the hardware for these earbuds are really great. I love the fact that the button is a clicky push button instead of the capacitive buttons you see on many of these headphones. Good battery life though I haven't tested extensively. The case a a little large to be comfortable in a front pocket but not terrible. Not too heavy, pretty comfortable in the ear and really good sound.The issue with these is that a couple small things ruin the overall experience for me. Firstly, I use 1 earbud at a time a lot, probably more than I use both together. When you first put a single earbud in your ear it connects then searches for the other earbud to connect. The problem with this is that, whoever wrote the firmware for these, decided it would be a good idea to have the earbud beep every time it runs the search for the other earbud. So, every 7 seconds or so for about a minute after you put it into your ear, it beeps at you and there is nothing you can do but wait for it to time out. For someone who puts the buds in and out of his ears many times throughout the day this got old and unacceptable real fast.The other issue is that the LED on the earbuds will blink briefly every 5 seconds as long as they're on, just to remind you that they're there. Why?! If I have these on at night listening to something while my wife falls asleep, these things are practically lighting up the room. Again, nothing you can do about it.It's really a shame since I really love these headphones otherwise. Fix those 2 lines of code and I will gladly use these but, for now, they are getting returned before they drive me insane.

title: Updated Review
body: This is the out the box review. So far everything is there and working fine. The right ear paired a little faster then the left ear. But after a few tries it finally connected. The sound is awesome so far. I had to change out the ear bud pieces because the one that came with them were a little to big and kept falling out. Once I switched the fit was perfect. You can use one ear bud at a time since I mostly use then for talking. So far I haven't had any problems with calls, I've talked to 3 different people and no one said anything about not being able to hear me. Love the fact that it comes with like an keychain attachment or attach it to your bag make it less likely to loose. It does charge on the wireless charger with no problems. I will update the review after a few while since I've only had them one day.Update: So its been a month since I had these ear buds and I must say I'm kind of disappointed with them. The first day they worked like a champ after that nothing but problems. My right ear bud constantly cuts out. You can not have your phone to far away from you. I had my phone in the living room charging while I was in the kitchen washing dishes and they kept cutting out. Sometimes I can be heard other times I can't. Its hit and miss. They fit pretty decent with the small pods but still have to adjust them from time to time while doing Activities. The sound for music is about the best thing with these ear buds. With both ear buds in you can nearly hear your surroundings so that is a plus. This is still a decent product for the price but they need a little more work done to them. Not the worst but not the best.

title: Very nice for the price, but battery doesn't last as long as advertised.
body: Pros:-Sound: quality is much better than expected at this price point-Case: The case is sturdy and very straightforward with magnets that pull the buds into place for charging. Indicator light for the battery level of both the buds, and the actual case itself. Also a nice feature that the case supports wireless charging.-Value: I don't expect much better at this price point, and I actually paid $10.00 more than the current price as I type this.-Ear pieces: Comes with multiple sizes of rubber ear fittings to accommodate all sizes of ears.Cons:-Battery Life: It is advertised that these buds can last up to 4 hours. I've never gotten past 2 hours, and it seems to be getting shorter. Again, I listen to music very softly while I work. This can be a bummer having to charge every 90 minutes or so.-Pairing: To work around my battery problem, I tried using just one bud at a time, while leaving the other in the case, so when one died, I could switch to the other bud. . Unfortunately the right side earbud is the "main" earbud, and if it is not connected the left wont work. As soon as you put the right side earbud back in the case to charge, it disconnects therefore disconnecting the left side as well.Overall:-Decent earbuds for the price. Surprising sound quality and very easy to connect to bluetooth on your phone or computer, I use them on my HP Laptop and my GALAXY Note 10+. Some things to be desired, but at this price point you should have realistic expectations. Now that I know I will get the use out of them, I will likely go ahead and purchase some Galaxy Buds.

title: It's only OK...
body: I have had this for a few weeks now. My final thoughts are kinda mixed. First, the quality and material of the earbuds and the case is great quality for the price. It does not feel that cheap. It is sort of plastic but it is nicely done. The earbuds fit nicely in my ears and comes with different sizes of cushion. So far I have had no problems with pairing with my phone and using a bluetooth adapter on a PC. Battery life is pretty accurate but wish it was longer.Now for the bad, not sure if it is just this earbuds but the sound quality sounds bad and flat. The sound quality sounds no different from a cheap knock off product for like 2 bucks. The bass is sub par and nothing too exciting about. As for the microphone, it isn't that great either. It pretty much catches a lot of additional noise in the background. I used it in a meeting while on a computer and people can hear me type with my low-profile keyboard so I had to turn off the earbuds and just use my phone. It is also very prone to static. Walking around with my phone in my pocket, it cuts in out walking on carpet. Then I completely stop and works fine again until I start walking on carpet.Overall, the price is what you get. I wish it would've been slightly better on sound quality. I had to use an equalizer to make it sound 10x better. If I were to buy another bluetooth earbuds again, I would at least pay $70 for a really good one and call it a day. I wouldn't recommend these to anyone and get something better. Even if it costs a little more. These would only be good for you if you just want low quality sound and want to be wireless.

title: Pretty good for the money.  If it didn’t break!
body: (Updated below)I’d love to have noise canceling ear buds, but can’t wait for the new Bose model which was scheduled to come out this year. So I figured $40 is worth a shot. It took several attempts to find the correct sizing of the ear insert. Still, though I twist the buds so part of my ear is holding it in, I still lose one now and then. Fortunately, I haven’t had any problem finding it. The marking specifying R or L, is almost impossible to read, so I find myself putting them in randomly, then when they start up, they announced themselves as “right channel“or “left channel”. I have solved this problem by putting a tiny piece of scotch tape on one of them, so I can feel which is which. The battery life is good, but if you are wearing these for extended listening, you will be disappointed. When the battery gets down to 20 to 30%, it starts announcing low battery every minute or two, which is annoying enough to force me to stop using them and putting them into the charging case. Would be nice if it wouldn’t do that so often. No volume control on the buds, but it does allow you to answer or hang up the phone calls with a tap. Not disappointed.UPDATE: Okay, it’s been 3 months since getting these. For a full month now, the right ear piece won’t work at all. I’ve tried doing hard resets, discharging, charging, each time and again, but no luck. I’m still using the left piece for podcasts and calls, and that’s working fine, but really this is WAY too early for such a failure. I’m hoping the new Bose (costing over $250) will just make this something I keep in a drawer.

title: Surprisingly-good Earbuds! Don't let the price fool you!
