I actually bought this headphone as a waterproof alternative. For the pool and such. And let me tell you, the sound quality, is actually almost comparable to that of the AirPods. Now I’m not going to lie and say that a sub $100 wireless earbuds are going to sound better than an about $250 one. But the fact that I was able to enjoy my music without any strange frequency crackling or anything like that impressed me. Sound quality is not perfect. But it’s better than others in the market for its price range.Comfort: The case comes with a LARGE variety of ear bud sizes. I tried them all on until I was satisfied with the right one that fit me. It fits snuggly, and doesn’t feel like it’ll fall off.Waterproof: Now at first I was a bit hesitant with testing it’s claims of waterproofing. Different companies have different standards for their tests. But I decided to try it out anyways. For the sweat while running/working out it was able to handle it, no malfunctions or disconnects at all. I’ve used it countless times to listen to music in the shower, shampoo, conditioner, continuous water has been thrown on it, no disconnect. I will mention however sometimes when under the water of a shower, a droplet might hit the back plating on the earbud which is a touch sensor to pause and such. So sometimes my music would pause when the back button plating gets hit with a splash of water. But I just tap it again and it keeps going.I’ve had this I believe for about 2 months now and it’s been working perfectly. Occasionally there will be a split second where the audio cuts, maybe by Bluetooth interference, but that happens very rarely. Overall the engineers that worked on this managed to make a good quality wireless earbud for what I believed was a too good to be true price. I say get it

title: ***SEE EDIT*** Paired to Phone is fine.  Paired to laptop, not so much.
body: Sound quality is fine when paired to my Android device. When paired to my laptop, YouTube, Spotify, etc, all sound fine. When used for Teams meetings, other users say they can't hear me very well, like the mic isn't picking me up, and periodically, the audio coming through the headphones becomes garbled and staticy (not an issue when paired with my phone - phone calls are fine.) I have to unpair and re-pair them to my laptop to get the audio working again, and I have to yell to get people to hear me. When in the middle of an important meeting, this is unacceptable. I'm now tethered to my laptop by a corded headset.I'd be inclined to say it's an issue with the Bluetooth connection, but I have no issues listening to audio through downstreaming sources. It's only an issue when using the headphones with Teams (that is the only video conferencing software we use. Can't speak to its use with Skype, Zoom, etc.) Didn't have a problem with my last wireless headphones (Purity True Wireless Earbuds with Immersive Sound, Bluetooth 5.0 Earphones in-Ear with Charging Case Easy-Pairing Stereo Calls/Built-in Microphones/IP) and oddly enough, the only reason I switched from those was because I HATED the shape of the case.Aside from the issue with Teams meetings, the headphones are fine. Sound quality is good- rich, deep bass, clear treble. Definitely not audiophile quality but they definitely get the job done for me.Headphone batteries seems to last all day, case stays charged for about a week with constant use, I especially like the wireless charging capability.I'd say pick 'em up if you don't plan on using them for Teams meetings.***EDIT***The problem described above has become increasingly frequent and has now expanded into with use on Skype and YouTube. Since 70% of these headphones' use will be connected to the laptop, they're 70% unusable. I have lowered the score to two stars (I won't go to one because they do work fine with my phone) but I've requested a return.***EDIT 3/1/2021***I updated the Bluetooth driver on my laptop this morning at work and that seems to have corrected the issue with the audio intermittently going haywire, though the other attendees on my meetings still can't hear me. The mic isn't picking me up on Teams calls. Again, not an issue when on my phone. I will use the built in mic on my Teams calls, I don't mind that.Boosting my rating back up to four stars.

title: Waterproof bluetooth headphones.
body: TL;DR: noise-canceling works, definitely waterproof (but don't wear them under water because bluetooth), price is a good deal (better than Air Pods), be aware that they are so small people might not realize you're wearing them and will try to talk to you, and don't wear them when you need to be audibly aware of your surroundings (walking in public, riding public transportation, driving).I bought these because of the price. I searched for the best-rated bluetooth headphones on Amazon.com and found these. I could not bring myself to pay $150 for Air Pods. I just can't. These are typical noise-canceling headphones, which I actually don't like, but they work very well. They come with different sizes of rubber earpieces, so you can find one that fits you best. I took the smallest ones and still couldn't hear anything with them in - anything besides the headphones. I was a little confused by the instructional pamphlet about how to connect the headphones, and that the Right one will connect to your device and then the Left one connects to the right one... blah blah. This is not actually how it played out in real life when I took them out of the box. No matter, as they were set up in a few minutes. I paired my phone with the Right one and then the Left one caught up. It will still show up in your list of devices, but it won't connect. Don't worry, like I did, that this means something is wrong. You can't connect to the Left one.I wore them in the shower. The pamphlet says this is okay, and I can confirm, they work in the shower. They are very small and unobtrusive. They work very well on phone calls. One issue could be that since no one can see the devices in your ears (if you have long hair that covers your ears), you may have people come up to talk to you while you have them in and they don't know that you have them in. You won't be able to hear them talking to you because of the noise-canceling feature.I don't recommend wearing these while walking alone at night, while walking across busy streets, while riding public transportation, while driving a car or in any situation where you need to be aware of what's going on around you, as you will not hear it. The rubber earpieces fit so well in the ear canal as to block out any outside noise. They do also have the noise-canceling feature. I'm not an audiophile so I can't analyze the quality of music playing. If I can hear it, that's good enough for me.

title: Heavy bass headphones
body: TLDR: Too much bass and/or weak treble, and design shortcomings take away a star, but overall a good set of earphones.The sound quality of these earphones are decent, but the low-end either overcomes the high-end, or the earphones just don't produce the high-end well. The overall sound is a little muddy due to this, but if you like bass you probably won't be disappointed.Both earphones are the same, meaning you can wear either in either ear. I use them individually often, so this is a bonus for me as I usually prefer my left ear being clear. They do beep for a bit if you try to use them individually, but if you do it right they only beep for a short while.On the initial charges, I am getting well over 3 hours of play time.The earphones fit comfortably into my ears, but be careful to choose the right tip. The function button on the earphones is mechanical, meaning you have to press it to activate. The button is very stiff, so you have to press pretty hard, and if you aren't careful, you will be shoving the earphone deeper into your ear, which can be painful. After a few tries you can figure out how to press the button without jamming the earphone into your ear canal.The pairing and bluetooth signal strength are both good. I haven't had any issues pairing, both as a pair and singularly, and I consistently move 2 rooms away, 25-30 ft through 2 walls, and haven't had any issues with the signal cutting out.The case is simple and straightforward. It has a charge level indicator, but no way to activate it unless you plug in a charging cable, which is a flaw. I don't use any wireless charging pads so I can't test that function.The microphone tested okay. It picked up the audio at a decent volume, and wasn't garbled or muffled. I tested multiple times, and each time it seemed to glitch just for a half second every single time, but overall it worked well enough.In comparison of these earphones to the iKanzi X9, and the Enacfire E20, these would rank number 2 on my list. These Tozo brand earphones sound much better than the iKanzi ones, which had no bass at all, but the iKanzi was overall much better designed and had better features. The Enacfire E20 has all the awesome features and great design of the iKanzi, AND has the best sound out of all three. I would pay a little more and go with the Enacfire E20 earphones.

title: Good quality TWS earbuds that work as described
body: I've had these for 5 months and I can say they are very well constructed and of good quality. The reason I gave these 4.5 stars is that I do wish they came with either ear hooks or ear wings as I am having to readjust them at the gym from time to time. As most earbuds of this type, they stay on due to a vacuum caused by the silicone ear tips.This same vacuum is the sole cause for the noise cancellation, as most of the sound is blocked off from entering your ears. This means that you are not able to have a conversation with both buds in. The sound is decent, not comparable with Jabras or anything in the above $100 range, but if you are paying $50 for these then you should only expect decent sound. I use them mostly for podcasts and music streaming in the gym and they are fine.Battery life is good, they last about 2.5 hours or so. The case does provide about 5 additional charges so the overall of 12-12.5 hours is accurate. The case does charge wirelessly and it charges up when plugged in a couple of hours. I have never had an issue with them pairing, from the time I take it out of the case and put them in my ear I always hear the "connected" voice prompt. I do have the occasional issue of the left bud temporarily loosing sound if my arm gets in front of it, when doing a movement like a front raise for example. The sound does come back once I lower my arm, so just a minor annoyance. I've watched Hulu, Netflix and YouTube videos with them and there is no latency.I haven't tested the "waterproof" feature, mainly since Bluetooth does not conduct through water, I don't see the point. I do appreciate that I don't have to worry about the earbuds getting wet in the rain. Maybe I'll find out if they are really waterproof when I give these to one of the kids and the inevitably drop them in the pool.Although they are very good, at this price they compare directly for the JLabs Air. My wife has those and the sleeve they come with do help keep them more secure. BTW, she previously had different pair of $50 TWS earbuds from Amazon but one of the buds stopped charging after about 2 months.Overall I like these, but at this price they are right behind the JLabs mainly because of the readjusting I have to do at the gym. I guess the waterproofing and the wireless charging case makes up the difference. Maybe when I fish them out of the pool and they still work I'll up my score to 5 stars!

title: Very Happy!
body: I labored over the decision of which wireless ear buds to buy for days. None of the available choices in my price range have all the features I want, and they all have mixed, sometimes highly dubious reviews.I finally decided on the TOZO T10's, and I'm very happy with them.Construction of the buds and their case appears to be good and robust.The waterproofing makes them great for showers. I haven't used them for swimming, but I would recommend using a larger sized bud than may be comfortable for everyday wear as the addition of water to the mix may cause the earbud to slip out more readily. This isn't a problem, unless you have really big ear holes since it came with 4 sizes of silicone tips and can use standard replacement tips in whatever size you need.They do not have all of the control functionality I would like, the button basically answers/rejects calls and pauses/plays media. It doesn't skip, adjust volume or really much of anything else beyond that.Call quality is not great, but cupping your hand from the earbud to your mouth definitely makes enough improvement to the mic's pickup to make them functional for calls.Syncing via Bluetooth is super easy. The earbuds can both be used singly or paired, and either earbud will work in either ear (when paired together, the R earbud is always the Right Channel and the L is always the Left). Signal quality is pretty solid up to 30 feet, I can leave the case and my phone just about anywhere in the house and be anywhere else in the house without losing connection.The charging case works as advertised. I haven't had a day yet where I ran out of power, though I have occasionally had to drop to using one earbud so that one can be charging while I'm listening to the other.Noise cancelation is moderate, as it is more a byproduct of having something stuck in your ear than any sort of purposeful engineering.They do have the mildly annoying trait of beeping every second for the first 10 seconds or so after they link up, but it does stop just about the time you're trying to figure out WTF.They may not do everything I want as well as I want, but they do everything I required and most of what I wanted. There are undoubtedly better wireless earbuds out there; but, for the price, I don't think these can be beaten.

title: So far so good
body: I picked these up because my last set of iJoy's kind of broke. My old pair had the silicone loops that go over your ears and once just fell off. I had tried earbuds before and wasn't really big on the way they felt so I went to the type like the iJoy with the ear loop. I had been wanting to try the earbuds again so the time was right. I had read good reviews on these so here we are. So far I'm liking the way they feel. There was plenty of earpieces to find the size that fits. I had to jump up a size from the default size that comes on them so sizing went pretty fast for me as it was perfect fit with once size change. They stay in place unlike the ones I tried in the past so that's a big plus. Controls are okay. The controls are pretty easy to get down as the backs are touch sensitive. This is a big plus to me. The iJoy I had, had a button to push on. As much as I liked the iJoy that button really needed to go as pushing on a button that clicked while the device is in your ear is like a crack of lighting going off in your head. WAY TOO LOUD! I had just gotten used to controlling sound or phone calls from my phone. Sound quality is good for music for the price of these little buds. Size ways pretty good too. You don't feel like you have a huge growth hanging out of your ear. Charging is easy too. You just drop them in the case and off you go. I like the idea of the magnet case as you just have to get them close to their spot in the case and they get sucked in and start charging. The case is supposed to be able to charge them several times too. Since I just drop them in when not using them they are always topped off and ready for me. Charging the case is pretty easy too as I just set the case on my charging pad and it gets charged up. The case also has a usbc port to charge it. The case came with a short cord if you need to charge them this way. As of yet it's too easy to just set the case on my charging station pad. The big test will be in the summer when I'm cutting the grass. I'm hoping they stay put in my ears as even the iJoy had a little trouble but with the ear loop they stayed close and I just had to push them back into place. So summer will tell on these guys. Thou I'm not looking forward to cutting the grass I am looking forward to summer. If there are issues I will update then. If not this will stand for my review as so far so good. Price was very good too.

title: Great right out of the box! - Updated at 13 months
body: 13 month Update/Removed a Star:I have had these for just over 13 months. They get used anywhere from 2-6 hours a day, 5/7 days a week, a lot of times just one ear piece at a time as I prefer to have one ear available. Right around the one year mark the right side ear piece started having sound issues, the sound is really robotic and static-ey, so I kind of put that one out of commission and have only used the left one for the past month. At this point, although the sound is clear on my end, there is often a lot of static heard by the other party I'm talking to, and as of today, they couldn't hear me at all. I plan to take these home from work now and probably just use them to watch tiktok until they fully give out, but I'll need something else for at work. Undecided if I want to get another pair of these or another brand entirely. Honestly for the price, I'm not that put off by them quitting after a little over a year, especially with the fairly heavy use I put them through. I will update one more time when the left piece finally stops putting out decent sound quality.First impression is that these are great! I just got them and immediately used them. I really appreciate that they came fully charged so I could try them immediately. They fit comfortably in my ears, and I am definitely on the picky side of how things feel in my ears. They're pretty noise cancelling, honestly a bit better than my wireless over the ear beats that I paid $300 for just a few years ago. My brothers opinion is that they're okay quality, decent for the price, but he's quite the music guru and is very particular about the bass sounds. I'm not. I am a very average music listener, and I intend to use these in my office so I look a little more professional while listening to music and taking the occasional call throughout the day. Right now my options are regular corded ear buds or my big over the ear beats headphones, which means either looking a little obnoxious or ripping a headphone out everytime I swivel too far in my chair. I have adhd and am prone to losing and breaking things easily, so I didn't want to spend a lot, and I think these are a great first pair of wireless earbuds that will more than suffice for me and my purposes. If my opinion changes at all, Ill update, but my first impression out of the box is that these are totally worth the money!

title: Works and incredible for the price -- but lack of volume/skip is a real bummer
body: First of all, I feel like it's insane you can get $50 Bluetooth headphones like these. So the value is amazing. They don't always automatically pair (I have to select them on my phone) and Bluetooth audio sometimes skips a handful of times an hour but that's fine, I chalk that up to Bluetooth in general. (And if I put my phone in my front jacket pocket instead of my side pocket, it never skips -- that's radio waves for ya.)BUT the fact that I can only pause/play music by tapping the button, and can't change volume or skip... UGH. It's really annoying. I'm the type of person who keeps a big playlist of everything, and when a song comes on skips it half the time because it's not my mood... and this is while biking/running... so to really enjoy these I'm forced to create more separate "mood" playlists, which I guess is fine, but I'd rather not.Instead I'd **much** rather have a double-tap do the next track and triple-tap go to the previous one, like other headphones do. (Right now double-tap makes a phone call, which is useless.) Even better, I'd happily pay an extra $20 if it had a touch-sensitive surface to tap and gliding would change volume. (Clicking the button in my ear puts pressure on my inner ear which is not painful exactly, but also certainly not comfortable.)Also -- holding the charging case and feeling its weight distribution makes it clear that it's mostly empty space on the inside! So make it *smaller* please... it's too big to keep in your pocket, which is where you want to keep it. A slimmer version would make this even nicer.The sound quality is meh -- serviceable but nothing special, and the bass is a bit too strong for me -- I actually pick the "bass reducer" EQ on Spotify to compensate a little bit, though it doesn't do much. I mean, I assume these are essentially $10 headphones with another $40 worth' of batteries and microchips and Bluetooth.Finally the eartips are great -- I have large ears and so I can only buy in-ear headphones that come with large tips included, and these do. Honestly if Apple made AirPods with the in-ear seal like these, I'd buy them in a heartbeat... but they don't. (Don't know why... they used to sell in-ear headphones as an alternative to their normal ones.) But until Apple does, it honestly seems like these ones are your best bet.

title: Work well for the price, but lacking in some features that other similarly priced earbuds have
body: I purchased these earbuds after some previous (slightly cheaper) earbuds weren't working. These didn't work either, but the problem was with with my phone after a recent update. I got them working. I kept these even though I liked the others better, because it was not the manufacturer's fault.I have a problem with wired earbuds always falling out of my ears -- but these wireless ones stay in no problem. I have yet to have one fall out.These buds paired easily. The "click" on these buds is a physical click. (My previous ones had a touch sensor without a click.) Now, all I have to do is pull the buds out of the case, and they turn on, pair with each other and with my phone. The sound is pretty good, but not top quality (which I expected at this price). The click button answers calls and hangs up. A double click is redial. When not doing phone calls, the single click halts the music (or audiobook) playback, and another click restarts the sound. These appear to be standard controls.However, there is no volume control from the buds -- I have to use my phone's volume buttons. There is also no "skip forward" or "skip backwards" features. (The previous slightly cheaper earbuds had these features using long-touch or three touches as commands.) I really wish these buds had volume controls.Another small problem -- sometimes the Bluetooth does not disconnect when I put the buds back in the case. I have to turn off Bluetooth on my phone, and turn it on again.One nice feature I liked that the case can charge wirelessly on a charging pad. However, the little rubber door over the USB charging input was glued shut -- I had to use needle-nosed pliers to get it open. But now that I got it open, I can use a USB cord to charge the case (at home) OR a wireless charging pad (at work). The case is small enough to keep in my pocket.Since I got these earbuds, I have been mainly using them to listen to audiobooks. They occasionally drop the bluetooth and I miss a couple of words, but they work well most of the time. When I have to turn up the volume, it goes to a decently high level -- enough to drown out ambient sound.So, I'd say these earbuds are a good value for the money, but a few little things could make them better.

title: Almost perfect for my needs.
body: I must say to start that I was more attracted to the Tozo T6s in the beginning, but the negative reviews about the touch being too sensitive made me go this route instead.The other major issue I had with the Tozo T10s was the fact that most reviews said I couldn’t turn the volume up or down via touch, but I was so pleasantly surprised when they arrived and I saw a “UPDATED” statement in the front of the box. They added volume up and down capabilities and made them soft touch as well, which completes solving the main issues most people seemed to have with this model in the beginning. Now you don’t have to press so hard to tap buttons.In the sound quality subject, they sound very clear, great bass and clear highs. Being a singer, I personally prefer the mids a little clearer, for there is where most vocals are recorded, but I find the amount in this model very decent. The mix sounds homogeneous in general, which makes them a good option for most types of music I listen to. (That are a lot).They fit perfectly and won’t fall from my ears, no matter what I’m doing.As for the battery life, I don’t use them for long periods of time, so I haven’t run them out yet. But I have been using them on and off for periods of about an hour and then put them in the charging case to charge and have been using them for a whole week and a half with a single charge, and still going without charging the case. I would say these could give you a good 3 or 4 hours straight with a single charge, but honestly, I have to do further testing in this matter.My draw back is still the charging case being too big for pockets. It is totally fine if you keep them in a backpack or a bag or purse. But for pockets, it is just too bulky. I regularly use them in a bag or in my cargo pockets in my pants.In the connectivity section, I usually ride a scooter, and even with the phone in my backpack or in my pockets, the connection stays strong and I haven’t had any connection loss issues in the two months using these.Finally, I would say this is a good pair of earbuds if you are up to something sturdy, useful and great for the price. If you want something better and can pay big money, then go for EarPods or something, but this is a great option for everyday use.

title: Good product. Very worth the money. Fit may vary. Do not use in water.
body: I originally got these headphones for sailing because they're small and waterproof. I have since used them a lot and have decided that I would not take these out on the boat because I know I would lose them immediately.Sound quality is good and battery life is acceptable. I rarely have them run out of juice because when you store them in the case, they charge. You can get about six hours out of a fully charged set/case. And all the charging involved is very quick. The case has wireless charging which I can state is compatible with a samsung wireless puck charger. I have only used that to charge it and have had no problems. The batteries charge very quickly, the case takes about 30 minutes to charge fully.Will they fit you? Maybe. The right one fits me perfectly and I have no issues with it. But I can't get the left one to fit the same way. It's not the product, it's my ear canal that is shaped a bit different only the left. There are 4 sets of interchangeable plastic buds and I was able to make it work, but the fit is not bomb-proof.Bluetooth pairing and connection is nearly flawless, I never have problems that aren't solved by putting the buds back in the case and taking them back out. The bluetooth phone functionality is acceptable, but it's not as good as just holding the phone to your ear for the people on the other end of the line. Controlling the phone via the buttons on the buds is annoying because it clicks loudly in your ear, and there isn't any control to be had anyway aside from answering calls and pausing music.Edit to add one negative piece of information. The bluetooth range and reception is pretty pathetic. The headphones will skip and have bad connection if my phone happens to be in my left pocket or stashed at the small of my back, and the range you can walk away from your phone while wearing these is about 20 feet tops. Other bluetooth devices I own do not have this problem.Do not use these buds in the water or where you can drop them deeper than you're willing to swim for them. I wore these in the shower to try out the waterproofing, and they fell out the second they got wet. The waterproofing is meant to save them from falling in a puddle, nothing more.Would I buy them again? That remains to be seen.

title: For the price, these can't be beat. However, there are some drawbacks. Read on
body: Just received these yesterday. I wanted some wireless earbuds but refuse to pay $200 for airpods. Upon searching in amazon, these pop up as the first result with almost 180k reviews and pretty much 5 stars. The $30 price tag threw me off, no way could these be so cheap and have nearly 5 stars with 180k reviews. So I purchased them to find out for myself.Build: Nice presentation. Charging case is well built, USB-C charging cord is quite short however. The buds magnetically snap in when you place them inside the case. The buds themselves are well built, they don't seem cheap. A drawback is that there are no buttons on these buds, they are touch sensitive which can get annoying at times when placing these in your ears while music is playing.Performance/Sound Quality: Ok, so here's the thing. (NOTE: I've never used wireless earbuds before so this may be a issue in ALL earbuds vs just this model.) These do sound great, HOWEVER you have to get them placed PERFECT in your ears in order to hear them at the highest quality. These come with a bunch of different sized rubber tips to fit your to your ear. I find the medium ones (the ones that are already on the buds) fit best. If you push them too far in, the sound becomes filtered and volume is low, and if you have them too loose, there is ZERO bass. But when they are just right, the bass is VERY impressive and the mids/highs are clear and clean. There is a trick that helps get a perfect fit. Pull the top/back of your ear to stretch open your ear canal and then place them in sideways and rotate clockwise. Its easier if you have music playing already so you can hear when the music sounds just right while fit them in.Once they fit right, these can handle some really heavy low end tracks. Dubstep/Bass music sounds low and prominent when basslines hit. 808's in hip hop sound full. Vocals are crisp.Battery Life: I can't report fully on the battery life yet as I had just got these and just started using them today. So far I have them going for over an hour and a half and they're still going. I read that they last around 3 hours. Charging can take about an hour.TL/DR: For $30 bucks the sound quality is up there with high end ear buds. Tricky to fit right, but worth it when you do.

title: Extremely short range. Likes to fall out of ears. Still pretty great.
body: CONS:• The weight balance of these is designed very poorly. The main part of the earbuds are constantly wanting to fall out of your ear, meaning you will expect to reposition them frequently. This includes during workouts and showers. It is not an issue with the tips, as I've never had this issue with other brands or other types of bud-style headphones. You can use some other brands' tips on these, but this didn't fix the problem for me, just made it happen less.• If there's a wall between you and the audio source, expect problems. This is fine if you're using your phone as the source and keeping it on you, but this limits its use if you're planning on walking away from your source. Don't get this if you wanted to use PC audio while doing something in another room or walking around. Luckily, once you're in range again, it corrects all the weird issues quickly and automatically.• Annoying to pair for the first time to devices. Sometimes takes dozens of attempts. Once paired for the first time with that device, it's easy to reconnect.• Moisture hitting the buds can trick the buds into thinking you want to pause, change volume, or even turn them off. Can be caused by the shower, exercising, or being in heavy rain. Would be nice to be able to turn off these functions with a "lock" setting, but for the price, it's acceptable.PROS:• Water resistance (Remove them when you wash your hair! See cons)• Sound quality surprisingly good• Seal in the ear is pretty good, with the exception of them falling out all the time. Moisture from sweat, shower, or rain will make this so much worse.• Battery life of buds and charging case is great. Shows how much charge the case has when you put the buds in to charge. You can even have one in-ear and the other charging, to always have at least one ear connected. This is super useful, my favorite feature.• Comes with multiple silicone tips, and is compatible with some other brands' tips if you have a favorite fit from another pair of headphones.• This price point is pretty great for a relatively robust pair of headphones. As long as it continues to have solid performance and a decent lifespan, I'd absolutely get another pair once these kick the bucket despite their problems.

title: Great for audiobooks and phone calls!
body: I usually use my MPOW Jaws headphones, but they aren't safe to use in the pool. So, I bought these earbuds -- and I *love* them! I love that I can put the earbud back in the case to charge. I love that I can set the case down on my wireless charger and the whole thing just charges. (It *also* has a port on the back of the case, if you don't have a wireless charger.)In comparison to my MPOW Jaws, the battery life of these buds isn't as spectacular -- but I expected that. I still would like to see them last a *little* longer. I knocked 2 stars off battery life because of the incredibly annoying audio that tells me to charge it -- every 20 seconds or so! Why give me a warning when it renders the device useless because of the warnings? (It makes more sounds when calls connect and disconnect, too.) I haven't stopped to see what my phone thinks the bud's battery level is, when I get the warning. (My Jaws start warning me at 30% battery remaining -- which is quite ridiculous!)Since I rarely listen with both buds in, I can't speak to the noise cancellation. But, I do feel the sound quality depends on a good fit in the ear. If I'm listening to music, I frequently check to make sure the bud is still seated well. I'm not sure I'd be comfortable doing anything truly strenuous or active, for fear of losing a bud.I like that I can use *just* the right earbud, or both. (But not the left alone.) The left connects to the right automatically when they are both removed from the case. Both buds are clickable, though I've never used the left one long enough to reach for it to pause/play or answer/disconnect.The biggest disadvantage these have over my neckband-style headphones is not being able to remove the bud without having to put it someplace. With my Jaws, I can pull the bud and let it dangle from the wire. That said, I *do* love being completely wire-free when I use these! No catching the wires and yanking the bud out by accident!Overall, I would recommend these. I'm hard-of-hearing and have found them to be just as capable of carrying on a phone conversation as my neckband Jaws. And between 2 Jaws headphones and these, I'm rarely without a charged device for phone, audiobooks or music.

title: T10 compared to T8
body: T10--Great sound-Very waterproofT8-decent sound-very lightThe T8 has a 90 degree design compared to the T10 130ish degree design. The T8 stays In my ear better but you can cram the T10 in a lot deeper so you can really hear that superior sound quality.The T10 seem about twice as massive as the T8, while still being nearly the same actually size. Obviously the larger elements require bigger batteries and they’ve crammed all that in a ultra compact size. This extra weigh sometimes make the T10 feel less natural then the T8Overall the T10 is far superior, yet has its own flaws. I would only recommend the T10 over the T8 if your interested in the high waterproof rating or deeper bass capabilities.—Update—After a few weeks I’ve noticed a few other thingsThe T10 battery warning voice plays layered over the input audio, rather then completely coving the audio like the T8 does. This is great because you don’t miss any of your audio. I really like this upgraded feature.The T10 magnetic case is great because the ear buds don’t turn on until you take them out of the case, unlike the T8 that turn in as soon as you open the lid, which causes them to try to connect to each other. This was an inconvenience for me since I often use only one bud at a time.And since I only use one bud at a time, The (R) and (L) decal on each are essential for knowing which channel im trying to connect. The T8 has some low quality ink that rubs off within a few weeks. The T10 is completely different and will not rub off. Much betterBattery life of both sets is not great, but it’s definitely what one should expect with such a small unit. Just Keep the case charged and handy and it’s not a big deal.I seem to always have trouble with the left channel not wanting to connect after a few months of use, But considering that these cost about the same as a set of premium wired earbuds (that I seem to always destroy with in a week) I’m pretty happy with the longevity of these.I still stand by My original recommendation. The T8 is good enough for most, but the T10 is arguably better

title: Decent Sound Quality - Cheaper Thank AirPods
body: I've now had these for a few months, and overall I'm happy with the purchase...They can occasionally be a little fiddly when it comes to pairing - you absolutely have to remove the left earbud from the case before the right, if you don't the two buds won't connect to each other. This can be sorted by dropped both back into the case and trying again. Along the same lines, I've found that they prefer to connect to my iPhone 6 more than my iPhone Pro (one is a work phone and the other is personal). It doesn't matter which device they connected to last - if they can see the iPhone 6 that's what they connect to first. Shutting the bluetooth off on the iPhone 6 solves the problem but it can be annoying.But what you're buying these for is sound quality. I'm not an audiophile but when paired with my phone or my iPad they sound just fine to me. Music is clear and the volume is plenty loud. The same can be said for calls - I can hear them just fine. Where things aren't so great is the microphone in the earbuds - they're awful and universally my callers can't hear me when I use them. These are useless on a call unless you're just planning on listening. I've also not been impressed with these when using them paired to my Mac - that tends to drop out, a lot...Noise cancelation is pretty decent. Clearly these aren't active noise cancelling, but they fit tight enough into the ear canal that they block out a lot of ambient noise. Walking through downtown Chicago at rush hour I could listen to a podcast without having to turn up the volume to overcome the noise of traffic and the EL.They fit tightly and comfortably in my ears - something that I can't say about Apple's wired earbuds. But it's the tendency for Apple's earbuds to fall out of my ears that had me looking for something other than AirPods when I was looking to go wireless. I've worn these on the train to work and while walking downtown and they've never budged - and considering the stories I've heard about people loosing AirPods, these staying secure in my ears is a big deal. Plus, if I loose one of these, I'm out a whole lot less money than I would be if I lost an AirPod.

title: Stoped charging within 3 months.
body: Comfort was not too bad. The design was decent. They did not fit properly with any of the ear pieces. However I have had this issue with other earbuds as well, so I am not deducting much for this. The battery life was poor. I had connectivity issues. However, what was worst of all; they stoped taking a charge after between two and three months of intermittent light use. No, I did not drop them. No, I did not get them wet.Edit:The company reached out to me, and offered to replace the Tozo T10 with Tozo T6. With the Tozo 6 the battery life is longer, and they do pair easily with my phone. The signal travels a good distance allowing me to move around up to 5 metes from my phone while still getting good sound quality, and signal. However, the Tozo T6 are heavier, and bulkier than the Tozo 10. As mentioned, I cannot get a good fit the same as with the Tozo 10. The ill fit along with the weight allow the earbuds to fall out easily, and frequently. As previously mentioned of the T10 I do not deduct for lack of fit as this is an issue with every earbud I have tried.The poor fit combined with the weight, and bulk (they stick out from my ears at about a quarter inch which aids them in falling out.I tried wearing them in the shower as they are waterproof. They repeatedly fell out, so I can say without a doubt that they are waterproof. With that, I would not suggest wearing them at any outside sports activity unless that activity included wearing something around the head. I can confidently wear the earbuds outside as long as I am wearing a knit hat pulled down well over my ears.The case is larger than the T10. Not as cute, but it does charge the EarPods at least 4 times before needing a charge. Note: I do not run down the earbuds, or the case completely so do not take this as a testament of time used. Overall they are decent. If you do not have any issues with the fit of earbuds this may be the product for you. The company was very quick I getting the replacement to me even though it was during the height of the holiday season. I would recumbent for someone wanting something fairly unobtrusive to wear at the office, at home, or other contained environment.

title: TOZO T10 Bluetooth Good value
body: I bought these to use with my iPhone 7 Plus. Right out of the box they are impressive. The charging pod is solid, and I like that it's magnetic. Position the earbuds near where they go and they magically pop right into their place. The USB cable is only around 10 inches (25 cm) which is a little inconvenient, but I have half a dozen other USB cords to use. The earbuds must have had some charge because I only plugged in the pod for a few minutes and they were ready to test them out. Be careful to read the instructions about pairing these. It's pretty easy, but you should only pair the RIGHT (not left) earbud to your device. The left earbud pairs to the right one automatically after you remove them from the pod. At first I mistakenly paired both the right and left to my device, and the result is there was no sound through the earbuds. To rectify this I had to go into my phone Bluetooth settings and forget both devices. Then I started fresh and everything worked fine. The sound quality is pretty good. I would like a bit more bass, but I'm spoiled by mostly using larger headphones. At first I was a bit dismayed, because there seemed to be an excessive high frequency response, making hiss and noise much more noticeable. I then remembered I could use the EQ feature on Apple Music to tailor the sound better, and now I'm satisfied. The color is a nice deep blue. The "R" and "L" imprinted on the buds does not stand out as much as it seems on the picture, which is a very slight disappointment. I purposely picked this color because I wanted to see this better with my old eyes to tell right from left. I mainly bought these to listen to music on dry land. I have not tested the mic or water resistance so I cannot speak to those. The package includes both earbuds, battery equipped charging pod, 10 inch USB to mini USB cable, lanyard, 4 pairs of earpieces for sizing (including the ones already on the buds), instruction booklet, quick-start guide, and warranty card. As some have stated already, you do not need a wireless charging pad to charge these. You charge the pod with a cable and the pod charges the earbuds. So far as I can tell the battery in the charging pod is not removable.

title: Nice first pair of completely wireless buds
body: I was excited to get my first set of completely wireless earbuds. These came very nicely packaged - high end and professional - I’d say they remind me of iPhone packaging...was kind of impressed with it frankly. They were very easy to pair. At first, only the left one had blinking light. I read the instructions and it said the right one is the master so I thought it was broken. But I pressed the on/off button on the right earbud a few times and then the light came on and paired without any problems. The case is very nice, a little heavier than I expected but I hadn’t realized the case was a charger so it makes sense. Small and easy to carry around and it comes with a string should you choose to attach it to the case for transport.The earbuds came almost fully charged, and the case came half charged (2 out of 4 lights). So far I’ve been able to use the earbuds for a couple of hours without recharging. Haven’t tried longer than that. They fit well in my ears with the attached buds; haven’t tried the other sizes that came with it. I like how they are not visible when I have my hair down. I love the mobility and not having to worry about getting tangled up in the wires.I got two of these - one at $35 before Cyber Monday and another at $30 on Cyber Monday. I should’ve gotten it a few days before that as it was 60% off so would’ve been $20...had it in my cart but forgot to checkout and lost the deal.The one thing I was a little disappointed in is I feel like the stereo sound did not meet my expectations. I read so many good reviews about the sound quality and at first I thought it was because of my surroundings outside the home where there are other noises. But then I tried it at home in a room by myself and it’s still the same. Comparing to my wired earbuds and wireless earbuds with wires between the two buds, I don’t think the sound quality is as good....bass is not as pronounced and it just doesn’t sound as stereo as the other earbuds I have which are not high end ones.But overall, I like my purchase and I'm waiting to see if the price can go down a bit so I can get another one for my other daughter.

title: Very good earbuds in this price range, but it does have a few limitations
body: I got these for when I'm out in the pastures working - I didn't want the hassle of having a cord dangling. (I kinda wish I had that cord now - see below).Good: The earbuds are light, and with the additional sizes of earcaps provided, you should be able to get the correct size for your ear canal. The pairing with a phone is very quick and easy, and each earbud announces when it has connected. Sound quality is pretty good, and well worth the price. Noise cancellation is good due to the in-ear sealing. Battery life is pretty good - I got three to three and a half hours of continuous use before it announced battery charge was low and to recharge.Not as good: I found there are limitations as to how far you can get away from your phone before the signal drops out, and it's less than what I would expect. I left the phone on the tractor, and found if I got more than 25 or 30 feet away, the signal would be intermittent at best, then drop out. I don't know if that is a function of the unit, or limitations on bluetooth. Most people will never experience this if they are carrying their phone with them at all times. Also, infrequently, the signal between the ear pieces would develop latency issues, where it wasn't uniform between the two ears. Very disconcerting having the sound in one ear slightly ahead of the other.Now the bad (for me): I was in the pasture working yesterday, and I reached up to swat a fly away off my ear. Unfortunately, I hit the left earbud, and it popped out of my ear into long field grass. I never found it again. The black color makes it effectively disappear between the stalks of grass; I tried using a magnet to see if I could retrieve it that way - not enough metal or magnet in the earbud to attract it. And I don't think there is any way to have it make a sound loud enough to find it. So the left ear is lost forever as far as I can tell.Luckily, I see there is a lightning deal on right now to reorder it. I think for this next set, I'm going to paint them with a bit of florescent orange paint to help find them if this happens again :)

title: The Perfect Stocking Stuffer
body: Probably my favorite low to mid tier wireless earbuds. Sadly I lost one, but I have definitely enjoyed the T10s more than I thought. The reviews were what caught me initially. Thousands of people vouching for a low cost item was wild to me?? I’ll spend the 25 to try them and they have become my favorite. I wish they had a bud replacement program but they’re so cheaply priced (no shade, no tea) I could just buy another pair *knaye shrug*. However what do I do with the other bud? *face palm* Anyway here my favorite qualities about the Tozo T10:- Perfectly sized, doesn’t hurt my ears. I often sleep with them and 8 out of 10 times, they remain there until I wake up with zero pain. Even the JLab Go Air Pop, their more compact wireless buds, aren’t as comfortable after few hours let alone all night.- The battery life is amazing. As stated before, I go to bed with them in and fully charged, they are capable of lasting 8 hours. There was time I went 2 full weeks without charging the case but I wasn’t going hard with usage. However, I can still go a week with out charging the case at my highest usage.- Range: better than my old JLabs and skull candies. My apt is a little over 1100 sqft and from the farthest points, connection remains solid: no cut out, no disconnection.-Connectivity: can be paired individually and capable of dual connection.- Great sound: I’m no audiophile, but I can at least tell decent audio. These are comparable to some of the more known brands.In the beginning the left bud would randomly disconnect but that could be fixed with putting it back in its case. After while I didn’t have that issue or because I often use one at a time, I stopped noticing. So they’re not perfect but pretty damn good for what you paid.Also, I recommend buying foam bud cushions. The rubber/silicone cushions cause my canal to sweat and become irritated. I bought a pack of 6 they may get dirty faster but ain’t nothin a little alcohol cleaning can’t handle. I tend to pop them off accidentally anyway so replacing them isn’t a gripe to me.

title: Great price
body: I'm not a music buff, nor have I ever used ear buds of a higher quality before - so please understand my review is based mostly on listening to books while running (not music).My order arrived very timely. The buds themselves paired easily with my phone (Samsung Galaxy) and I was able to get the volume I wanted by using my phone's volume control.The case is very compact, and charges with a USB. The earbuds fit into their slots with a magnetic fit, so they feel secure and like they won't bounce around.After 6 days of use, the right earbud stopped working. This was because it wasn't charging in the case. The right earbud is the 'master' and without it functioning, the left won't easily connect to any device or reset - regardless, I want two earbuds, right??I used Tozo's website to reference ways to fix the problem - nothing there about earbud charging issues. I also went to the internet to try to find anything regarding what might have gone wrong. Nothing there either. The left earbud would charge in the right earbuds' slot in the charging case, so the issue seemed to be truly with the right earbud itself.I contacted customer service at Tozo, and they gave me a few things to try - i.e. make sure the prongs in the charging case were not bent (they were not), ensure there is no visible 'stuff' in the earbuds charging holes (there wasn't) and finally use alcohol on a q-tip to clean the earbud in the event water or sweat got into the charging holes. This did not make a difference. They sent me a new earbud set after a bit of back and forth.So far so good with the new earbud set - I've been letting them sit outside the case after use to try to encourage any moisture to dry. I'm on day 3 of use....so I'll be sure to update this if there are any further issues with charging the earbuds.Overall i'm happy with them, and for the price, I think they look pretty good! It was a minor inconvenience to have to wait for the new pair to arrive. Hopefully these last!

title: A nice product, with a few caveats
body: I bought this product after becoming intrigued at the prospect of completely wireless earbuds, with a case that was capable of charging. I spend a lot of time on my phone, between listening to audio and making and answering phone calls while working from home. The addition of the waterproof factor was also something that was really interesting to me, since I do run and get caught in the rain sometimes.While I'm still impressed with the audio quality and waterproof factor, I did run into a few annoyances once trying these out, which make be dealbreakers for other buyers. While you can start and stop audio or calls using the buttons on the units, you cannot control volume at all, unless you do so on your phone. I also found that call quality was TERRIBLE, and those I was talking to on the phone often couldn't understand me. The earbuds were also often slow to connect to Bluetooth - I even had to place them back in the case and remove them again to "restart" the pairing process a time or two. Battery life was shorter than some of my other bluetooth earbud units, but still long enough for a good run and a few hours of around-the-house listening.I will say that I contacted the seller upon experiencing my issues, and they immediately apologized for the inconvenience, acknowledged the issues, and offered me a partial refund (I believe I bought this product when it was priced on the higher end of its average). I was very impressed with their prompt response and interest in maintaining good customer service. My understanding is that they intend to work on these issues and improve them in future models.Despite the issues experienced, I do think the product is worth the purchase, as long as user expectations are realistic. If you don't talk on the phone with them, have use for the waterproof factor (it IS really cool being able to listen worry-free in the bathtub), and are prepared to adjust volume on your phone, they're a really nice little "spare" buy. I just wouldn't pay my original purchase price of $50 for them.

title: Adequate but not great
body: I’ve been waiting for a new company to finish crowdfunding some sweet wireless earbuds, but I got too impatient. I picked these because price and product seemed very similar to multiple products getting great reviews. They aren’t very expensive so I was mainly worried I’d get what I paid for. I’m cautious spending too much on pirated tech since it’s usually inferior. These earbuds are very adequate but are far from being great. They pair first to each other, then they pair to my device. A pleasant voice tells me what’s happening, but to hear it you have to get them in your ears fast. The startup happens when you remove the buds from the charging/storage case. The pairing occurs when I’m putting them in my ears and getting the fit as comfortable as I can. I have a small head with similar ear canals. I’m using the small flanges that fit the ear well, but doesn’t provide a lot of surface to counter balance the weight of the bud. With the battery and tech, they have some weight to them. I went on a 1 1/2 mile walk on a dirt road wearing these. I adjusted each side about three times to shift the weight, find comfort, and once to stop it from rubbing the same spot. They may get more comfortable once I figure out their sweet spot. They do a great job blocking external noises because of the fit. I didn’t hear whistling wind from my brisk walk. The sound is adequate but not high quality. The range just flat out sucks with these. I had a pair of shorts with a cell phone pocket by the right knee pit. I’m not tall so it’s not too far from my ears. I would get no noise from one ear, the other, or both just walking. When I moved the phone to my front hip pocket I was able to get a good connection. I had to do nothing more than move the phone from one spot to the other, the earbuds did all the rest to get me listening again. If I did not have to tether the phone so close to the buds I would like them better. I’m hoping technology improves quickly so that our bodies don’t interfere with the signals, but until it’s affordable and effective, I’ll use these.

title: Worked great, some cons tho
body: These worked great and to all the people commenting about them falling out. At first these were perfect I've had them three months now. My biggest issue isnt what most people are saying though. They got perfectly for my ears, however I believe exchanging a bigger silicone ear piecee instead or the ones I have right now will fix the issue pretty easily as it came with ur 3 diff sized sm, med, large.The sound quality is greater then expected for the price and they seem to not die quick at all when in use.Now for the cons...As I said I've had these for three months and my biggest concern of the moment is that they started to create an electrical fire over heating smell. This is concerning as I am also asthmatic as well as I believe it caused me to have temp ear aches as well as a migrain trigger. Messaged manufacturer and I hope to see their response as I really love these.The other issues I'm having is after using them so long they are now falling put of my ears more but again I feel as though that's an easy fix to just exchange the silicone ear piece removable part.As well as I have noticed that the ear pieces do not hold an equal charge and do not charge evenly when used. They seem to always stay charging in the wireless case which is fine because wi believe that's how they r suppose to work however I do feel as though this self charging case is part of the electrical burning smell as it comes from both the case and the ear pieces. Am hoping that the seller can send me a new pair as I adore these ear pieces but also it's a fire hazard and concern for the overheated electrical smell coming from the product.I saw another review saying they don't have a volume change option when in use and that comment is wrong as they do. These sounds r adjusted by diff set of taps on either ear one ear I'd a sound up the other is a sound down as well as double tap left to pause and double tap right to play when pausing a video like Netflix or YouTube.I hope this was helpful

title: A mixture of excellent and poor design.
body: If you get these for under $30 they are a decent bang for your buck all things considered.ProsWater proof. I've used them in the shower, sweating, etc. and dropped them in the shower. Never had a problem.While they are designed to be paired with a master/slave configuration you can also solo pair the left (slave) ear. I often listen to podcasts while working with only one in my ear. This enables me to alternate charging and using each side, something that even high end brands often don't allow. This also means the wear on the battery can be evenly distributed. With most competitors even high end ones when the master earbud runs out of juice you are done.Good bass and mids.ConsHorrible highs! I mean scratchy and sounds like its clipping. I've been told this can be due to the water proof barrier. It's the price you pay for cheap and water proof I guess.They take so long to pair each time you pull them out of the case that it is faster to manually pair to them from my phone even if my phone is in my pocket. Not sure why because they can pair very fast if you manually do so on your phone.The battery indicator on the case is very inaccurate. I can charge both side 2 full times and case still shows its full. Then charging one side will drop the case to showing empty. Although when it shows empty I can get another full charge or more on both sides.Others have reported the case gets stuck continuously discharging with the battery indicator on. Ive seen this a few times and pulling out an earbud and putting it right back in stops it. It does tend to significantly discharge over time. If you haven't used it in 2 weeks from a full charge the earbuds will be ready to go but the case will be nearly dead.I would love to see a model that pairs faster and has decent highs. Perhaps a less waterproof one. Overall if you need water proof these really are and for listening to podcasts all day switching out which side you are using they are the best I have found in any price range.

title: More than decent quality and leaves your wallet happy!
body: Had these for a solid month and a half now.. been using them daily and so far...Pros:1. Find the right size silicone bud allowing a snug (not vacuum-sealed) fit and noise cancellation is pretty solid. Bass is more than satisfactory.2. Took a shower with them on and its working just fine. (don't submerge them in water though) they're water-resistant.. not waterproof.3. Battery life is great and the four additional charges you can have on your docking case allows even more prolonged use. Quick 45 minute charge gave me 4-5 hrs of music playtime. (haven't tested out calltime)4. Personal pet peeve of mine but the low battery announcement isn't rushed (good five minutes before device actually powers off from no more battery life)5. Call quality is fantastic and people on the receiving end of the line never complained about not being able to hear me when I am talking.6. Magnetic ends on the buds allow for more convenient access to the charging dock - no need to fit them inside like a puzzle piece.7. Answer/Reject Calls function is neat and announces the phone number of who's calling.Cons:1. Even with the optimal silicone bud size for my ears, they do tend to fall out of your ear every now and then. Especially for me, it was when walking down stairs and rigorous sprinting.2. No playback function or volume function.. have to adjust volume or next/prev track on connected bluetooth device.3. Another small pet peeve of mine but if you do decide to use one bud at a time, there will be four to five intermittent beeps until the opposing bud is paired as well. Not a huge dealbreaker though - it becomes more bearable as time passes lol.TLDRSure you can go with Bose/Beats/Jabras etc but why spend that extra cash when there are gems like these that are more than capable and satisfactory. Maybe time will truly tell whether if these buds will last as long but for people looking to make a quick (less guilty) purchase - you won't regret these.

title: Excellent Customer Service!!!  This is for bass lovers and folks with multiple devices...
body: Since posting my original middling review on Amazon, the company’s customer service proactively reached out to me with concerns that the earbuds I received may have been a defective item. I must say that floored me because I had previously stated in my review that I had no intention of returning the earbuds... and I am still within window to return them as I write this updated review.Tozo’s customer service offered to either give me a gift card for the purchase price I paid or to give me an upgraded set of Tozo earbuds. Not really interested in the cash, I took them up on the offer of the upgraded earbuds. I received the Tozo T6 earbuds and could not be happier. A quick note on the differences for folks who may be comparing these two:Tozo T10 - I think the bass is better on this pair and after I purchased a fast charging USB-C cord, I am able to get the charging base to fully charge. The key benefits of this one over the T6 is that you can pair each earbud as if it was a separate blue tooth device AND you can pair each/both to two devices at a time. So I would say the Tozo T10’s are for the guy/gal with multiple devices they use daily and bass lovers.Tozo T6 - In hindsight, this is the pair I should have purchased. The charging case is essentially the same size as an Airpods case, it’s IPX8 waterproof has very good bass. The case is noticeably smaller (in a good way) in your pocket. These earbuds have the same quality that I often describe Apple products with: “it just works.” You won’t pair these earbuds separately and with multiple devices at a time... and I don’t care. I love them.So all in, I love Tozo’s products and especially their customer service. Read up on the differences between the Tozo T10 and T6 and make the best decision for yourself... and know that their customer service folks are responsive and want you to be happy! That’s rare and I’m eternally grateful.===============================ORIGINAL REVIEW:It hard for me to rate this product because I want to love it. Here are my pros, cons, things to consider and final thoughts:PROS:-The bass is legitimately excellent. The speaker drivers are better than any wireless earbuds I have ever used. I am not an audophile nor do I buy $150+ headphones but the sound is legit for hip hop, dance, and other similar music.-the sound is also very good. Not excellent, like the base, but it is very good. No complains here.-Other miscellaneous features that I liked were true to description: water protection, battery life, overall build quality.-Price is extremely affordable compared to other higher end alternatives. I'd rather buy three of these than one pair of Beats headphones.-the earbuds batteries never have run out on me. I run/walk for about two hours each day, so I admittedly have never tried to really drain them. When you read the problems below, this will seem more like a pro point.-these earbuds can pair to two devices at the same time. So I usually have my iPad and iPhone paired up without having to switch between them in the settings.CONS:-bluetooth connection - if you follow the instructions, the separate earbuds pair automatically with each other with no problems. the problem is that I feel like there is a 1-10 minute period where the headphones are "searching" for my phone no matter how close it is to me for a successfully, no drop pairing. The sound can cut out or dip for a minimal period while it is going through this phase, but it's annoying.-Using two earbuds at the same time - My usual usage case is walking/standing with my earbuds in and my phone in my pocket. If i tilt my head in one direction or another one or both headphones lose the connection momentarily BUT they do automatically pair back up. It usually goes away the longer I have them in my ears but I am afraid to leave my phone on a table and walk any reasonable distance away. i am not confident they will stay paired if I was on the opposite end of the same room with no obstructions.-Turning off the earbuds. I think this annoys me the most... I may be wrong in this desire, but I wish the earbuds would automatically shut off when I put them in the charging case. They DON'T! I have put them in the charging base for hours and then realized that they were on the whole time. Which means that they do have a charge, but they were draining the base unnecessarily while I thought I had it plugged in and was charging the base and the earbuds. I've bought a fast charging USB C to micro USB charging cord in hopes that it'll help. The current safest thing I can do to get them to turn off faster/automatically is to have both my iPad and iPhone "forget the device" so that it times out versus remains on and draining the batteries.-the base takes forever to fully charge. related to the above point, I have NEVER had a fully charged base unit.Things to consider:If you're still reading this, these are true wireless headphones. I am currently within the return window, but I won't be returning these earbuds. This is mostly because my expectations are low given the price point. My core headphones are a pair of Apple Airpods Pros. So this pair suffers from me comparing them to a $200+ pair that "simply works." Some additional thoughts and tips that are neither pros/cons to me:-You can use each earbud as a separate "device." When you turn on bluetooth you see each earbud listed as a separate bluetooth device. So, follow their recommendation when it says ONLY use the right earbud as your connection via our phone/device. The right will automatically "add in" the left earbud, but the reverse is not true. What this means is that you can pair one earbud to one device and one to another. This sucks when you then want to pair them together again.. so I use the "forget this device" setting after every independent use of the left earbud.In summary, I want to love these earbuds, but the annoying fickle ways that the bluetooth works or momentarily drops whenever I first put them in my ears was something I could have accepted because of my use case. Add in the fact that its an annoying process to get the earbuds to turn off 100% of the time which effectively makes it near impossible to fully charge the base and you understand why I am writing this review. Again, I am not returning them! I love the sound. I almost always use them in exercise use cases or when I know I am going to be acting carelessly or around water/rain or in a situation where I am concerned about potential theft of my Airpod Pros. So as a second bluetooth device, these are excellent. As your primary headphones, I give them a three out of five stars.

title: Solid Set of TWB Earphones
body: I have only owned these earphones for one day (purchased them for $27 on a Lightning Deal), so this "review" is really just my first impressions. I'll update this review if any issues come up that make me reconsider my feelings on IEMs. I am not a hardcore audiophile, but I do appreciate quality headphones and earphones. I'll talk first about my intial impression of the build quality, set up and bluetooth performance and sound quality.The build quality on these Tozo T2 is pretty darn good. The case is well made and very solidly assembled. I have no doubt it can handle a good drop from your pocket onto concrete. I also don't doubt Tozo's claim that the case has an IPX8 rating (and I usually take those types of claims from chinese manufacturers with huge grains of salt). The micro USB port on the case is covered by a rubber seal and the case shuts firmly and is held in place by three heavy earth magnets. The same type of magnets are also used to hold the earbuds in the case when they are charging. They aren't going to rattle loose or pop out unless you want them to come out. The earbuds are also really well made. They have a very solid feel and the plastic doesn't feel cheap at all. Because of these are TWB earphones, they are not the lightest I have used though and, overall, they feel heavier than typical wired bluetooth IEMs. This isn't really a big deal, since I had no issue using them through my workout at the gym this morning, including HIIT session on the treadmill and an intense weight training session. I also tested the Tozo's claims of IPX8 water resistance rating by giving them a wash in the sink and using them in the shower. The held up just fine and it was nice to be able to listen to my tunes during a shower!!! This capability makes these earphones perfect for the beach or pool, where you wouldn't risk more expensive and/or delicate earphones.As for the feature set, I really like the fact that the case for these earphones can be charged using a qi wireless charger. I could have lived with the micro USB, but this just makes the process of charging the case (and earphones) so much easier!! On the other hand, these phones have one button on each bud that controls your phone. Click once and it will pause your music. Click again and the music will restart. Those are only media functions these buttons serve. Other than that, the buttons are used for accepting, rejecting or hanging up on phone calls. It would have been nice if you could use them to change music tracks and control the volume. Other earphones in this price range ($40 list price) have these capabilities, but they don't have the waterproof rating or the wireless charging capability. So, you have to choose what is more important to you.As for bluetooth connectivity, set up is as easy as everyone claims. Take the IEMs out the case, they go into pairing mode and connect to each other. They show up as available on your phone's bluetooth menu, and you click on the right earphone--that's it. The automatically turn on and connect to the phone when the IEMs are removed from the case. This makes them very simple and easy to use. Due to their size (although they are not large by any means), it does take a little longer to get them comfortably situated in your ear than most wired IEMs (bluetooth or otherwise). This is probably true for most TWB IEMs (with a few exceptions). Once situated, they do stay where they are suppose to, although you need to make sure you are using the correct sized earphone tips. The medium sized silicone tips that came pre-installed on the earphones fit me just fine and held the earphones firmly in place. However, I am currently using a pair a medium sized comply foam tips (they did not come with the earphones), since they are comfortable and hold the earphone securely in place (and create a better seal than silicon tips IMO).Before I get into sound quality, this is an element that is very subjective. Although I am not a hardcore audiophile, I care about sound quality and have invested in some well respected headphones and IEMs (Grado S80s, Audio Technica ATH-M50X, Shure SE425, Tin Audio T2, KZ AOS10 etc).. I tend to like a earphones that have a fairly neutral sound, but are capable of generating tight controlled base (which can be brought out using the equalizer on your phone or tablet). These are NOT neutral sounding earphones. In fact, it sounds to me as if Tozo designed these earphones with a fixed EQ setting that overly emphasizes bass (particularly in the lower and mid range). This causes the bass to bleed over into the mids. If you ever listened to a 5.1 surround sound system where the subwoofer's crossover is set too high and at too high of a volume (creating that booming/overwhelming sound), these earphones have that effect. There is also some sibalence in these earphones--far from the worst I heard, but it is definitely present (especially for female vocals). These are earphones that are tuned for bassheads, but the 8mm drivers used by Tozo don't have the chops to really deliver clean quality bass. Tozo would have been better off not trying to put a fixed EQ setting in these earphones and left them with a balanced setting, which anyone can tweak to their taste using the EQ on their phone.In short, these are NOT the best sounding earphones I have ever heard. The sound quality is on par with a set of wired Philips Extra Bass headphones that you can buy for $15 at CVS or Rite Aid (which is they type of earphone that most people use). What makes them special is the fact that for $39 ($27 if you got them on a lightening deal), ,these earphones are rugged TWB earphones that are waterproof, come with a waterproof case that also serves as wireless charging base, and is, itself, capable of being charged with a Qi wireless charger. They aren't going to replace my Shure SE425, but they will compete with my superior sounding Tin Audio T2 (which I have connected via an MMCX Fiio bluetooth cable) for time at the gym and certainly during the summer at the beach and pool. I don't have much experience with other TWB earphones, but it doesn't seem like Tozo's competitors are offering anything significantly better in the sub-$50 range--so this is about as good as it gets for now (I am sure things will be much different in a year or two).

title: Comfortable Airpod-like Earbuds for Android
body: Despite the fact that I have several wired "wireless" bluetooth headphones for my devices, I have friends who use Airpods with their iPhones, and it looked rather convenient to have such a small device ready at the moment's notice while out and about. So I decided to shop around and see what I could find. This is my first foray into a device of this style, you know - completely wireless, but so far I am very pleased with them.My first concern was size. Airpods have that little stick portion that hangs out of the ear, and there are several Android styles similar to that, but I have a few pairs of Skullcandy earbuds that use this model's offset ear canal design, and I find them far more comfortable than older, straight line earbud designs. The TOZO earbud is a good size, and the weight is very comfortable in the ear. It is not difficult, even with how small they are, to pick them out of the case, or to fit them into the ear when needed. The directions included make it easy to understand how to use them, but if you fit the right size cups, and then insert and twist like you are told, you will have a tough time shaking the buds loose. That said, I still bought the Amazon 3 Yr Warranty, it's $5, just in case something happens, better than shelling out another $40.OF NOTE: I read several reviews of people who didn't seem to understand the interchangeable ear cups. The fact is that everybody has different size ear canals, so you have to take the time upfront to try out each size cup in your own ear to find the best fit, then use that pair of cups on your buds. If you, like my wife, don't like ANYTHING in your ears, then this is NOT the product for you, so don't buy it and then review it like it should be an over-ear headphone.My second concern was sound quality. I am definitely a techie, and an audiophile, though not to the level of snobby hipster, if I say so myself. The TOZO buds play my Amazon music from my phone (LG G8) with good clarity and nice sound, but I had to play with my phone's equalizer to shift the tonal quality around, remove some of the highs because it was a bit tinny on the normal phone settings. Once I shifted it around, I found that the Hip Hop EQ gave the best sound, no matter what music style I was listening to (in case you are curious, I tried some rock with Hoobastank and Avenged Sevenfold, some country with Sam Hunt, and then R&B with Tank and Ne-yo, and it sounded excellent across that entire spectrum in Hip Hop EQ). If your device has EQ settings, I absolutely recommend you adjust those to find your musical sweet spot! The only thing that I took one star for in this area is that, even with volume for my buds turned up to 100% on my phone, the buds themselves did not thrash my eardrums, which is probably a good thing, saving my volume for my old age, but sometimes, you need to headbang to death metal and want it LOUD. Considering the size of the buds speaker driver, they are adequately loud, and with it fitted snugly in the ear canal, it does a great job of reducing external noise. Totally plan on using these for when I am riding my mower or working in my wood shop!Next: Bluetooth connectivity. The pairing process is super easy, because the button design is simple. Unlike some of the other buds on Amazon, these are not "touch-style" but have a mechanical "click" button. Fantastic! Nothing worse than something tapping your bud in the middle of your favorite jam and then having to stop whatever you are doing to fix it. Some people noted that TOZO has R or L models, but the reality is that you can use either bud independently. The model choice just assigns the default earbud, because some people have an ear preference. If you want to use either one, then take the time to actually pair both in your bluetooth menu, and then you just have to pick the active one if it doesn't sync automatically. Handy when the primary bud is dying in the middle of a phone call!
