Lets face it on Wish you get a LOT of stuff that claims to be Bluetooth 4.0 that’s really BT 2.0 or something.I was worried about these Earbuds. But these? These are so great.They arrived beautifully and simply packaged. Though there were a couple of tiny errors in the English instructions, they were small, and did not in any way make the instructions un-readable. They were the kind of mistakes anybody could make because English is weird.The Earbuds were already all charged up, or very close to the top anyway, and that was great, I could try them out right away.In about 1 minute, I was already up and running on my phone.Then, just for fun I decided to see if I could (since they’re BT5) pair them with my Apple TV because I was watching a horror movie and didn’t want the screams echoing through the house.BAM! Worked first time, no problem. I sat here and watched a whole movie with the buds in and they didn’t itch, or make my ears sweat I hardly noticed them.I don’t have fantastic hearing. Its okay, but I’m not the guy who is looking for the ability to hear Luciano Pavarotti’s intestinal bacteria working in my ears, you know? That’s what some people want but for me I just near good volume, decent bass and synch’d sound with video and between the two buds.All of that was fantastic, they won’t fall out, and they are comfy enough that my French Bulldog even wore one for about 5 minutes (we share a lot) and he is SUPER SENSITIVE about his ears, doesn’t like them touched at all if he can avoid it. He thought they were really great. :-)The only tiny really insignificant issue I had is that I ordered the “blue” ones, I love the color, but on the blue ones the “R” and “L” indicators are REALLY hard to see. I had to use my phone’s flashlight and a magnifying glass to see which was which. I marked the right one in a place you can’t see when they’re in so I won’t have that problem any more.All in all I highly recommend these you cannot beat the price, and they work just great.

title: Nice basic BT earbuds at a low price
body: I use these primarily for podcasts while I'm walking, so my primary criteria isn't music quality, but listening now, it sounds very nice. Enough bass and highs and the midrange is nice.The charging case is a little big, but that's probably a reflection of the battery size, which makes it fine.I felt I should write this review since I had to give a bad 1 star review to Tozo's T12 ear buds, which also sounded very nice, but the touch controls were endlessly malfunctioning, which made them unusable.Compared to the T12's, the charging case isn't quite as informative (T12 had a numeric representation of battery remaining), but the 5 LEDs on the T10 case are totally sufficient. And the semi-transparent top cover allows you to see the status of charging (both buds and case) with the cover closed, which is clever and again, totally sufficient.I typically walk 90min to 2hrs and so far, the charge on the buds has held up for this duration, which is good for my needs. The fact that the charging case supports wireless charging is great and a big selling feature to me vs having to mess with small connectors. It links to my charger instantly and charges quickly.The IPX8 waterproof rating gives me confidence I can get these as wet as I'd like with no issues, even though I haven't tested that feature out.Some have complained in the low star ratings that there are connection issues. These are different than the T12's in that the right bud links to your device via BT and the left seems like a "slave" that links to the right, but I've only had an issue once where there was a phasing/delay effect where they were slightly out of sync which made for some echoing sound. But I just disconnected and reconnected and all was good. And it only happened once.As far as noise cancellation, it's passive and dependent on how tightly they fit within your ear canal. Even fitting tightly, some noise will get through, but overall it's fine and I gave it a 4/5 on that metric.In summary, I find these great for my needs, and given the nice features (informative status of case/bud charging, nice battery life on both, IPX8 waterproof status, wireless charging support), this is easily a 5-star product for the price.The thing is, given the small battery within the ear buds themselves and the inevitable wear-out of batteries when charged over a certain # of times and frequently, it makes all the sense in the world to get lower priced BT ear buds like this (vs Apple AirPods that sell for $150-250). In either case, if you use them daily, they'll likely wear out after 1.5-2yrs, and I'd much rather have spent $25-50 than $150-250 if I'm going to have to replace them every couple of years.The only potential concern I have is reliability, but I will just have to wait and see on this. But for now, I love these and they totally check off all the boxes as far as needs/wants on features as well as performance.

title: Incredibly happy with these earbuds!
body: I'm immensely pleased with this purchase. I've had trouble in the past finding earbuds that were comfortable in my ears (especially anything Apple-made). These earbuds are comfortable and snug, and it includes different-sized heads to ensure you find a right fit.Given the price compared to the features included in this product, I was worried there would be obvious sacrifices in audio quality. Nope! I'm no audiophile, but the sound is crisp. I've previously bought cheaper wireless headphones on Amazon in the $20 range, and there's a world of difference between these high-quality speakers and some of the cheaper options out there.I was I was in for a nice surprise when I realized that the case supports wireless charging. I lazily skimmed the product listing before purchase, so I assumed the charging case wireless charged the earbuds and thought "cool, whatever." But it's much better than that: the case itself can be placed on a wireless charging pad, which charges the case and the headphones. Neat! It works great with my Samsung wireless charger, and I'm about to purchase a Galaxy S10 phone which advertises that it can wirelessly charge *other* devices, so I'm optimistic that I'll be able to charge these headphones from my cell phone in a pinch!Another worry I had was that I've owned products that charge using magnetism to pull exposed contacts together, and sometimes you have to line it up *just* right to make contact and start charging. Not a problem with these earbuds! Each headphone is pulled snugly into its slot with a satisfying "click", and I've had a true 100% success rate with the metal contacts making a connection.The carrying case is a bit bulky, but it includes a built-in 600 mAh battery (along with the wireless charging hardware) which has to go somewhere. Can't complain on this front.How's the battery life? Not sure. I have yet to push the limits of my battery since it's simply so convenient to charge these. I largely just use these for 1 hour or less and then return them to the case. No problems on that front.Each earbud has a tactile button that can long-press to power off, or short-press to play/pause on the connected device. I do wish the media control could've been taken a short step further (skipping to the next song is the main use I have in mind, and volume control would be great). Oh, and double-clicking a button will redial the last called number. This is probably something I will literally never want to do, so it seems like an accidental call waiting to happen. Luckily the buttons have some nice tactile resistance, so it seems unlikely that I'll accidentally double-click.Finally, the pairing process is as easy as I could ask for. The state of the pairing process is announced in clear, plain English over the earbuds as things get connected (instead of forcing you to rely on obscure patterns of flashing light like some other devices I've used).

title: Reference this review if you are looking to use these for Exercise (Long story short, it's worth it)
body: I am a 5'8", 210 lbs male that exercises frequently and varies their type of workouts. I powerlift, olympic lift, bodybuild, and run.I wanted to run a full workout with these earbuds before I made this review.Today, I started my workout at 4PM and ended at 6:00PM with a full charge on the earbuds. I squatted, deadlifted, clean and jerked, bench pressed, and ran at a moderate speed with these earbuds on. Here are some things to note:1. The buds are noise-cancelling to a degree. I had mid-range volume on my Google Pixel 3a and I could somewhat hear the background noise, but turning the volume slightly higher, the noise gets drowned out. It also helps that if you're playing something with bass, it helps with drowning out the noise around you.2. Sound quality was pretty clear. The bass was deep and handled my techno and rap music. I tested it with rock and the sound was on par with some of my friend's more expensive headphones.3. Battery Life - My android can tell me the amount of charge on the earbuds and by the end of the workout, I had 70% of battery life. This was with me playing music through the whole workout at varying volumes. The manual claims up to 3 hours of battery life, but I believe you can squeeze more juice out of it.4. I tested the claim about being able to shower with it, yeah it works. I took a shower in the gym locker room with them on, they pretty much held up against the water. Just don't get water inside your ear or else you won't be able to ear the earbuds well.5. Squat, bench, and deadlift didn't have my head jerking around too much, so the earbuds stayed in really well. I was worried the deadlift would cause them to fall out but they actually stayed in (read the manual when you get them to put the earbuds on correctly so they don't fall out).6. Clean and Jerk and Running: My head jerked a lot with these movements. My left ear bud kept on getting a little loose, but I assume that I didn't put in right size for my left earbud piece. My right ear bud stayed in the whole time, with minor adjustments here and there. When running on the treadmill, I could hear the earbuds bouncing in my ear, but when I moved to another treadmill with a different type of tread (AKA Assault Air Runner), I didn't hear them bounce, so it all depends on the equipment. These buds will slip out if you have water or sweat in the area where the earbud lodges so keep that in mind!7. If you're talking with someone on the phone with these, you can only do it in relatively quiet areas because I was in a busy restaurant and the buds were picking up on the background more than my voice when I was talking to someone on video chat.Other than all of what I said above, this is a great product, my very first completely wireless bluetooth headphones and for $32, I am not disappointed.Give them a try!

title: Best BT Earbuds I've ever owned!!!
body: First, I've owned 5 pairs of BT headphones/earbuds over the years and all of them were underwhelming in terms of battery life and reception, regardless of my case or phone, they were consistently annoying.This pair, however, is AMAZING! I've had them for a few days now and can't find anything I don't like. They VERY rarely have an audio hiccup/skip, regardless of where my phone is (my old ones would hiccup if I just bent over and my belly blocked my phone).The range is outrageous! I have an old-style 2-bedroom flat that's really long and left my phone in the front of the apartment, then walked almost to the back of my apartment before the earbuds even had a slight hiccup at the kitchen door (all of my old ones would break up if I walk out of the same room as my phone).They're small and well-weighted, so they don't bounce around as you walk and work their way out of the ear canal. The silicone tips are great and I'm really happy they included a intermediate sizes, since the size that's just a little smaller than the ones that come pre-installed make them stay in my ears even better.I was super impressed by the audio quality, as well. I get good bass, excellent mid-range, and nice treble, with absolutely no tinny sound. Everything is crystal clear. If you're concerned about how they sound, try the other silicone ear pieces they included, so you can find the right one for your ear, as with all in-ear devices, the silicone ear piece will have a tremendous impact on your audio quality. If they're not staying in, sound weird, or are uncomfortable, try another size!I was concerned about the touch controls, vs physical buttons, but I've adjusted to them. This is actually my only (and excessively minor) gripe about these earbuds! I occasionally accidentally pause my music or turn my volume up/down. It took a little practice to get used to the touch control, but it's truly not bad at all once you get the hang of it.They were at 90% charge when I got them and paired very easily with my mid-tier phone. After about 3 hours, I was only down to 80% charge, which was impressive! I also love that the moment I pull them out of the case, they automatically start pairing with my phone, without me having to press a button or do anything. It's seamless and far easier than I was expecting (and far better than any previous BT earbud I've used).Also, since I've seen a review or two about the case... it's a magnetically closed box... Don't go putting it on your keychain or the outside of your backpack! I know it comes with a little strap for the loop, but that doesn't mean you should go trusting a magnet to hold your earbuds in when you go slamming this against door frames or dropping the case or pull it out attached to your keychain. It's obviously not THAT kind of case.I highly recommend these earbuds to everyone!

title: I recommend buying TWO pairs!
body: These bluetooth earbuds are a very good product -- for the price point, I'd say excellent. I found a handy workaround for my one significant complaint: you can listen for an hour or so, and then you have to charge for a while (from the case's big battery to the earbud's small battery) before you can keep listening. I found this annoying for when outdoors doing yard work for several hours. My workaround is to buy TWO PAIRS. I set aside the left earbud from each pair, and I use only the right earbud from each pair. I keep them together in one little case. When my first "R" earbud's battery drains, I put it into the case and I switch to the second "R" earbud. I can ping-pong back and forth this way for 4 or 5 hours. (If I were willing to carry both cases in my pocket, I could use two whole pairs, but I prefer to carry around only one case.)If I could change one feature of this earbud, I would allow allow one to use "L" only in the same way as one can use "R" only. Then my ping-pong trick would work without buying a second pair.The next feature I would change would be the button functions. I often use a single short-press to pause and restart my podcast when I need to focus on the here-and-now. But if I accidentally long-press, I wind up auto-redialing whomever I happened to have most recently called on my phone, which can be surprising and awkward. If there were a way to make long-press instead do "back 10 seconds" or something similar, that would be a win for me.For a long time, I was annoyed that even after I had put the earbuds away in their charging case, they would still pair with my phone if I happened to be nearby, even when obviously not in my ears, since they were in their little case. But I recently realized that you can long-press while in the charging case to explicitly power the buds off, avoiding that problem.If I had paid well over $100 for a pair of genuine airpods, I would be worried about losing them and would thus use them much less. For around $40 per pair (my first pair was $30 on special and my second pair was $40), I can feel a bit more cavalier about wearing them outdoors.One final comment: I once tried using these while swimming laps in the pool. It didn't work. With my head underwater, they went out of range very quickly. With my head above water (doggie paddle or maybe kickboard), they stayed in range for about 10 meters or so, roughly half the length of the pool. So probably I could have put my phone in a sandwich bag on the edge of the pool at mid-length and done kickboard for a while, but alas these are of no use for swimming freestyle. This is a shame, since many podcasts are much easier to get via smartphone app than via mp3 download. (I have an underwater mp3 player that I normally use for swimming; it works only for whatever you can download in mp3 format of course.)

title: UPDATED COMPANY REACHED OUT 5 Star Customer Service!! Ordered 2 pair, 1 defective, 2nd didn't last
body: UPDATED 4/21/2020 Received the upgraded earbuds, the T6. After using them over the last few weeks I find they are a great product for this price point and even higher price points. I actually prefer the way these fit and really like the smaller charging case. They seem to last a while between charges. The smaller charging box makes it easier to take these in your pocket or purse. I recommend both but would order the T6 again. I WILL UPDATE IF THEY DO NOT LAST. Appreciate this companies customer service.UPDATED 3/2020 The company reached out to me via email this week after reading my 1st review. They apologized and made 2 offers, a refund, or an upgraded pair. I chose the upgraded earbuds, and will update on them once I get them and use them.I TRULY APPRECIATE that this company listened to my feedback. Great Customer Service paired with ensuring customer satisfaction is lacking in much of today's marketplace. I thank them for reaching out with 2 very acceptable options. They care about their ratings, and that's a win for their customers. Will update on new set soon. But so far, 5 STARS for Customer Service .I ordered two pair, one for myself and one for my spouse, at the same time. One set arrived with a defective bud, so I returned it.I kept the other pair, and truly enjoyed them, until this week. I waited awhile after purchase to do a full review, because of how one arrived, and to see if they lasted more than a few weeks/months.Initially they sounded well for wireless buds in this price range and higher. They paired quickly, and easily allowed me to just pair one bud at a time, if I wanted a solo bud option.The battery base itself recharges quickly, but the buds, and their charge base, do not last as long as the pair from another name brand ($100) I had for over a year.I'm glad I wait to give my review, because one earbud started to periodically cut in and out while in use.I'm disappointed. These were a good price point, and felt they were a good replacement for my lost ones. I was happy enough with them up until recently, to consider ordering myself a 2nd pair to keep in my purse.It's a shame, because I had referred others in my circles to purchase them. I hope they have a better experience with longevity.Please give them some time of use before giving a review, or at least update your review if you run into issues with them. I definitely leaned into this purchase more because of the positive reviews.My husband ended up with the Cowin E7 wireless headphones, while they're too bulky for my needs, they're wonderful, and have held up well, the sound, battery life, range, and noise cancellation, out perform his Bose he has had for 2yrs.I hope companies start standing behind the quality of their products. To not last even a year is a shame.

title: 3hr battery is better than 5hr!
body: So I got a pair of these for using at work. I do remodel/construction so I move around a lot and it can get noisy. I was using wired ear buds running the wire inside my shirt to my phone in my back pocket.. of coarse they always ended up failing at the jack where you have bend the wire just right to get them to work. Plus it isn't always practical to have a phone in your back pocket getting sweaty, dirty, crawling through an attic etc.. and you can't put your phone aside for a couple minutes and still use your wired ear buds. So I ended up buying these on a lightning deal from Amazon. As an aside, they arrived the very next day which always amazes me about Amazon. Anyways, I opened the charging case and took them out and they connected in seconds, no connect buttons to push no hesitation, just bam there they were. So I brought them to work the next day for their first real test and by the end of the day I wanted another pair of these. I listen to am talk radio all day practically. My first show starts at 9 a.m. and ends at 12 next is 12 to 3 and then 3 to 6. Yes they have 3 hour battery time but that's actually better in my opinion for me all day at work. The thing is I turn them on at 9 exactly and 3 hours and literally 9 minutes later I got my first warning that the batteries needed to be charged. Exactly what they promised. The reason I think this is better is because for this price compared to most other bluetooth earbuds I can buy two sets of these and then when one gets low on battery I put them in the charger and pop in the ones that are already charged and I never have any down time while I recharge. Most of the wireless earbuds I looked at have a 4 to 6 hour battery time which doesn't last a whole day at work so I would have to stop listening while they charged and usually they cost more than two pairs of these oh, so that's why I believe having two sets with 3 hour battery life is better than one with five or six hours worth. Like I said I listen mostly to talk radio and as for the sound quality it's great but seems a little bass-heavy heavy and the base is kind of thick rather than clear bass. But I haven't given them a chance yet really with music so I can't make a fair judgement on that. Also they do not have active noise-cancelling they act more like earplugs to handle noise. I think they did a good job of blocking noise and that regard I had them on while a saw was running and still heard clearly. So like I said I just bought a second pair and I look forward to another review after having them for a week and I can better tell how the microphone phone calls and music work out. But I can already say for sure I'm happy with this purchase and I think they're a great deal and serve my needs perfectly.

title: Great Quality Headphones
body: UPDATE 5/14/20So I have had these earbuds for two weeks now, and honestly, they're still holding up wonderfully. My husband loves his pair, though he had some trouble using the tips and getting them to fit properly. Still would highly recommend!___________My husband had a pair of wireless headphones that lasted... 3 weeks? They were trash, paid about $35-40 for them.I wanted to replace them for him and stumbled upon these with their roughly 40k reviews. Wow!I did tons of research and reading and most everything I saw people were so impressed by these earbuds. So I took the plunge and bought 2 pairs for my husband and I. I got white and black. They got here in a few days and I was really anxious to try mine out. I've taken up running and have a corded wireless ear set that pop out of my ears all the time. I wanted to put these to the test and let me say, they held up beautifully!The one step connection was true, so simple to connect them to my phone. The fit was good, I had to use the smallest silicon fits they had.They arrived packaged wonderful, and look so professional. They are a little large so there's some poking out, but I find that to be a non-issue.I put them in my ear and they sound absolutely great for the price you're paying! I loved the audio quality. Maybe some bassheads or extremely sound savy individuals could pick it apart, but I was absolutely pleased. I'd put them close to a pair of Skullcandy headphones I had a few years back. Definitely on par. I haven't used them in long enough intervals to speak to the battery, but it's lasted me.They have an 18 month warranty inside the package you go to register within 14 days of receiving. I went on and there was the offer for a case or charger. I thought that was nice, I'm not sure how it's redeemed or the process for it.My husband has yet to try his out, but I'd love to update with his opinion later on.I tested them on phone calls, hubby says I sounded loud and clear. So phone calls are good. Noise cancellation works well, we have two AC units in our home that are almost entirely blocked out when I have them on.The one con I've had is the double click for redial. It doesn't make sense to make that an option in my humble opinion. However, they are so great in every other aspect it's hardly worth mentioning. I love my set and would highly recommend them to just about anyone else.I've yet to test them against water but I can update with that later as well.The true test I want to measure is how long they last for us. Because like I said, the other pair were out in less than a month.If you're looking for a sign, here it is! They're worth the money!

title: Final Update - These things rock.
body: FINAL UPDATE (#2): I am updating this review after getting to examine a 3rd pair of earbuds and finding that they were completely trouble free. I have kept all previous reviews below for completeness sake. Quick version: I got a bad pair, but I was quickly sent a replacement, and have used a 3rd pair. The 2nd and 3rd pair worked flawlessly (the first pair were clearly not the “normal” quality) so I feel compelled to update my review based on this. I also discovered that if you have a wireless charger (like one you use with your phone), you can rest the charging case on that wireless charging pad and they charge! That’s so convenient. I am also finding them to be very comfortable and I enjoy the sound. Great for the gym!UPDATE 1: TOZO contacted me by email and sent me a new pair of earbuds. These earbuds indeed work fine and don't have any issues at all. I think TOZO understands the importance of reviews and very much wants people to give them good reviews. I would say they are even a bit aggressive about their customer service. They were always incredibly polite and professional, but they wanted to be sure I got the second pair to try. I can appreciate their hard work in trying to make sure that I walked away with good things to say about the product. I have now given this 4 stars because the final product works great, but the fact that I got one that seems to be way too susceptible to interference won't allow me to give it 5 stars in good conscience.Bottom line:- first product worked well except for drops - but they were so frequent in certain places it was maddening- second product worked like a charm and does not seem to have any of the dropping issues- they want to be sure you are happy so the customer service was lightning fast and with no questions asked- If you are looking for a relatively inexpensive pair of Bluetooth wireless earbuds, give these a try.ORIGINAL REVIEW: They pair easily, have a nice charging case, and seem to have a good battery life. Unfortunately, they drop signal 3-4xs per minute. I did a bit of troubleshooting to make sure it was the earbuds and not the device. I paired it with several devices and the earbuds always dropped signal. I then paired all of the devices with several other bluetooth headphones and speakers I have (including an Echo). All of the devices played flawlessly through all of the speakers without dropping signal. However, these drop regularly. Sometimes it will go for a minute or two without dropping, sometimes certain places seem to make it worse, but, overall, it happens several times per minute. It makes an otherwise decent pair of earbuds unusable. I will return these.

title: Seriously Impressed with TOZO T10 Wireless Earbuds!
body: I was originally going to buy a pair of these for my wife as a present. She has an iPhone and didn't want the Airpod design. She wanted something that would not hang down the side of her ear. But, when I saw these, and read the reviews, I wanted a pair for myself as well. So, I bought 2 sets. My only regret was I bought 2 white sets and I would have preferred black for me (my fault). Okay, back to the review. First, the packaging is first class! Beautiful box which contained the charging case, earbuds, bud caps (small, medium, large, extra-large), carrying strap for the charging case, charging cable (for connecting to the charging case and a USB port). Also included are well-written instructions. I'm listening to music through them now as I write this review. I do not yet know the length of usage time before I'll need to recharge. When I first opened the package, I was first impressed with the way they look - not just the styling, but their claim that these are waterproof. It's pretty obvious they are (although I haven't tested that feature) as they appear to be a sealed design. I do know having the correctly-sized earbud caps will affect the sound quality. Great for them for providing all the choices! I tried the ones that came installed on them, but felt they were too small for me, so I removed them and put on another set. After trying a couple different sets (provided), I found the ones that are comfortable and provide quality sound. I've paired this set of earbuds to my Samsung S8 (Android) phone, as well as my laptop (Dell Precision 7720). Pairing was SO EASY! The earbuds first pair with themselves, then wait for you to initiate pairing with a device. After they pair with themselves, they tell you (in your ear) "Left earbud paired", "Right earbud paired" - or something like that. Even though they are labeled on the outside of them with "L" and "R" for Left and Right, respectively, it's really nice to know that you'll be listening to your music with the proper left and right channels. Noise reduction / cancelling wasn't tested too much. However, I can say that it is definitely reduced. As for music sound, it's crisp and clear. Bass response is decent. Getting a good "seal" with the earbud caps will provide a better bass response. I've already purchased 2 sets. I may purchase another 2 sets. These are that good! For those concerned that these are made in China, don't be. I live in the United States and the instructions were in various languages. The English section of the manual was excellently written. The voice, from my earbuds is in English and easy to understand. To the folks at TOZO store, I thank you! This is a great product!

title: Unbelievable!!
body: I am an iPhone SE 2020 owner who lost my airpods (which I came across at Black Friday sale at an incredible price), Gotta say, when I bought a replacement set the sticker shock hurt! The fact that the 1st set was lost made me reluctant to really use the 2nd set (a side note: they are not comfortable AND tend to come out when putting my face mask on or taking it off).I went in search of of something that if lost would not make me want to cry, I tried a couple different brands, One pair in-particular had great sound quality and worked well with my iPhone, however regardless of the tip used, they wouldn't stay in my ear. I've spent too much time picking them up off the floor in my car or off the ground after they'd fall getting in and out of the car. As a musician who has to record music parts remotely using click tracks, comfort is VERY important and being perfectly honest, my Airpods are not really comfortable after a few minutes.I put my search to the side until one evening last week, while strolling the blackhole of Facebook, I came across an ad for the TOZO T10. I spent two days with the browser window open to the earbud site, open but not buying. I read review after review. I was determined not to make an impulse buy. I found a discount that brought the price down to $20, A price I found both unbelievable and unpassupable (I know it's not a word, but hey).I pulled the trigger! A decision I don't regret!They arrived nearly fully charged so I could get started right away. Super easy to pair, thank God because the instructions are written in itty-bitty print that will surely have me making an appointment with my optometrist.Here's what I found:1. The sound quality is AMAZING!!! Deep rich bass, highs that are not scratchy but nice and clear.2. Called my daughter to check the call quality and again crystal clear, I then set my phone on my desk and took a stroll through my house while on the call and was surprised that the call didn't drop, even when I reached the other side of my home and was sitting on my bed, As someone who is now working from home this is important.3. Popped them in and hit the elliptical loading my podcasts. Nothing amiss, amazing!4. Took a ride to the gas station to top off the tank before the weekend price increase. Threw on my mask and they didn't fall out.To say I'm a happy camper is an understatement! Price, quality, comfort all at very least compatible with my Airpods. I could buy 6 pair of these for the price of 1 pair of Airpods I forgot to mention the battery life is longer than my 2nd gen Airpods.TOZO you nailed it with these earbuds!!!!!

title: Great sount quality, amazing battery life, best notice canceling earbuds. Price can't be beat!
body: So I'm not one to usually leave reviews for products as I like to give it time before really decided on how I feel about a product. I am gladly giving the TOZO T10 bluetooth headphones a solid 5 out of 5. For the last few years I've been burning through LG bluetooth headphones as the earbuds usually stop working after a year or so. After 4 pairs I finally gave up and decided to look for a next gen replacement. I know wireless pods are the big new thing so when I saw the price I had to try it. What was there to lose for $30. I was blown away.Noise Cancelation: first off this was the big shocker. I've had headphones that claim to be noise cancelling but really aren't. These are almost alittle too good at canceling out sound. I work in a warehouse and a lot of the time I can barely hear what I'm listening to since we can only wear one at a time for safety reasons. Another boon for this product is the ability to solo sync the headphones. If I can't listen to both then I can pop just one in and boom instant connection and audio enjoyment. 5/5Sound quality: at first I had to dial in my phones audio settings as I had them adjusted to my previous headset (the lg bluetooth ones) after I got the sounds dialed in it was amazing. Great base without being too much to distort the audio but just enough for that warm audio sound. Best part is if you only want to use one pod you still get great stereo sound out of just one pod. Add the other and you can literally hear them balance the audio for even distribution of sound. That is a feature I have yet to find in other headsets. 5/5Battery life: what isnt to like about the battery life with these. If they are getting low just pop em in the case and boom they start charging and they charge fast. Even when just charging the case it was under an hour and I was good to go without fear of them dying on me. Best thing for me since typically only use one at a time at work. If one dies on me I can pop the other in and still listen uninterrupted while I charge of the other. Last coolest feature is that I can pop the case onto my wireless Samsung charging pad and these will quick charge wire free. 5/5My sister in law had just bought the raycon version of these for $120. Yeah $90 more than these and literally they are identical though to be honest the raycon has too much base that is unavoidable which makes the TOZO T10 the clear winner in a tech comparison.If you are thinking of getting bluetooth pods make sure the TOZO T10 are your first choice.(Disclaimer: I am not sponsored nor do I have any affiliation with TOZO. This is an honest true happy customer review)

title: I like it, not without problems however.
body: Overall I am quite satisfied with this product. Here are some comments to aid your decision on whether or not to buy it:Good:It definitely is waterproof. I've used it in water quite a few times now and they still work great.Sound quality is great, it has bass, I don't listen to music loud enough to notice however.They fixed static crackle that bluetooth sound devices suffer from. It still occasionally loses connection for a moment if position it wrong, but instead of an annoying static blast it just goes quiet.(For the physics people among us, I'm assuming if you accidentally position your head in just the right position to get it into the anti-node of the signal, the signal is gone and normal devices just create static, but this one probably has some clever filtering that just has no sound instead of having terrible sound, which I can appreciate)I have used this item for a few months now and its still working like new.Bad:The case is bigger in real life than in the picture. Not by a lot, but then again, they need to fit in wireless charging for less than 100 dollars in addition to providing the earbuds.They decided to go with the voice prompts instead of just a small sound effect. The voice is at least a regular volume, but a quick bloop will get the same information across much faster.In the same vein, the battery life notification is terrible. It makes more sense to have a quick notification say, 30 minutes before its about to run out, and maybe a couple more 5 minutes before it dies.This thing will suddenly shout BATTERY LOW PLEASE CHARGE. And then you think you have a little bit. LITERALLY seconds later, it will repeat itself, and maybe a maximum of 5 minutes after you've received your initial notification, the device runs out of power.Button functionality isn't great. Yes, they push with ease. However, when listening to music, you can only stop and start. There's no way to skip or raise or lower volume.You can't hold down a button for a second or something in order to get google assistant on android like some other devices can do.Here's what it can do.If you double press a button, it will redial.Yes, a feature that I use all the time. (Sarcasm)I recommend not giving the device access to your call records, since I doubt you'll need this "feature".In fact, not allowing the bluetooth device access to your call records will still redial the number you called last if you double press a button by accident. It doesn't happen super often, but I still think its a stupid feature to add, especially since it can't be disabled.

title: Great sound, small battery (updated 7/21/2021)
body: Update 7/21/2021: I have received my replacement a few days after my February review and I have to say that the updated earbuds I received are even better than the original ones. The battery seems to last longer (haven't really measured it), sound is just as good, they pair quicker and easier, and they fixed the charging port (it is USB C now)!All in all I am very happy with these, especially for the price. (Updated my rating back to 5 stars.)(Update 2/9/2021: TOZO service has responded to my email. They requested that I send them a video of the product problem, which I did. After this they said that they will send a replacement, but I needed to provide a product review, a bit strange to say the least, but I already have a review, so I will be sending this to them and updating my rating to 4 stars.)(Update 2/5/2021: I still like these a lot, and they've held up really well, except for one part - the charging port. Now it is very lose and it is very hard to keep the cable in while it is charging. On top of this TOZO makes it very hard to submit a warranty claim. As of today, there is not a link to that on their website - it was there yesterday but did not appear to work. So, while I really like the product, you need to stand behind the warranty and I am not seeing that right now. I have sent an email to their support, we'll see if anyone answers.)Wireless headphones are a very personal choice. I went through half a dozen before I settled on Tozo T10. As with any headphones, especially the ones in this price range, there are going to be negatives, and it will depend how much you weigh each of their characteristics as to how you end up liking or disliking them.Here are the reasons I like them in order of importance (to me) :1. Sound quality2. Fit3. Value4. Range5. Ease of pairingHere are things I dislike about them:1. Baterry capacity2. Charging port (mini USB)4. SizeTo verbalize this a bit more, the sound is pretty great, especially for the money. Bluetooth range is great, pairing, and switching between left, right or both is super easy. They fit well, but are somewhat large.Battery capacity, both for the earpieces and the case is below average. I assume the idea is to make up for this with wireless charging, but I don't have a charging pad and did not want to buy one just for these. Hence, I use the charging port and cable, which is USB mini and just about all my other devices are USB C.Overall I think these are a great buy for the money.

title: Great product for the price, Stellar customer service
body: Amazing customer service, provided replacement with no hassle.Great pair of earbuds, sound quality is good, overall feel of the product is very nice. Blocks out sound quite well for a product that's not active noise cancelling.I've always been resistant to the idea of getting earbuds because I'd lose them, and because I usually go for the wider earpieces that don't go directly into your ear canal since they ear canal pieces usually cause my ear to become sore (my ear canals are small). However, I haven't had this issue with these earbuds. The smallest rubber caps are perfectly sized, don't cause ear distress, and never fall out unless I really try to put them in as loosely as possible.Some things to nitpick:The day I bought this I tried seeing how they would handle direct spray from my shower head, it being IPx8 rated and all. First, as other reviewers had mentioned, the touch buttons are activated by water contact, meaning that even though these earbuds are water resistant, putting them in the direct trajectory of water spray or having too much water drip onto them will cause them to pause audio or change the volume, etc., which is not that ideal. Further, I found that after I tested the water resistance of the right earbud by putting it up against showerhead spray, the right earbud was still functional but played this faint continuous high pitched tone in the background that my left earbud didn't. I'm aware that IPx8 is rated for still water, but if anyone was curious to how that translated to shower performance, that's what it is.I really wish they would revert to using a manual button, because of the water sensitivity, because the touch button also sometimes takes a second or two to register, because you don't get tactile feedback on if you hit it "right", and also because you can't use the touch button with gloves on.The range of the bluetooth signal is noticeably shorter than other bluetooth devices I've used before. My other bluetooth earphones would be able to work from across the house, but these earbuds had difficulty transmitting two rooms away. Sometimes there would also be a tiny delay between the two sides when starting to play some audio.After 5 months of regular use in and out of the shower, my left earbud has started dying on me more frequently at around 60% battery. However, after unsuccessfully trying to resolve the issue with TOZO customer support, they offered to send me a new pair for free with no hassle. Their email replies were also very prompt. Overall, amazing customer service!

title: TWS done right
body: Every Christmas I usually have enough personal money to make an impulse purchase, usually in Electronics. This year, I wanted to upgrade the $20 pair of totally wireless sound (TWS) earbuds I had. They were good, but needed better sound to make them perfect.With a budget of about $100, I started off looking for something good from a known manufacturer (like Samsung, Jabra, etc.) There was just nothing out there in my price range. I couldn't see myself paying that kind of money from a brand I didn't know, so I started looking for a cheaper model. For around $50 I found 3-4 that looked good and reviews were good enough. I liked Anker's other Bluetooth earphones, so the Soundcore Liberty Lites were considered. With the good reviews and previous good ownership of Monoprice products the Monoprice IPX4s were looking good, too.Due diligence and a desire to be able to use one earbud at a time drive me to search Amazon's best rated under $50 TWS sets, and Tozo looked pretty good. While the buds are labeled L and R, they are both the same, and can be put into either ear. They come with 4 tips, and for the first time, I'm not using the largest ones. So if you have really big ear canals and find it hard to get tips that fit, you may find a good fit here.The sound won't knock Bose out of the water, but for the forty dollars I paid, it is good, and an outstanding upgrade from my $20 pair. It has nice bass, and well defined mids and highs.Battery time is right around 4 hours at low volume (I wear them at work as background noise). The travel case can charge one while the other is playing, so I don't find it hard to get through a work day with them. I like the charging case, too. It's well built, and can use a Qi wireless charger, which I don't care about really. There are 4 lights to tell you how charged it is. it closes and fits in a pocket. I can't tell you how many charges it will take, because I usually keep it plugged in.Pairing, once initially done, is easy. Just pull a bud off the charger and it will turn on and start looking for your paired device. Since I'm using one at a time, I find I need to turn the one I'm using off completely first, then get the other one. I paired them first together, and then separate. This is the best part of these for me, almost seamless use.All in all, very happy with the purchase. And I save up some money to get something else. I have already recommended these to people (my brother is not convinced, he has bone conducting headphones he likes). I do like them that much.

title: Exceeded expectations without a doubt!!
body: I am surprisingly pleased with the TOZO T10 Waterproof earbuds. I read about them in a tech article online that I stumbled upon and for the price, decided to go ahead and try them out. I also find Apple earbuds to be incredibly uncomfortable and the price, therefore, ridiculous.My brother and son both have the most up-to-date Apple wireless EarPods. While they are the “in” thing and the design a bit more pleasing to the eye, I think the sound quality kind of sucks because they don’t fit into my ears comfortably. Therefore, I never get clear sound or noise cancellation. I also can never get enough sound because of the fit. If I’m going to pay upwards of $250 for earbuds of ANY kind, that sound better be crisp, clear, loud enough, and the bass pounding to my liking. 😏The reviews in the article I initially read on the TOZO T10’s said the sound quality was actually better than Apple’s EarPods. Both my brother and son unhappily agreed after trying my TOZO’s out for a bit. Score. We are a family of people who are religious to iPhone’s so for them (and me, for that matter) to deviate in opinion, does say a lot.Upon the very quick arrival, it was so simple to pair the TOZO’s with my iPhone 11 Pro Max. SO simple. And every single time I’ve used them, with the exception of one when I was on a remote beach trying to use iHeart radio, I’ve always easily connected with music, calls, etc.My ONLY issue thus far was when I first received the earbuds. The box was to include the earbuds, wireless charging case, adjustable case strap, charging cord to connect the charging case to a charging block or charge center, and 4 different sizes of clear rubber ear caps such that you could find the best fit for your ears. My box was missing the different sizes of caps and just included the pair already on the earbuds. I immediately contacted the seller with the issue. They were very quick to respond. I had simply requested that they send the small baggie with the other ear caps. I returned home from vacation to find that they’d replaced the entire setup....earbuds, case and all. The customer service was fantastic.I’m now in the process of registering for my one-year warranty and no, this review was not solicited or part of any “deal”.Overall, unless you’re stuck on needing to look like everyone with an iPhone and need to have EarPods, I’d go above and beyond to suggest you ditch the discomfort and less than premium sound quality and get these. They exceeded my expectations more than any product I’ve purchased in years.

title: Nothing better out there for your money
body: I don't know that there's such a thing as a "Bluetooth expert" but I'm about as veteran as they come - I've used Bluetooth headsets regularly since before Bluetooth even existed on phones (yeah, when you had to have an adapter to plug into your old 3.5mm jack). We're talking nearly 20 years of experience using Bluetooth. But those are stories for another day.I'll cut straight to it - there is no better Bluetooth headset I've found below $50 that work as well as these. I'd even argue that they're better than most headsets over $100 I've used. Could the mic be better? Perhaps. But I admittedly use these 95% of the time to listen to music/podcasts/videos on my phone. If I really want to talk to someone on a BT headset, I use my plantronics set with superior noise cancellation (I can have running water in front of me while conversing and the other person just hears my voice loud and clear).These bad boys last a LONG time - we're talking 8+ hours - and they work one bud at a time so I can literally charge one while using the other and hours later when it's running low I simply swap one bud for the other, charging the dying bud with the fresh one and never skip a beat on whatever I'm listening to. One expensive BT bud brand, for example, only works in the left ear or both ears but the right ear doesn't work on its own. Why the hell would they design it this way?! Once your left bud runs out of juice, you cannot just swap to the right bud you have to wait for lefty to recharge. Horrible design...These also connect quickly and easily (some BT buds or headsets are a PAIN) and stay connected! They're also water proof - I wear them in the shower almost daily (have I mentioned how I listen to podcasts almost all day long?)I've tried a handful of "cheap" BT buds with very similar design/price range and this one outperforms them all with all things considered. And the case even charges wirelessly! Can't say that with half of the ones I've tried in this range. I've even tried the T6 and while I like it, I still prefer the T10 - the T6 charges in the T10 case but T10 doesn't charge in the T6.Really the only con I can think of - besides call quality perhaps - would be the sensitivity of the touch button on the unit. But I rarely have an issue accidentally activating it and the ease of using the touch button to turn on/off, connect, pause/play etc is far better than any other BT bud I've used. Some are so poorly designed you can only imagine the designer hates humanity and is getting his revenge on it...

title: Stop shopping and just buy these.
body: Fist off, why should you listen to me? I love music, I am a sound engineer, a musician, and someone who uses a different pair of wireless earbuds everyday. I bought these for my wife and we both love them. I was skeptical at first, my razor hammerhead earbuds cost $20 more and I consider them a steal at that price. Well this weekend the unimaginable happened and I left my wireless buds at work. Gasp. How would I live. My wonderful wife graciously offered to let me use hers for the weekend. I used these connected to my phone, my kindle fire, and my firestick. in the case of my firestick on Friday night I watch all of the two towers (extended edition).1) Sound. As someone who cares about how my headphones and earbuds sound. These get a 4 out of 5. The audio was great, crisp and responsive. I'm only taking a point off because the audio profile was slightly scooped and I like it more flat. (I will acknowledge that this is how most people like it and I shouldn't expect these to match up with my $300 westone or Ultimate Ears stage monitors) My joy was mostly because the bass wasn't extremely overdriven (I'm looking at you beat$ by Dre)2)Bluetooth. They worked right out of the box. Paired with both iOS and android devices. Just open the case stick em in your earholes and wait until it says paring, (if you don't hear it you may have to tap the side of the buds at the same time of something like that) and select them from the list of Bluetooth connections on your device.3)Battery life. I wasn't able to run these all the way down to the end but I did use them for 4 hours straight (the two towers extended edition is no joke) and the battery was only about halfway down.4)wear-ability. It's a good thing there are a R and L stamped on the side of each of these things because they are really not molded to your ear very well. When you get the bud down into your ear canal I had expected the rest of it (what I can only call the bulge) to nestle nicely into the outer ear, it almost gets there but kind of sticks out at a bit of an awkward angle. The tips that they sent with are the 'empty' silicone type where it kind of feels like a mushroom. These are alright and you can switch them out until you find the ones that fit. I would suggest looking in the compressible foam type for an even more comfortable and sound isolated experience, search for comply foam tips and make sure you get the right size where it connects to the ear buds.TL/DR buy these, they are worth more money that what they are charging. Stop shopping around and do it.

title: Comparison of 4 earphones for running
body: I have a lot of reviews I haven’t written yet, but since I’m buying earphones for a gift, and since I purchased some for my daughter, I’m sharing this review here and also in the other product pages (maybe it’ll help somebody).She wanted earphones for her daily running, with these features:1. Didn’t fall out2. Clear calls3. Bluetooth not cutting off while running (she has an iPhone)4. Option for allowing ambient noise (for safety, she runs outside)5. Faithful bass, not “boosted”. My grownup kids play instruments and prefer the closest sound to the original recording (that’s why we also have the Sony MDR7506 Professional}So I purchased four for running, waterproof, with (pro) reviews that also said they didn’t have “high-range”, tinny or high trebled sound. The high range is the first to go as we age.For the tests: A good acoustic seal provides maximum bass and the true sound of any earphones for assessment. The earphones need to be well plugged in your ears (I usually wear one large sleeve in one ear, and one medium sleeve in my narrower ear, in order to get the perfect seal).My daughter tested these while running (I did too, without running LOL):- Kept (her favorite) => Earfun Free Pro => 1 (didn’t fall, most comfortable) – 2 (great) – 3 (great) – 4 (great, note she didn’t test the active noise cancellation mode) – 5 (bass a little boosted, but great sound)https://smile.amazon.com/dp/B08LYXGB89- Kept as a backup (she loses everything) => Tozo T10 => 1 (great) – 2 (great) – 3 (great) – 4 (great) – 5 (faithful bass, high and mids)https://smile.amazon.com/dp/B07J2Z5DBM- Not kept => Anker Soundcore Life Dot 2 => (1 (fell off, bunky) – 2 (great) – 3 (great) – 4 (no option) – 5 (boosted bass, softer highs and mids)https://smile.amazon.com/dp/B086MZ9HQT- Kept (by me, not her) => Back Bay Runner Wireless => 1 (didn't fall but she didn't like the style over the ear) – 2 (great) – 3 (great) 4 (great) - 5 (little bass, but faithful highs and mids)https://smile.amazon.com/dp/B07KVMH6SWOther info: - All were loud, though loudness was not considered (we avoid loudness whenever we can to stall hearing loss). - We also tested calls at home between the first and second floor of our house, for Bluetooth connection and voice clarity, and all passed the test.- Their battery of all lasted at least from the time she left for work and came back late in the afternoon.

title: Great Bluetooth headphones and wonderful customer service!!!
body: I have always used the standard chorded earbuds, as I have always been picky about earbud comfortability/sound quality. My go-to have been the chorded Skull Candy ear buds, but after these broke (as they always do every couple of months), I decided I'd invest in a better 'bud.After sifting through Amazon, I concluded that TOZO were the best headphones I could buy on Amazon for the most affordable price, and purchased the TOZO 5.0 Wireless earbuds via Prime. After arriving, I was impressed by the design--the sleek case, the comfort and weight of the earbuds, and the way they "snapped" back into the case because of the magnetic force. And on top of that, I know I didn't have to worry about these earbuds getting ruined by water. After listening to some tunes, I was impressed by the sound quality, specifically the base. I've used the Apple Airpods and Airpods Pro, and the sound quality of the TOZO earbuds is quite competitive. No, it is not as good nor better, but I would say the TOZO earbuds were about 9/10 of the sound quality for 2/10 of the price of the Apple earbuds! Otherwise, I would say both the case design and earbud design are more appealing than the Apple earbuds.Everything was going good up until one day my right earbud unfortunately stopped working after I simply set it down. Nothing would work to fix the earbud (charging it in both the left and right input, blowing on/cleaning it...), so I reached out to TOZO for my free 1 year warranty.TOZO gave me some of the best customer service I have ever experienced from a company. Although they did take several days to respond, their first response stated that this was probably "a manufacturing defect" and gave me the option to (1) get the upgraded TOZO 6.0 wireless earbuds for free or (2) get a refund. I was pleasantly surprised, as whenever I usually try to utilize a warranty for a product, I am bombarded with requests of information and proof that my product is indeed defective. This was not that case, however, as the TOZO customer service simply asked for my shipping details to I could get the free pair of updated earbuds (the option I chose). And I must say, the TOZO 6.0 wireless earbuds are only an improvement of the 5.0 earbuds. The case is much smaller and sleeker, as well as the touch-sensitive earbuds providing more commands that I can do with the earbuds still in my ears. If you are short on money, the TOZO 5.0 earbuds will satisfy all your music needs but if you don't mind investing a little more, the TOZO 6.0 earbuds are definitely worth it.Thanks TOZO!

title: So Far - A Solid Shower Companion
body: THE SHORT: Perfect-for-the-price throw-around wireless waterproof earbuds. I've used these in the shower several times a week since I bought them 2 months ago and they're still going strong. Keep reading for more details.WATERPROOFING -I'd imagine you're looking at these because the IPX8 waterproof claim - There are countless cheap wireless earbuds on Amazon you could probably find a better deal on that look cooler or have better battery life, but you settled here because you've been wading through IPX7 buds looking for something that will actually work in water.The extent that I use them may be different from what you want: I use these in the shower to listen to music and podcasts - I am not careful with them, I let soapy water run all over them. Once or twice I've dropped them into the soapy water at my feet on accident. As of right now, two months after receiving them, I have not noticed any difference in the day I bought them.AUDIO QUALITY -It's passable. At low volumes it sounds just fine, but can start getting distorted once the volume gets higher. That's fine, they're budget-priced, they're buds, and they're wireless. If I want audio quality I've got plenty of nice over-ear headphones to use.BATTERY LIFE -They'll go for a few hours at a time on a charge before giving you a "LOW BATTERY" warning. If you're wanting buds that will keep going for your entire work-day, I'd keep looking. These will last for a good chunk of the day, but you're going to need to take a break and charge them - They don't seem to take very long to charge in the included case, and the SNAP they make when they slot into their magnetized places is quite satisfying.FIT -As with most buds, these comes with interchangeable ear pieces to match your ear size. There are times when I wish that they had removable pieces to wrap around my ears - honestly I think any "sport" earbuds should - it may not be pretty but it works. As it stands, I have no issues with them loosening up or falling out when I'm puttering around the house doing chores, going on walks or jogs.However, if I start doing workouts where my head is tilting up and down, or when soapy water starts to get into my ears in the shower, they'll start slipping out a bit. That's going to happen no matter how well the rubber pieces fit, so I don't really fault them for that - it's slightly annoying but not a dealbreaker. If you don't want earbuds to fall out, get the kind that wrap around your ear, or don't move as much.

title: Cannot find a complaint, these are incredible for the cost
body: I must admit that while I don't expect things to fail, I don't assume things will invariably work. So, when things do go wrong, I'm generally not surprised or flustered. In the first month of daily use, these headphones have given me zero issues. I literally cannot find a complaint.They come with four sizes of rubber tips for best fit. I've only had one fall out twice, due to it being seated loosely during a fast and sudden head movement but that was my fault.The sound quality is solid. I usually only use one at a time because I need to hear my toddler. When I do put in both, I find myself marvelling at how good they sound. To a trained ear, they'd probably not hold up, but who knows, that's just speculation.I've paired these with my galaxy s20+ and a USB bluetooth adapter on Debian Buster. No issues - if anything, pairing has been outstandingly reliable.You can use only one headphone while the other charges, but I haven't tested to see if you can pair them each simultaneously with different devices. If you remove both simultaneously, they pair with eachother for stereo. So, they have never been far enough apart to not pair with eachother to test this, though I might try.The case charges with usb c or wirelessly with a dedicated qi charger or my phone's wireless power sharing. In a month of daily use I have charged the case three times. There are four blue LEDs indicating the charger's power level, while each earbud has an LED indicating red for charging or blue for charged. The earbuds charge quickly, usually less than an hour when close to fully depleted. This is rare as I like keeping them in the case when not in use. This practice, along with the included case strap, has kept me from losing them.These have not drop connection through the exterior brick and plaster walls of our house's addition, from inside or outside. I have left my phone on the main floor without dropping connection as I walk to the basement or second floor (in a 2500 sqft remodel).I've showered with one in, and while water does get into the rubber, it's still safe and functional. I just dry my ear with a qtip and towel off the rubber. I try my best to keep water out, but I question if that's possible but without a shower cap. I've also immersed one earbud in water with no issues (it fell out in the tub while bathing my kid).For the price, these are just incredible. No neck cord or over the ear hooks getting snagged on things.

title: Great for heavy use by university students
body: I love these earbuds. I'd recommend them to anybody who is looking for solid sound quality and noise cancellation but prioritizes durability for heavy use (such as university students like myself).Recently my Jaybirds died on me; I had used them for 3+ years for listening to music when I walk to class and exercise, cancel out noise when I sleep, and make calls. I was looking for something that could last me a while at this level of heavy use while providing good noise cancellation and decent quality.When I got these I was immediately very pleased. I have very weird ears that most Apple products and other ears buds don't fit. These fit great, stayed in, and were hardly noticeable. The pairing process was also very easy.The sound quality and noise cancellation is good and easily comparable to my $100+ wired Jaybirds. The call quality is decent but nothing to write home about. It won't blow your mind or anything but it is definitely more than sufficient for me.I've used them for close to a month constantly and have had no problems. I am especially impressed with the battery. I charge the case at night and wear the earbuds when I sleep. When I set Spotify to a 30 minute sleep timer there is still plenty of battery to get me through my morning walk to class. I rarely use the whole case charge in 1 day, even with 2/3 hours of intermittent use.I also love how they are water proof, something that I did not have previously with my Jaybirds. When I shower in the dorms late at night sometimes I don't want to play my music out loud publicly, and these have worked great in the shower directly in water.My only nitpicks would be that there is no skip song button, extreme extended use can hurt your ears, and the volume doesn't go that low. The skip song button is not a huge deal, it just means having your phone accessible if skipping songs is something you do. When I sleep I like to put the earbuds on min volume, which on these is significantly louder than on my Jaybirds. With that said, so far it hasn't hindered my sleep. Additionally I think I slept on my right ear one night and developed a blister on the back of my ear. To be fair, I wear these things up to 10 hours a day, and I don't think there is a single design out there that would not hurt after THAT much time. Plus I just slept without them for a day or two and I was back to normal. So if those issues don't sound major to you, I see no reason why you shouldn't get these.

title: Excellent quality and great sound
body: UPDATE: After over 1 month of use I am VERY pleased with this purchase. I can wear them with NO irritation to my ears, the sound is still very good, and I am not having any connection issues. I have learned to always take the right ear piece out of the charging case first and then the left - otherwise they don’t connect smoothly to my devices. The ear buds stay in my ears whether I’m walking around, exercising, or laying down. I love how easy and fast they are to charge.Initial impressions:The charging case feels nice and sturdy. It has a strong magnetic closure that securely holds the lid closed. The ear pieces lock into their slots magnetically as well. Very well made.The ear pieces feel comfortable and easily fit in my ears. It comes with several pairs of ear tips so it is easy to find a good fit.Out of the box, the ear pieces had a partial charge but not enough to successfully use them - could not get them to pair with each other or my phone. I recommend before first use, completely charge both the case and the ear pieces. After charging, the ear pieces worked exactly as described. They paired with each other within 10 seconds of removing them from the charging case . Then they easily paired with my iPhone 6s - the Right ear piece shows up in the bluetooth list. After pairing the first time, they now automatically pair with my phone every time I take them out of the charging case. I can also use them with my iPad easily.If you see both a Right and Left ear piece in your bluetooth list turn off you bluetooth and let the earpieces pair together first - you will see a slow blinking blue light on each ear piece when they are paired to each other then turn your bluetooth back on. You should see only the Right ear piece in the bluetooth list. Select it and you will be set to go.My first day using them has been very successful. I popped my ear pieces in, selected some music, put my phone in my pocket and went for a nice walk. They never lost connection with my phone. They seem to have adequate noise cancellation for what they are and I am satisfied. I like the sound quality - the music sounded rich with good bass. I don’t like my bass to be thumping loud and these ear pieces seem to have a good balance to them for any type of music.Overall, Day 1 with this product has been very satisfying. I am enjoying them very much. I will continue to update this review.

title: Excellent sound, poor battery/ updated
body: Update: I dropped one of my earbuds and it stopped charging so I replaced with the white set. I didn't notice a difference in the product description but the white set has a touch interface rather than a button, which is quite sensitive but I'm getting the hang of it. The battery life is about half again as long as the black set with the button, and with comparable audio quality. I'm happy with my purchase and increasing my rating to 5 stars.I purchased these earbuds to replace an older pair with nearly identical specs from a different manufacturer. These earbuds immediately impressed with excellent sound quality and noise cancelling. I work in a warehouse with a decibel level just below the point at which OSHA requires the wearing of earplugs, so that was really important for me. This far outstrips my old earbuds, of which one still works acceptably in quieter environments. At work, I can't clearly hear my audiobooks at max volume on the old one; new earbuds, still in the "safe" volume on my phone, I can hear with crystal clarity.However, I can't give these earbuds 5 stars as, for my purposes, they do fall somewhat short. The battery life with my old earbuds was excellent, I could listen for 8-10 hours for each side of listening to audio in a moderately noisy environment at 3/4 to full volume. With music, that time was reduced to perhaps 7-8 hours. These earbuds provide a maximum of 4.5-5 hours playtime before needing to be charged in the case again, and if the volume required is higher because of my proximity to moving parts/machinery, it can be as few as 3 hours. This unfortunately can mean that I have to change from side the side as often as 4 times in a shift, never less than twice. I get a little less than 40 total hours of playtime from a full charge of base and buds, wearing only one earbud at a time and charging the other.The plus side of the battery is that it has lights that show how much charge the base station has, and these light up in sequence when charging to let you know it's going. It takes less than 2 hours to fully charge base and earbuds, and the earbuds do charge much more quickly than they discharge.Cosmetically, they are clunky and unattractive. Unlike the streamlined look offered by buds from certain fruit manufacturers, these are rather sizable and stand out. This is good if you want to keep people you're standing near from answering you when you're on a call.
